questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In a certain camera, a combination of four similar thin convex lenses are arranged axially in contact. Then the power of the combination and the total magnification in comparison to the power (p) and magnification (m) for each lens will be, respectively:
questions.explanation: Refer to NCERT for the complete explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A 2 amp current is flowing through two different small copper coils having radii ratio 1:2. The ratio of their respective magnetic moments will be:
questions.explanation: Refer to NCERT for the complete explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The radius of Martian orbit around the Sun is about 4 times the radius of the orbit of Mercury. The Martian year is 687 Earth days. Then which of the following is the length of 1 year on Mercury?
questions.explanation: Refer to NCERT for the complete explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: To an ac power supply of 220 V at 50 Hz, a resistor of 20 Ω, a capacitor of reactance 25 Ω and an inductor of reactance 45 Ω are connected in series. The corresponding current in the circuit and the phase angle between the current and the voltage is, respectively:
questions.explanation: Refer to NCERT for the complete explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A wire of resistance R is cut into 8 equal pieces. From these pieces two equivalent resistances are made by adding four of these together in parallel. Then these two sets are added in series. The net effective resistance of the combination is:
questions.explanation: Refer to NCERT for the complete explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two identical charged conducting spheres A and B have their centres separated by a certain distance. Charge on each sphere is q and the force of repulsion between them is F. A third identical uncharged conducting sphere is brought in contact with sphere A first and then with B and finally removed from both. New force of repulsion between spheres A and B is best given as:
questions.explanation: Refer to NCERT for the complete explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle of mass m is moving around the origin with a constant force F pulling it towards the origin. If Bohr model is used to describe its motion, the radius of the nth orbit and the particle's speed v in the orbit depend on n as:
questions.explanation: Refer to NCERT for the complete explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A balloon is made of a material of surface tension S and its inflation outlet has small area A. It is filled with a gas of density ρ and takes a spherical shape of radius R. When the gas is allowed to flow freely, its radius r changes from R to 0 in time T. If T∝S^α A^β ρ^γ R^δ then:
questions.explanation: Refer to NCERT for the complete explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A parallel plate capacitor made of circular plates is being charged such that the surface charge density on its plates is increasing at a constant rate with time. The magnetic field arising due to displacement current is:

options.A: Zero between the plates and non-zero outside
options.C: Constant between the plates and zero outside the plates
options.D: Non-zero everywhere with maximum at the imaginary cylindrical surface connecting peripheries of the plates

questions.explanation: Correct answer is D: Non-zero everywhere with maximum at the imaginary cylindrical surface connecting peripheries of the plates (NEET 2025).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: There are two inclined surfaces of equal length (L) and same angle of inclination 45° with the horizontal. One is rough and the other perfectly smooth. A given body takes 2 times as much time to slide down on the rough surface than on the smooth surface. The coefficient of kinetic friction (μk) between the object and the rough surface is close to:
questions.explanation: Refer to NCERT for the complete explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The Sun rotates around its centre once in 27 days. What will be the period of revolution if the Sun were to expand to twice its present radius without any external influence? Assume the Sun to be a sphere of uniform density.
questions.explanation: Refer to NCERT for the complete explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A model for quantized motion of an electron in a uniform magnetic field B states that the flux passing through the orbit is n(h/e). According to the model, the magnetic moment of an electron in its lowest energy state will be (m is the mass of the electron):
questions.explanation: Refer to NCERT for the complete explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The intensity of transmitted light when a polaroid sheet, placed between two crossed polaroids at 22.5° from the polarization axis of one of the polaroids, is (I₀ is the intensity of polarised light after passing through the first polaroid):
questions.explanation: Refer to NCERT for the complete explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
questions.question: A photon and an electron (mass m) have the same energy E. The ratio (λphoton/λelectron) of their de Broglie wavelengths is (c is the speed of light):
questions.explanation: Refer to NCERT for the complete explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An unpolarized light beam travelling in air is incident on a medium of refractive index 1.73 at Brewster's angle. Then:

options.A: Transmitted light is completely polarized with angle of refraction close to 30°
options.B: Reflected light is completely polarized and the angle of reflection is close to 60°
options.C: Reflected light is partially polarized and the angle of reflection is close to 30°
options.D: Both reflected and transmitted light are perfectly polarized with angles of reflection and refraction close to 60° and 30° respectively

questions.explanation: Correct answer is B: Reflected light is completely polarized and the angle of reflection is close to 60° (NEET 2025).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In a plane electromagnetic wave, the electric field E and magnetic field B are:

options.A: Parallel to each other and to the direction of propagation
options.B: Perpendicular to each other but parallel to the direction of propagation
options.C: Perpendicular to each other and both perpendicular to the direction of propagation
options.D: Parallel to each other and perpendicular to direction of propagation

questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 8 — Electromagnetic Waves, Section 8.3: Electromagnetic Spectrum
questions.ncert_line: Electromagnetic waves are transverse waves. The speed of EM waves in vacuum is c = 3×10⁸ m/s. E and B are perpendicular to each other and also perpendicular to the direction of propagation.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
questions.question: In the photoelectric effect, increasing the intensity of incident light (keeping frequency constant above threshold) will:

options.A: Increase the maximum kinetic energy of photoelectrons
options.C: Increase the number of photoelectrons emitted per second

questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 11 — Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter, Section 11.3: Photoelectric Effect
questions.ncert_line: Einstein explained the photoelectric effect using the concept of photons. The maximum kinetic energy of photoelectrons is: Kmax = hν - φ. The stopping potential depends on frequency and not on intensity of light.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: According to Bohr's model, the angular momentum of an electron in the nth orbit is:
questions.explanation: Correct answer is C. NCERT Reference: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 12 — Atoms, Section 12.2: Bohr Model of the Hydrogen Atom
questions.ncert_line: In the Bohr model, the electron revolves around the nucleus only in certain stable orbits (stationary states) for which the angular momentum is a whole number multiple of h/2π (L = nh/2π).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle starts from rest with uniform acceleration a. The distance travelled in the nth second is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 3 — Motion in a Straight Line, Section 3.4: Kinematic Equations
questions.ncert_line: For uniformly accelerated motion: v = u + at; s = ut + ½at²; v² = u² + 2as; s_n = u + a(2n−1)/2. These equations are valid only when acceleration is constant.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The range of a projectile is maximum when the angle of projection with the horizontal is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 4 — Motion in a Plane, Section 4.6: Projectile Motion
questions.ncert_line: In projectile motion, the horizontal component of velocity remains constant (no air resistance). The range R = u²sin2θ/g is maximum when θ = 45°. The time of flight T = 2u sinθ/g.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A ball is thrown horizontally from the top of a tower. During its flight, the horizontal component of velocity:

options.B: Decreases due to air resistance (neglected here)

questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 4 — Motion in a Plane, Section 4.6: Projectile Motion
questions.ncert_line: In projectile motion, the horizontal component of velocity remains constant (no air resistance). The range R = u²sin2θ/g is maximum when θ = 45°. The time of flight T = 2u sinθ/g.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A body of mass m is on a smooth horizontal surface. A force F is applied at 30° above the horizontal. The acceleration of the body is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 5 — Laws of Motion, Section 5.2–5.5
questions.ncert_line: Newton's first law: A body at rest stays at rest and a body in uniform motion stays in uniform motion unless acted upon by an external force. Newton's second law: F = ma. Newton's third law: Every action has an equal and opposite reaction.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The work-energy theorem states that the net work done on an object equals:

options.B: The change in its kinetic energy
options.C: The product of force and time
options.D: The change in its potential energy

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 6 — Work, Energy and Power, Section 6.3: Work-Energy Theorem
questions.ncert_line: The work-energy theorem states: the net work done on an object equals the change in its kinetic energy: Wnet = ΔKE = ½mv² − ½mu².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In a perfectly inelastic collision, which quantity is conserved?
questions.explanation: Correct: D. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 6 — Work, Energy and Power, Section 6.10: Collisions
questions.ncert_line: In an elastic collision, both kinetic energy and momentum are conserved. In a perfectly inelastic collision, only momentum is conserved and the objects stick together. The coefficient of restitution e = 1 for elastic and e = 0 for perfectly inelastic collisions.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The coefficient of restitution e for a perfectly elastic collision is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 6 — Work, Energy and Power, Section 6.10: Collisions
questions.ncert_line: In an elastic collision, both kinetic energy and momentum are conserved. In a perfectly inelastic collision, only momentum is conserved and the objects stick together. The coefficient of restitution e = 1 for elastic and e = 0 for perfectly inelastic collisions.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The moment of inertia of a uniform disc of mass M and radius R about an axis through its centre perpendicular to its plane is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 7 — Systems of Particles and Rotational Motion, Section 7.6–7.7
questions.ncert_line: Torque τ = r × F = Iα. Angular momentum L = Iω. The moment of inertia of a solid sphere about its diameter = 2MR²/5; a ring about its axis = MR²; a disc about its axis = MR²/2; a hollow sphere about its diameter = 2MR²/3.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: System of Particles and Rotational Motion
questions.question: A spinning ice skater pulls her arms inward. Which of the following is conserved?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 7 — Systems of Particles and Rotational Motion, Section 7.7: Angular Momentum Conservation
questions.ncert_line: The total angular momentum of a system is conserved when the net external torque is zero: if τ_ext = 0, then dL/dt = 0, so L = constant. A spinning skater pulls arms in to spin faster (I decreases, ω increases).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The escape velocity from the Earth's surface is approximately:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 8 — Gravitation, Section 8.5–8.6
questions.ncert_line: The gravitational potential energy at distance r from the Earth's centre is U = −GMm/r. The escape velocity from Earth's surface is ve = √(2GM/R) = √(2gR) ≈ 11.2 km/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The orbital velocity of a satellite close to Earth's surface is approximately:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 8 — Gravitation, Section 8.7: Earth Satellites
questions.ncert_line: For a satellite in a circular orbit at height h above Earth's surface: orbital velocity v₀ = √(GM/(R+h)). The time period T = 2π(R+h)/v₀. For h << R, v₀ ≈ √(gR) ≈ 7.9 km/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Young's modulus of elasticity is defined as:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 9 — Mechanical Properties of Solids, Section 9.3–9.6
questions.ncert_line: Young's modulus Y = (F/A)/(ΔL/L) = stress/strain. Bulk modulus B = −V(ΔP/ΔV). Shear modulus (rigidity modulus) G = shear stress/shear strain. For a material: Y > B for most metals.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The excess pressure inside a soap bubble of radius r and surface tension S is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 10 — Mechanical Properties of Fluids, Section 10.7: Surface Tension
questions.ncert_line: Surface tension (S) is the force per unit length acting tangentially on the surface of a liquid. The excess pressure inside a soap bubble is 4S/r (two surfaces). Inside a liquid drop it is 2S/r.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Bernoulli's principle is based on which conservation law?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 10 — Mechanical Properties of Fluids, Section 10.4–10.6
questions.ncert_line: Bernoulli's principle: For a streamlined flow, P + ½ρv² + ρgh = constant. Pascal's law: Pressure applied to an enclosed fluid is transmitted equally in all directions. Archimedes' principle: upthrust = weight of fluid displaced.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: For an adiabatic process, which of the following is true?

options.A: Q = 0, so ΔU = −W (work done by gas)

questions.explanation: Correct: A. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 12 — Thermodynamics, Section 12.5–12.10
questions.ncert_line: First law: ΔU = Q − W (heat absorbed = increase in internal energy + work done by gas). For an adiabatic process, Q = 0, so ΔU = −W. Entropy increases in irreversible processes (Second law).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The average kinetic energy per molecule of an ideal gas is related to absolute temperature T by:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 13 — Kinetic Theory, Section 13.4–13.5
questions.ncert_line: From kinetic theory: PV = (1/3)Nmv²rms. The average kinetic energy per molecule = (3/2)kBT. The rms speed vrms = √(3RT/M) = √(3kBT/m). For a monoatomic ideal gas, Cv = (3/2)R and Cp = (5/2)R.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The number of degrees of freedom of a diatomic molecule at room temperature is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 13 — Kinetic Theory, Section 13.6: Law of Equipartion
questions.ncert_line: The law of equipartion of energy: each degree of freedom contributes ½kBT to the average energy. A monoatomic gas has 3 translational degrees of freedom; diatomic gas has 5 (3 translational + 2 rotational) at room temperature.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The ratio Cp/Cv (γ) for a monoatomic ideal gas is:
questions.explanation: Correct: D. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 13 — Kinetic Theory, Section 13.6: Law of Equipartion
questions.ncert_line: The law of equipartion of energy: each degree of freedom contributes ½kBT to the average energy. A monoatomic gas has 3 translational degrees of freedom; diatomic gas has 5 (3 translational + 2 rotational) at room temperature.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Water has maximum density at:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 11 — Thermal Properties of Matter, Section 11.3: Thermal Expansion
questions.ncert_line: Coefficient of linear expansion α: ΔL = L₀αΔT. Coefficient of volume expansion γ = 3α. Water shows anomalous expansion — it expands on cooling below 4°C and has maximum density at 4°C.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In simple harmonic motion, the restoring force is:

options.A: Constant and directed toward mean position
options.B: Proportional to displacement and directed toward mean position
options.D: Proportional to displacement² and directed away from mean

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 14 — Oscillations, Section 14.3–14.4
questions.ncert_line: In SHM, the restoring force F = −kx (proportional to displacement, directed toward mean position). Displacement x = A cos(ωt + φ). Angular frequency ω = √(k/m) for a spring-mass system. Period T = 2π/ω = 2π√(m/k).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following does NOT affect the time period of a simple pendulum?

options.D: Both C (mass) and the amplitude for small oscillations

questions.explanation: Correct: D. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 14 — Oscillations, Section 14.5: The Simple Pendulum
questions.ncert_line: The time period of a simple pendulum: T = 2π√(L/g). It is independent of the mass of the bob and the amplitude (for small oscillations). T increases if g decreases (e.g., at higher altitude or in a lift accelerating downward).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The speed of a transverse wave on a string depends on:

options.C: Tension in the string and linear mass density

questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 15 — Waves, Section 15.2–15.4
questions.ncert_line: For a transverse wave y = A sin(kx − ωt + φ). Wave speed v = ω/k = λf. For a string: v = √(T/μ), where T is tension and μ is linear mass density. Speed of sound in air increases with temperature.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A source of sound moves toward a stationary observer. The observer will hear a sound of:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 15 — Waves, Section 15.9: Doppler Effect
questions.ncert_line: Doppler effect: The apparent frequency of a wave changes when source or observer is in motion. When source and observer approach each other, apparent frequency increases. Formula: f' = f₀(v + v₀)/(v − vs).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The electric field inside a conductor in electrostatic equilibrium is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 1 — Electric Charges and Fields, Section 1.8: Gauss's Law
questions.ncert_line: Gauss's law: The total electric flux through a closed surface is equal to the net charge enclosed divided by ε₀: φ = Q_enc/ε₀. The electric field inside a conductor is zero; charge resides only on the surface.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A capacitor is charged and then DISCONNECTED from the battery. A dielectric is inserted between its plates. Which of the following is CORRECT?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 2 — Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance, Section 2.10.2: Effect of Dielectric
questions.ncert_line: When a dielectric is inserted between the capacitor plates (capacitor disconnected from battery), the capacitance increases by factor K but charge remains constant, so voltage decreases and energy stored decreases.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The energy stored in a capacitor of capacitance C charged to voltage V is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 2 — Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance, Section 2.9–2.10
questions.ncert_line: Capacitance C = Q/V. For a parallel plate capacitor C = ε₀A/d (no dielectric) or C = Kε₀A/d (with dielectric). Energy stored in a capacitor U = Q²/2C = CV²/2 = QV/2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The resistivity of a metallic conductor generally:

options.D: First decreases then increases with temperature

questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 3 — Current Electricity, Section 3.5: Drift of Electrons
questions.ncert_line: Drift velocity vd = eEτ/m = I/(nAe), where n is number density of electrons, A is cross-sectional area and e is electron charge. Resistivity ρ = m/(ne²τ), where τ is relaxation time.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The drift velocity of electrons in a conductor is of the order of:
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 3 — Current Electricity, Section 3.5: Drift of Electrons
questions.ncert_line: Drift velocity vd = eEτ/m = I/(nAe), where n is number density of electrons, A is cross-sectional area and e is electron charge. Resistivity ρ = m/(ne²τ), where τ is relaxation time.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In a Wheatstone bridge, no current flows through the galvanometer when:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 3 — Current Electricity, Section 3.14–3.15
questions.ncert_line: The Wheatstone bridge is balanced (no current through galvanometer) when P/Q = R/S. The metre bridge is a practical application. The potentiometer is used for accurate measurement of EMF and has no current drawn from the cell under balanced condition.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Statement I: A potentiometer can be used to compare the EMFs of two cells without drawing any current from them at the balance point.
Statement II: At the balance point in a potentiometer, the galvanometer shows zero deflection.

options.A: Both are true and Statement II explains Statement I
options.B: Both are true but Statement II does NOT explain Statement I
options.C: Statement I is true but Statement II is false

questions.explanation: Correct: A. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 3 — Current Electricity, Section 3.10: Kirchhoff's Laws
questions.ncert_line: Kirchhoff's current law (KCL): The algebraic sum of currents at any junction is zero. Kirchhoff's voltage law (KVL): The algebraic sum of potential differences in any closed loop is zero.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The magnetic field inside a long solenoid carrying current I with n turns per unit length is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 4 — Moving Charges and Magnetism, Section 4.7: Ampere's Law
questions.ncert_line: Ampere's circuital law: ∮B·dl = μ₀I_enc. The field inside a long solenoid: B = μ₀nI, where n is the number of turns per unit length. Outside a solenoid, B = 0.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: According to Lenz's law, the induced current in a loop:

options.A: Aids the change in flux that produces it
options.B: Opposes the change in flux that produces it
options.C: Is always in the clockwise direction
options.D: Is proportional to the rate of change of current

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 6 — Electromagnetic Induction, Section 6.3–6.4
questions.ncert_line: Faraday's law: The induced EMF in a loop is ε = −dΦ/dt, where Φ = BA cosθ is the magnetic flux. Lenz's law: The induced current opposes the change that produces it (the negative sign in Faraday's law).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The energy stored in an inductor of inductance L carrying current I is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 6 — Electromagnetic Induction, Section 6.7–6.8
questions.ncert_line: Self-inductance L is defined by ε = −L(dI/dt). Energy stored in an inductor: U = ½LI². For a solenoid: L = μ₀n²V, where V is volume. Mutual inductance M: ε₂ = −M(dI₁/dt).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In a series LCR circuit at resonance, the impedance is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 7 — Alternating Current, Section 7.5–7.8
questions.ncert_line: In an LCR series circuit, impedance Z = √(R² + (XL − XC)²). At resonance, XL = XC, so Z = R (minimum) and current is maximum. Resonant frequency f₀ = 1/(2π√(LC)). Quality factor Q = ω₀L/R.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following EM waves has the highest frequency?
questions.explanation: Correct: D. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 8 — Electromagnetic Waves, Section 8.3: Electromagnetic Spectrum
questions.ncert_line: The electromagnetic spectrum in increasing order of frequency (decreasing wavelength): Radio waves < Microwaves < Infrared < Visible < Ultraviolet < X-rays < Gamma rays. Gamma rays are produced in nuclear transitions; X-rays by bombarding metal targets with high-energy electrons.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the production of X-rays?

options.B: Bombardment of a metal target with high-energy electrons
options.C: Electron transitions in the outer shells of atoms

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 8 — Electromagnetic Waves, Section 8.3: Electromagnetic Spectrum
questions.ncert_line: The electromagnetic spectrum in increasing order of frequency (decreasing wavelength): Radio waves < Microwaves < Infrared < Visible < Ultraviolet < X-rays < Gamma rays. Gamma rays are produced in nuclear transitions; X-rays by bombarding metal targets with high-energy electrons.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Maxwell's displacement current arises due to:

options.A: Flow of free electrons in a vacuum
options.B: A changing electric field between capacitor plates

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 8 — Electromagnetic Waves, Section 8.2: Displacement Current
questions.ncert_line: Maxwell added the concept of displacement current (ε₀ dΦE/dt) to Ampere's law to make it consistent. Displacement current arises due to changing electric field and produces a magnetic field just like ordinary conduction current.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Total internal reflection can occur when light travels from:

options.A: A rarer to a denser medium
options.B: A denser to a rarer medium, at angles greater than the critical angle
options.D: A medium of n=1.5 to a medium of n=2.0

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 9 — Ray Optics, Section 9.3: Refraction
questions.ncert_line: Snell's law: n₁ sin θ₁ = n₂ sin θ₂. Refractive index n = c/v = sin i/sin r. Total internal reflection occurs when the angle of incidence in the denser medium exceeds the critical angle θc = sin⁻¹(n₂/n₁).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: When white light passes through a glass prism, which colour is deviated the MOST?
questions.explanation: Correct: D. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 9 — Ray Optics, Section 9.7: Dispersion by a Prism
questions.ncert_line: Dispersion is the splitting of white light into its component colours by a prism. Violet light is deviated most (highest refractive index, lowest speed) and red light least. The angle of minimum deviation δm = A(n−1) for small angles.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In Young's double slit experiment, if the slit separation d is doubled keeping D and λ constant, the fringe width β will:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 10 — Wave Optics, Section 10.3: Young's Double Slit
questions.ncert_line: In Young's double slit experiment, fringe width β = λD/d, where λ is wavelength, D is slit-to-screen distance, and d is slit separation. Constructive interference (bright fringe): path difference = nλ. Destructive: (n + ½)λ.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: According to Malus's law, when plane polarised light (intensity I₀) passes through an analyser at 60° to the polariser, the transmitted intensity is:
questions.explanation: Correct: A. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 10 — Wave Optics, Section 10.6: Polarisation
questions.ncert_line: Malus's law: When plane polarised light passes through an analyser, the intensity I = I₀ cos²θ, where θ is the angle between the polariser and analyser axes. Polarisation proves the transverse nature of light.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In single slit diffraction, if the slit width is decreased, the central maximum will:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 10 — Wave Optics, Section 10.5: Diffraction
questions.ncert_line: In single slit diffraction, the condition for the first dark fringe is a sinθ = λ, where a is the slit width. The central maximum is twice as wide as secondary maxima. Narrower the slit, wider the central maximum.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
questions.question: An electron is accelerated through potential V. Its de Broglie wavelength λ is proportional to:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 11 — Dual Nature of Radiation, Section 11.5: de Broglie Hypothesis
questions.ncert_line: de Broglie wavelength of an electron accelerated through potential V: λ = h/√(2meV) = 1.227/√V nm. For a particle of momentum p: λ = h/p = h/mv.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In the Bohr model of hydrogen, the radius of the nth orbit is proportional to:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 12 — Atoms, Section 12.2: Bohr Model
questions.ncert_line: In the Bohr model, the radius of nth orbit: rn = n²a₀ where a₀ = 0.529 Å (Bohr radius). Energy of nth orbit: En = −13.6/n² eV. The frequency of emitted radiation: hν = En₁ − En₂.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The Balmer series of hydrogen spectrum lies in which region of the electromagnetic spectrum?
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 12 — Atoms, Section 12.4: Hydrogen Spectrum
questions.ncert_line: Hydrogen spectrum series: Lyman series (UV, n→1), Balmer series (visible, n→2), Paschen series (IR, n→3), Brackett (IR, n→4), Pfund (IR, n→5). The Rydberg formula: 1/λ = R(1/n₁² − 1/n₂²).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The binding energy per nucleon is maximum for elements with mass number around:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 13 — Nuclei, Section 13.3: Binding Energy
questions.ncert_line: Binding energy per nucleon is maximum (~8.8 MeV) around mass number A ≈ 56 (iron group). Elements lighter than iron gain energy by fusion; those heavier than iron gain energy by fission. Mass defect Δm = Zmp + (A−Z)mn − M(nucleus).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In beta-minus decay, the atomic number Z of the nucleus:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 13 — Nuclei, Section 13.4: Radioactivity
questions.ncert_line: Radioactive decay law: N = N₀e^(−λt). Activity A = λN. Half-life T₁/₂ = 0.693/λ. In alpha decay, A decreases by 4 and Z decreases by 2. In beta-minus decay, Z increases by 1 and A is unchanged. In gamma decay, only energy is released.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The half-life of a radioactive sample is 30 minutes. After 2 hours, the fraction of the original sample remaing is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 13 — Nuclei, Section 13.4: Radioactivity
questions.ncert_line: Radioactive decay law: N = N₀e^(−λt). Activity A = λN. Half-life T₁/₂ = 0.693/λ. In alpha decay, A decreases by 4 and Z decreases by 2. In beta-minus decay, Z increases by 1 and A is unchanged. In gamma decay, only energy is released.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Statement I: Nuclear fission of U-235 releases about 200 MeV per fission event.
Statement II: Nuclear fusion releases more energy per unit mass than fission.

options.B: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.C: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: Correct: A. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 13 — Nuclei, Section 13.5–13.6: Fission and Fusion
questions.ncert_line: Nuclear fission: A heavy nucleus (e.g., U-235) splits into two medium-sized nuclei on neutron absorption, releasing ~200 MeV of energy and 2-3 neutrons. Nuclear fusion requires extremely high temperature (≥10⁷ K) to overcome Coulomb repulsion.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In an n-type semiconductor, the majority charge carriers are:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 14 — Semiconductor Electronics, Section 14.4–14.5
questions.ncert_line: In intrinsic semiconductors, electrons and holes are always equal (ne = nh). In n-type (donor impurity), electrons are majority carriers. In p-type (acceptor impurity), holes are majority carriers. At 0 K, semiconductors behave as insulators.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In forward bias of a p-n junction, the depletion region:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 14 — Semiconductor Electronics, Section 14.6: p-n Junction
questions.ncert_line: In a p-n junction diode, forward bias reduces the depletion region and allows current flow. Reverse bias widens the depletion region and allows only a tiny reverse (leakage) current. A diode acts as a rectifier — it conducts in one direction only.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A Zener diode is used primarily as a:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 14 — Semiconductor Electronics, Section 14.7.2: Zener Diode
questions.ncert_line: A Zener diode is a heavily doped p-n junction designed to operate in reverse breakdown (Zener breakdown). It maintains a nearly constant voltage across it (Zener voltage Vz) over a wide range of currents. It is used as a voltage regulator.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which logic gate is called a universal gate?
questions.explanation: Correct: D. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 14 — Semiconductor Electronics, Section 14.8: Logic Gates
questions.ncert_line: NOT gate: output is complement of input. AND gate: output is 1 only when all inputs are 1. OR gate: output is 0 only when all inputs are 0. NAND and NOR gates are universal gates from which all other gates can be constructed.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The output of a NAND gate is LOW (0) only when:

options.C: One input is HIGH, other is LOW

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 14 — Semiconductor Electronics, Section 14.8: Logic Gates
questions.ncert_line: NOT gate: output is complement of input. AND gate: output is 1 only when all inputs are 1. OR gate: output is 0 only when all inputs are 0. NAND and NOR gates are universal gates from which all other gates can be constructed.

questions.question: A horse pulls a cart. By Newton's 3rd law, cart pulls horse back equally. Why does the system move forward?

options.C: Newton's 3rd law does not apply here

questions.ncert_line: Action and reaction act on different bodies. Ground friction on horse's hooves gives net forward force.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: At the highest point of a vertical throw, the kinetic energy of the body is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. KE = 0 at highest point. All energy converted to PE.

questions.ncert_line: At highest point, v = 0 so KE = ½mv² = 0.

questions.ncert_line: All mass at distance R from axis. I = MR².

questions.ncert_line: KE_rot = (1/5)Mv², KE_total = (7/10)Mv². Ratio = 2/7.

questions.question: Angular momentum of a system is conserved when:

questions.ncert_line: L = Iω is conserved when net external torque = 0.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The escape velocity from Earth's surface is approximately:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Escape velocity = 11.2 km/s. Orbital velocity = 7.9 km/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If Earth's radius is halved keeping mass constant, escape velocity becomes:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. ve ∝ 1/√R. Halving R multiplies ve by √2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: For an isothermal process involving an ideal gas:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Isothermal: ΔU = 0, all heat converts to work (Q = W).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: When an ideal gas is compressed adiabatically, its temperature:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Adiabatic compression → temperature rises. TV^(γ-1) = constant.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Energy stored in a capacitor C charged to voltage V is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. U = ½CV².

questions.ncert_line: U = ½CV² = Q²/2C = QV/2.

questions.question: Force on a charged particle moving parallel to a magnetic field is:

questions.ncert_line: F = qvB sinθ. θ = 0° → F = 0.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: At resonance in a series LCR circuit, the impedance is equal to:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. At resonance Z = R. Current is maximum at resonance.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 7 — Alternating Current, Section 7.8
questions.ncert_line: At resonance: XL = XC, so they cancel. Impedance Z = √(R² + (XL−XC)²) = √(R²) = R.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Power dissipated in a pure inductor connected to AC source is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Pure inductor dissipates zero power. Current leads/lags voltage by 90°.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 7 — Alternating Current, Section 7.5
questions.ncert_line: Pure inductor: phase difference between V and I = 90°. Power = VIcos90° = 0.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following electromagnetic waves has the highest frequency?
questions.explanation: Correct: D. Gamma rays have highest frequency (and energy, shortest wavelength).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 8 — Electromagnetic Waves, Section 8.3
questions.ncert_line: Frequency order: γ-rays > X-rays > UV > visible > IR > microwave > radio waves.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which EM radiation is used in RADAR?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. RADAR uses microwaves. IR used in remote sensing/night vision. UV used in sterilisation.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 8 — Electromagnetic Waves, Section 8.3
questions.ncert_line: RADAR (Radio Detection And Ranging) uses microwaves (wavelength ~ cm range).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: According to the law of equipartion of energy, energy per degree of freedom per molecule is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Energy per degree of freedom = ½kT. Monatomic gas (3 dof): total = 3/2 kT.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 13 — Kinetic Theory, Section 13.5
questions.ncert_line: Law of equipartion: each degree of freedom contributes ½kT to average energy of a molecule.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Time period of a simple pendulum is independent of:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. T = 2π√(L/g). Mass of bob does NOT affect time period.
questions.ncert_line: T = 2π√(L/g). Time period depends on L and g only — not on mass or amplitude (for small angles).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The speed of sound in air increases with:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Humid air has lower density (H₂O lighter than N₂/O₂) → speed increases. Speed also increases with temperature.
questions.ncert_line: v = √(γP/ρ). Humidity replaces heavier O2/N2 with lighter H2O molecules → lower density → higher speed.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which electromagnetic radiation has the highest frequency?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Gamma rays have highest frequency and energy. Radio waves have lowest frequency.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 8 — Electromagnetic Waves, Section 8.3
questions.ncert_line: EM spectrum order (increasing frequency): Radio < Microwave < IR < Visible < UV < X-ray < Gamma.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In a pure resistor AC circuit, voltage and current are:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Pure R: V and I in phase. Pure L: I lags V by 90°. Pure C: I leads V by 90°.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 7 — Alternating Current, Section 7.3
questions.ncert_line: In a pure resistor, V and I are always in phase (phase difference = 0).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: At resonance in a series LCR circuit, the impedance is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. At resonance Z = R (minimum impedance). Current is maximum at resonance.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 7 — Alternating Current, Section 7.7
questions.ncert_line: At resonance: XL = XC, so they cancel. Z = √(R² + (XL−XC)²) = √(R²) = R.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In a common emitter transistor, the current gain β is defined as:
questions.explanation: Correct: A. β = IC/IB. The base current controls larger collector current — transistor action.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 14 — Semiconductor Electronics, Section 14.8
questions.ncert_line: β (DC current gain) = IC/IB. Typical values: 20-200. β = α/(1−α) where α = IC/IE.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which logic gate gives output 1 only when all inputs are 0?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. NOR = NOT OR. Output 1 only when both inputs are 0.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 14 — Semiconductor Electronics, Section 14.9
questions.ncert_line: NOR gate: output = 1 only when ALL inputs = 0. It is the complement of OR gate.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Within the elastic limit, stress is proportional to strain. This is known as:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Hooke's law: stress ∝ strain. E = stress/strain = Young's modulus.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 9 — Mechanical Properties of Solids, Section 9.3
questions.ncert_line: Hooke's law: stress ∝ strain (within elastic limit). Stress/strain = elastic modulus (constant).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: When water at 0°C is heated to 4°C, its density:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Water density increases 0→4°C (anomalous expansion). Maximum density at 4°C = 1000 kg/m³.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 11 — Thermal Properties of Matter, Section 11.6
questions.ncert_line: Water has anomalous expansion — density increases from 0°C to 4°C, maximum density at 4°C.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: According to kinetic theory, temperature of a gas is a measure of:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. T ∝ average KE per molecule. KE = (3/2)kT per molecule.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 13 — Kinetic Theory, Section 13.4
questions.ncert_line: Temperature is proportional to average translational kinetic energy per molecule: ½m<v²> = (3/2)kT.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Bernoulli's theorem is based on the principle of conservation of:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Bernoulli's theorem = conservation of energy (pressure + kinetic + potential energy = constant).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 10 — Mechanical Properties of Fluids, Section 10.4
questions.ncert_line: Bernoulli's theorem: P + ½ρv² + ρgh = constant. It is conservation of energy per unit volume.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A steel needle can float on water due to:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Needle floats due to surface tension, not buoyancy (steel is denser than water).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 10 — Mechanical Properties of Fluids, Section 10.5
questions.ncert_line: A steel needle placed carefully on water floats due to surface tension — water surface acts like a stretched membrane.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Newton's law of cooling states that rate of heat loss is proportional to:

options.C: Temperature difference between body and surroundings

questions.explanation: Correct: C. dQ/dt ∝ (T − T0). Valid when temperature difference is small. Stefan's law for large differences.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 11 — Thermal Properties of Matter, Section 11.9
questions.ncert_line: Newton's law of cooling: dQ/dt ∝ (T − T_surroundings). Valid for small temperature differences.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The rms speed of gas molecules is proportional to:
questions.explanation: Correct: A. vrms ∝ √T. At constant M, doubling T increases vrms by factor √2.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 13 — Kinetic Theory, Section 13.5
questions.ncert_line: vrms = √(3RT/M) ∝ √T at constant molar mass. Doubling T increases rms speed by √2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Electric field lines never intersect each other because:

options.B: At intersection point, field would have two directions which is impossible
options.D: They always go from positive to negative

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Two field lines intersecting would mean two directions of E at one point — impossible.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 1 — Electric Charges and Fields, Section 1.10
questions.ncert_line: If field lines crossed, the field at that point would have two directions simultaneously — which is physically impossible.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The SI unit of resistivity is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Resistivity unit = Ω·m. Conductivity σ = 1/ρ has unit S/m or Ω⁻¹m⁻¹.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 3 — Current Electricity, Section 3.5
questions.ncert_line: Resistivity ρ = RA/L. Units = Ω × m² / m = Ω·m.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The materials that are strongly attracted by a magnetic field are called:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ferromagnetic = strongly attracted. Paramagnetic = weakly attracted. Diamagnetic = weakly repelled.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 5 — Magnetism and Matter, Section 5.4
questions.ncert_line: Ferromagnetic materials (iron, nickel, cobalt) are strongly attracted to magnetic fields and can be permanently magnetised.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a condition for total internal reflection?

options.A: Light must travel from denser to rarer medium
options.B: Angle of incidence must exceed critical angle
options.C: Light must travel from rarer to denser medium

questions.explanation: Correct: C. TIR needs denser→rarer medium (not rarer→denser). e.g. glass→air, not air→glass.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 9 — Ray Optics, Section 9.4
questions.ncert_line: TIR occurs when light travels from DENSER to RARER medium and angle of incidence exceeds critical angle.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Bernoulli's equation is based on conservation of:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Bernoulli's principle = conservation of energy for fluid flow.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 10 — Mechanical Properties of Fluids, Section 10.4
questions.ncert_line: Bernoulli's theorem: P + ½ρv² + ρgh = constant. It is derived from conservation of energy.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A body floats in a liquid when:

options.D: Its volume equals displaced liquid volume

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Float condition: Buoyant force = Weight. F_b = ρ_liquid × V_submerged × g = mg.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 10 — Mechanical Properties of Fluids, Section 10.3
questions.ncert_line: Archimedes' principle: body floats when buoyant force (weight of displaced liquid) = weight of body.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The phenomenon of splitting of white light into its component colours is called:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Dispersion = splitting of white light. Violet bends most, red bends least (prism).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 9 — Ray Optics, Section 9.9
questions.ncert_line: Dispersion: splitting of white light into VIBGYOR spectrum. Occurs because different colours have different refractive indices.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The electric flux through a closed surface enclosing a charge q is (Gauss's law):
questions.explanation: Correct: A. φ = q/ε₀ (Gauss's law). Independent of size/shape of surface.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 1 — Electric Charges and Fields, Section 1.13
questions.ncert_line: Gauss's law: φ = q_enclosed/ε₀. Electric flux through closed surface = total enclosed charge / ε₀.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A material that is repelled by a magnetic field is called:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Diamagnetic = repelled by B field. Paramagnetic = weakly attracted. Ferromagnetic = strongly attracted.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 5 — Magnetism and Matter, Section 5.3
questions.ncert_line: Diamagnetic materials (e.g. Cu, Bi, water) are weakly repelled by magnetic fields. χm is small and negative.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The half-life of a radioactive substance is 20 days. After 60 days, the fraction remaing is:
questions.explanation: Correct: A. After 3 half-lives: (1/2)³ = 1/8 remains.
questions.ncert_line: Number of half-lives = 60/20 = 3. Fraction remaing = (1/2)³ = 1/8.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The area under a velocity-time graph gives:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Area under v-t graph = displacement. Area under a-t graph = change in velocity.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 3 — Motion in a Straight Line, Section 3.4
questions.ncert_line: Area under v-t graph = ∫v dt = displacement. Slope of v-t graph = acceleration.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The gravitational potential energy of a body of mass m at height h above Earth surface is approximately:
questions.explanation: Correct: A. GPE = mgh for small heights. Negative sign in exact formula indicates bound state.
questions.ncert_line: For h << R (Earth radius), gravitational PE = mgh (taking surface as reference). Exact formula: −GMm/(R+h).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The SI unit of magnetic field (B) is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Tesla is SI unit of B. Weber = unit of magnetic flux. 1 T = 1 Wb/m².
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 4 — Moving Charges and Magnetism, Section 4.2
questions.ncert_line: SI unit of B (magnetic flux density) is Tesla (T). 1 T = 1 Wb/m² = 1 kg/(A·s²). CGS unit = Gauss (1T = 10⁴ G).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Power factor of a purely inductive AC circuit is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Pure inductor: φ = 90°, power factor = cos90° = 0. No power dissipated.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 7 — Alternating Current, Section 7.5
questions.ncert_line: Power factor = cosφ. In pure inductor φ = 90°, so cosφ = cos90° = 0. No real power consumed.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two charges +q and −q are placed at distance d apart. The electric field at the midpoint between them is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Both fields point in same direction (from +q toward −q) and add up.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 1 — Electric Charges and Fields, Section 1.7
questions.ncert_line: At midpoint: E from +q points away (toward −q) = kq/(d/2)² = 4kq/d². E from −q also points toward −q. Both fields ADD → 8kq/d² total. Wait: total = 4kq/d² + 4kq/d² = 8kq/d²... Let me give cleaner option.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): Diffraction of light is more pronounced with obstacles comparable in size to wavelength of light. Reason (R): Diffraction is a wave phenomenon depending on the ratio of obstacle size to wavelength.

options.A: A is true, R is true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: A is true, R is true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true, R is false
options.D: A is false, R is true

questions.explanation: Correct: A. Both true, R explains A. Diffraction most pronounced when d ≈ λ.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 10 — Wave Optics, Section 10.4
questions.ncert_line: Diffraction is maximum when obstacle/slit size ≈ wavelength. R correctly explains A using wave physics.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In hydrogen spectrum, the series that lies in the visible region is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Balmer series = visible. Lyman = UV (n→1). Paschen = IR (n→3). Common exam confusion.
questions.ncert_line: Balmer series (n=2 transitions): visible light (400-700 nm). Lyman = UV. Paschen, Brackett, Pfund = infrared.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In a p-type semiconductor, the majority carriers are:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. p-type: holes are majority carriers. n-type: electrons are majority carriers.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 14 — Semiconductor Electronics, Section 14.4
questions.ncert_line: p-type semiconductor: doped with trivalent impurity (e.g. B, Al). Holes are majority carriers; electrons are minority.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: On doubling the distance between two point charges, the force between them becomes:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. F ∝ 1/r². Doubling r → force becomes 1/4.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 1 — Electric Charges and Fields, Section 1.6
questions.ncert_line: Coulomb's law: F = kq1q2/r². If r doubles: F' = kq1q2/(2r)² = F/4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The radius of the first Bohr orbit of hydrogen atom is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. a₀ = 0.529 Å (angstroms). Note: 1 Å = 10⁻¹⁰ m = 0.1 nm.
questions.ncert_line: First Bohr radius a₀ = 0.529 Å = 0.0529 nm = 52.9 pm.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A wire of length L and cross-sectional area A has Young's modulus Y. Its spring constant is:
questions.explanation: Correct: A. k = YA/L. More area or shorter wire = stiffer spring.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 9 — Mechanical Properties of Solids, Section 9.3
questions.ncert_line: Spring constant k = Force/extension = (YA/L). From Y = (F/A)/(ΔL/L) → F = YAΔl/L → k = YA/L.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The root mean square (rms) speed of gas molecules is proportional to:
questions.explanation: Correct: A. v_rms ∝ √T. Also v_rms = √(3kT/m) per molecule.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 13 — Kinetic Theory, Section 13.5
questions.ncert_line: v_rms = √(3RT/M) ∝ √T. Doubling absolute temperature increases rms speed by √2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is used in radar systems?
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Microwaves in radar. Infrared = heat sensors/remote controls. UV = sterilisation.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 8 — Electromagnetic Waves, Section 8.4
questions.ncert_line: Microwaves (wavelength cm-range) are used in radar (Radio Detection And Ranging) and microwave ovens.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If the distance between two masses is tripled, the gravitational force between them becomes:
questions.explanation: Correct: D. F ∝ 1/r². Tripling distance → F becomes 1/9.
questions.ncert_line: F ∝ 1/r². If r → 3r, F → F/9. Force becomes 1/9th.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A parallel plate capacitor has plate area A and separation d. If a dielectric (κ) is inserted, capacitance becomes:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. C = κε₀A/d. Dielectric increases capacitance by factor κ.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 2 — Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance, Section 2.15
questions.ncert_line: With dielectric: C = κε₀A/d. Dielectric constant κ > 1, so capacitance increases.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A p-n junction diode is forward biased when:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Forward bias: p→+, n→−. Reduces barrier → current flows. Reverse bias: p→−, n→+ → no current.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 14 — Semiconductor Electronics, Section 14.6
questions.ncert_line: Forward bias: p-side to + terminal, n-side to − terminal. This reduces depletion region and allows current.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In nuclear fission of U-235, the energy released per fission is approximately:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. U-235 fission ≈ 200 MeV/fission. Chemical reactions: ~few eV. Hence nuclear energy is millions of times more.
questions.ncert_line: Each fission of U-235 releases ~200 MeV of energy. This is ~10⁷ times more energy per reaction than chemical combustion.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which particles are emitted in the decay: ²³⁸U → ²⁰⁶Pb?
questions.explanation: Correct: A. 8 alpha + 6 beta decays: ΔA = 32 (8×4), ΔZ = 10 (−16+6 = −10 gives Pb at Z=82).
questions.ncert_line: Mass change: 238−206=32 → 8α particles (each reduces mass by 4). Atomic number: 92−82=10, but 8α reduces Z by 16, so 6β restores 6. Net: 8α+6β⁻.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The process of superimposing a low-frequency signal on a high-frequency carrier wave is called:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Modulation = message signal + carrier wave. Demodulation = extracting message at receiver.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 15 — Communication Systems, Section 15.3
questions.ncert_line: Modulation: low-frequency message signal is superimposed on high-frequency carrier wave for transmission.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Sky waves are used for long-distance communication because they are:

options.B: Reflected by the ionosphere back to Earth

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Sky waves reflected by ionosphere. This allows radio signals to travel beyond horizon.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 15 — Communication Systems, Section 15.5
questions.ncert_line: Sky waves (shortwave, 3-30 MHz) are reflected by the ionosphere back to Earth, enabling long-distance communication.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A liquid drop is spherical due to:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Surface tension → minimum surface area → spherical shape for liquid drops.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 10 — Mechanical Properties of Fluids, Section 10.6
questions.ncert_line: Surface tension minimises surface area for a given volume. Sphere has minimum surface area → liquid drops are spherical.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Stefan's law states that the power radiated by a black body is proportional to:
questions.explanation: Correct: D. P ∝ T⁴ (Stefan's law). Doubling temperature increases radiated power by 16 times.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 11 — Thermal Properties of Matter, Section 11.9
questions.ncert_line: Stefan-Boltzmann law: P = σAT⁴. Power radiated ∝ T⁴. σ = 5.67×10⁻⁸ W m⁻² K⁻⁴.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An electric dipole in a uniform electric field experiences:

options.A: A net force and a torque

questions.explanation: Correct: C. Uniform field: no net force, but torque acts to align dipole with field.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 1 — Electric Charges and Fields, Section 1.12
questions.ncert_line: In uniform E field: equal and opposite forces on +q and −q cancel → net force = 0. But torque τ = pE sinθ ≠ 0.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The resistance of a conductor doubles if its length is doubled and area is halved. The new resistance is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. R ∝ l/A. Length doubled AND area halved → R becomes 2×2 = 4 times original.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 3 — Current Electricity, Section 3.3
questions.ncert_line: R = ρl/A. New R = ρ(2l)/(A/2) = 4ρl/A = 4R.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The wavelength range of visible light is approximately:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Visible spectrum: 400–700 nm. VIBGYOR: Violet (400nm) → Red (700nm).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 8 — Electromagnetic Waves, Section 8.3
questions.ncert_line: Visible light: 400 nm (violet) to 700 nm (red). UV < 400 nm. IR > 700 nm.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In a single slit diffraction pattern, the width of the central maximum is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Width ∝ 1/a. Narrower slit → more diffraction → wider central maximum.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 10 — Wave Optics, Section 10.4
questions.ncert_line: Central maximum width = 2λD/a. Inversely proportional to slit width 'a'. Narrower slit → wider central maximum.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In a parallel plate capacitor, if a dielectric slab is inserted between the plates while keeping the battery connected, which of the following INCREASES?
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Battery connected → V constant. C increases (dielectric) → Q = CV increases.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 2 — Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance, Section 2.15
questions.ncert_line: Battery maintains constant V. Inserting dielectric: C = κε₀A/d increases. Q = CV → Q increases. E = V/d decreases.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In a potentiometer experiment, when the jockey is moved along the wire, the galvanometer shows zero deflection at a point. This point indicates:

options.B: Null point where EMF of cell = potential drop across wire length

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Null point = balance point where galvanometer reads zero. EMF = φ × l (φ = potential gradient).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 3 — Current Electricity, Section 3.15
questions.ncert_line: Null point in potentiometer: potential difference along wire length = EMF of cell being measured. No current through galvanometer.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In a series LCR circuit at resonance, the phase angle between current and voltage is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. At resonance φ = 0° (current and voltage in phase). Circuit behaves as pure resistor.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 7 — Alternating Current, Section 7.7
questions.ncert_line: At resonance XL = XC, so net reactance = 0. Z = R. Phase angle φ = tan⁻¹((XL−XC)/R) = tan⁻¹(0) = 0°.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: When a ray of light travels from a denser medium to a rarer medium at an angle greater than the critical angle:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. TIR: angle > critical angle → no refraction, complete reflection. Basis of optical fibre.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 9 — Ray Optics, Section 9.4
questions.ncert_line: Total internal reflection (TIR) occurs when angle of incidence > critical angle in denser medium.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In Rutherford's gold foil experiment, most alpha particles passed straight through because:

options.B: The nucleus is very small and most of the atom is empty space

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Atom mostly empty space → most α particles undeflected. Few hit nucleus → large angle scattering.
questions.ncert_line: Most of atom is empty space — tiny dense nucleus at centre. Most alpha particles miss the nucleus and pass through.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The second law of thermodynamics states that heat cannot spontaneously flow from:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Heat flows spontaneously from hot→cold, never cold→hot (without work). This defines arrow of time.
questions.ncert_line: Second law: heat cannot spontaneously flow from a cold body to a hot body without external work (Clausius statement).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A spring-mass system has time period T. If the spring constant is doubled and mass is halved, new time period is:
questions.explanation: Correct: A. T' = 2π√(m'/k') = 2π√(m/4k) = T/2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Beats are produced when two sound waves have:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Beat frequency = |f₁ − f₂|. Audible beats when difference < 10 Hz.
questions.ncert_line: Beats = periodic rise and fall in intensity. Produced by superposition of two waves with slightly different frequencies.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: When a dielectric is inserted between capacitor plates (battery disconnected), which quantity increases?

options.D: Capacitance and charge stored remain same; energy decreases

questions.explanation: Correct: D. Battery off: Q fixed. Dielectric → C↑, V↓, E↓, U↓. Energy released → dielectric pulled in.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 2 — Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance, Section 2.15
questions.ncert_line: Battery disconnected → Q constant. Inserting dielectric: C increases, V = Q/C decreases, E = V/d decreases, U = Q²/2C decreases.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The net magnetic moment of an antiferromagnetic material is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Antiferromagnetic = zero net moment (anti-parallel moments cancel). Example: MnO, Cr.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 5 — Magnetism and Matter, Section 5.5
questions.ncert_line: Antiferromagnetic: adjacent magnetic moments are anti-parallel and equal → net magnetic moment = 0.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Power is defined as:
questions.explanation: Correct: D. P = W/t = F·v. Both are correct definitions of power.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 6 — Work, Energy and Power, Section 6.7
questions.ncert_line: Power P = W/t (average) = dW/dt = F·v (instaneous). Both definitions are valid.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The orbital period of a satellite at height h above Earth's surface depends on:

options.B: Radius of orbit (R+h) only (and Earth's mass)

questions.explanation: Correct: B. T = 2π√(r³/GM). Independent of satellite mass. (Kepler's 3rd law: T² ∝ r³.)
questions.ncert_line: T = 2π√((R+h)³/GM). Period depends on orbital radius (R+h) and Earth's mass M — not satellite mass.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Ohm's law is NOT valid for:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Diode: non-ohmic (non-linear I-V). Metals, electrolytes, resistors are approximately ohmic.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 3 — Current Electricity, Section 3.5
questions.ncert_line: Semiconductor diode has non-linear I-V characteristic — Ohm's law (linear I-V) does not apply.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The magnifying power of a simple microscope with focal length f, for image at near point D is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. m = 1 + D/f for image at near point. m = D/f for image at infinity.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 9 — Ray Optics, Section 9.10
questions.ncert_line: Magnifying power of simple microscope = 1 + D/f (when image is at near point D = 25 cm).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In a p-n junction diode under forward bias:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Forward bias: potential barrier reduced → large current. Reverse bias: barrier increased → negligible current.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 14 — Semiconductor Electronics, Section 14.6
questions.ncert_line: Forward bias reduces potential barrier → majority carriers flow easily → low resistance → large current.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Young's modulus has the same dimensions as:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Y = stress/strain. Since strain is dimensionless, Y has dimensions of stress (Pa).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 9 — Mechanical Properties of Solids, Section 9.4
questions.ncert_line: Young's modulus = Stress/Strain. Strain is dimensionless, so Y has same dimensions as stress = Pa = N/m².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following statements about friction is INCORRECT?

options.A: Kinetic friction is less than maximum static friction
options.B: Rolling friction is much less than sliding friction
options.C: Coefficient of kinetic friction μk > coefficient of static friction μs
options.D: Friction acts tangentially to the surfaces in contact

questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 5 — Laws of Motion, Section 5.8: Friction
questions.ncert_line: Static friction acts up to a maximum value (fs ≤ μs N). Kinetic friction fk = μk N is constant and less than maximum static friction (μk < μs). Rolling friction is much smaller than sliding friction.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Statement I: The moment of inertia of a solid sphere about its diameter is 2MR²/5.
Statement II: The moment of inertia of a hollow sphere (thin shell) about its diameter is 2MR²/3.

options.B: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
options.C: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: Correct: A. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 7 — Systems of Particles and Rotational Motion, Section 7.6–7.7
questions.ncert_line: Torque τ = r × F = Iα. Angular momentum L = Iω. The moment of inertia of a solid sphere about its diameter = 2MR²/5; a ring about its axis = MR²; a disc about its axis = MR²/2; a hollow sphere about its diameter = 2MR²/3.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is INCORRECT about a geostationary satellite?

options.B: It orbits in the equatorial plane
options.C: It can be at any altitude above the Earth
options.D: It appears stationary relative to a point on Earth

questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 8 — Gravitation, Section 8.7: Earth Satellites
questions.ncert_line: For a satellite in a circular orbit at height h above Earth's surface: orbital velocity v₀ = √(GM/(R+h)). The time period T = 2π(R+h)/v₀. For h << R, v₀ ≈ √(gR) ≈ 7.9 km/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The efficiency of a Carnot engine operating between source temperature T₁ = 800 K and sink temperature T₂ = 400 K is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 12 — Thermodynamics, Section 12.9: Carnot Engine
questions.ncert_line: The efficiency of a Carnot engine η = 1 − T₂/T₁, where T₁ is the temperature of the source and T₂ is the temperature of the sink. No engine can be more efficient than a Carnot engine operating between the same temperatures.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Statement I: No heat engine operating between two temperatures can be more efficient than a Carnot engine operating between the same temperatures.
Statement II: The efficiency of a Carnot engine can be 100% if T₂ = 0 K.

options.B: Statement I is true but Statement II is practically impossible (Third Law prevents T₂ = 0)
options.C: Statement I is false but Statement II is true

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 12 — Thermodynamics, Section 12.9: Carnot Engine
questions.ncert_line: The efficiency of a Carnot engine η = 1 − T₂/T₁, where T₁ is the temperature of the source and T₂ is the temperature of the sink. No engine can be more efficient than a Carnot engine operating between the same temperatures.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two tuning forks of frequencies 512 Hz and 516 Hz are sounded simultaneously. The number of beats per second is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 15 — Waves, Section 15.7: Beats
questions.ncert_line: When two sources of slightly different frequencies f₁ and f₂ are superimposed, beats are heard. Beat frequency = |f₁ − f₂|. Maximum beats per second that the human ear can distinguish is about 10.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A parallel plate capacitor with air between the plates has capacitance C. If a dielectric slab (K = 3) completely fills the space, the new capacitance is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 2 — Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance, Section 2.9–2.10
questions.ncert_line: Capacitance C = Q/V. For a parallel plate capacitor C = ε₀A/d (no dielectric) or C = Kε₀A/d (with dielectric). Energy stored in a capacitor U = Q²/2C = CV²/2 = QV/2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is INCORRECT about the magnetic force on a moving charge?

options.A: It is always perpendicular to the velocity
options.B: It does positive work on the charge
options.C: It cannot change the speed of the charge
options.D: Its direction is given by F = qv × B

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 4 — Moving Charges and Magnetism, Section 4.2: Magnetic Force
questions.ncert_line: The magnetic force on a moving charge: F = qv × B = qvB sinθ. This force is always perpendicular to both v and B, so it does no work on the charge and cannot change kinetic energy.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The cyclotron frequency (f = qB/2πm) is independent of:
questions.explanation: Correct: D. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 4 — Moving Charges and Magnetism, Section 4.5: Cyclotron
questions.ncert_line: In a cyclotron, the radius of circular motion r = mv/qB. The cyclotron frequency f = qB/2πm — it is independent of the speed and radius (for non-relativistic speeds). Cyclotrons cannot accelerate electrons (too light, quickly become relativistic).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is INCORRECT about a transformer?

options.A: It works on the principle of mutual induction
options.B: It can work with both AC and DC
options.C: In a step-up transformer, Vs > Vp
options.D: An ideal transformer conserves power (Pin = Pout)

questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 7 — Alternating Current, Section 7.10: Transformer
questions.ncert_line: For an ideal transformer: Vs/Vp = Ns/Np = Ip/Is. A step-up transformer increases voltage but decreases current proportionally (power is conserved). Transformers work only with AC, not DC.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two thin lenses of focal lengths f₁ = 20 cm and f₂ = −30 cm are placed in contact. The equivalent focal length is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 9 — Ray Optics, Section 9.6: Thin Lenses
questions.ncert_line: Lens formula: 1/v − 1/u = 1/f. Lensmaker's equation: 1/f = (n−1)(1/R₁ − 1/R₂). Power P = 1/f (in dioptres when f is in metres). For thin lenses in contact: 1/f = 1/f₁ + 1/f₂.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In Young's double slit experiment, the fringe width β = λD/d. If λ = 600 nm, D = 1 m, and d = 0.2 mm, the fringe width is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 10 — Wave Optics, Section 10.3: Young's Double Slit
questions.ncert_line: In Young's double slit experiment, fringe width β = λD/d, where λ is wavelength, D is slit-to-screen distance, and d is slit separation. Constructive interference (bright fringe): path difference = nλ. Destructive: (n + ½)λ.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
questions.question: In the photoelectric effect, which of the following statements is INCORRECT?

options.A: Increasing the intensity increases the number of emitted electrons
options.B: Kinetic energy of photoelectrons increases with frequency of light
options.C: There is a minimum threshold frequency below which no emission occurs
options.D: Increasing intensity of light increases the maximum KE of photoelectrons

questions.explanation: Correct: D. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 11 — Dual Nature of Radiation, Section 11.4: Photoelectric Effect
questions.ncert_line: The work function (φ) is the minimum energy need to eject a photoelectron from a metal surface. Threshold frequency ν₀ = φ/h. The stopping potential V₀ is related to maximum KE by eV₀ = hν − φ.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A photon is emitted when a hydrogen atom transitions from n=3 to n=2. This emission corresponds to which spectral series?
questions.explanation: Correct: B. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 12 — Atoms, Section 12.4: Hydrogen Spectrum
questions.ncert_line: Hydrogen spectrum series: Lyman series (UV, n→1), Balmer series (visible, n→2), Paschen series (IR, n→3), Brackett (IR, n→4), Pfund (IR, n→5). The Rydberg formula: 1/λ = R(1/n₁² − 1/n₂²).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: At absolute zero (0 K), a pure semiconductor behaves like a/an:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 14 — Semiconductor Electronics, Section 14.4–14.5
questions.ncert_line: In intrinsic semiconductors, electrons and holes are always equal (ne = nh). In n-type (donor impurity), electrons are majority carriers. In p-type (acceptor impurity), holes are majority carriers. At 0 K, semiconductors behave as insulators.

questions.explanation: Correct: B. h = ½gt² → t = √(2h/g) = √(40/10) = 2 s.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 4 — Motion in a Plane, Section 4.6
questions.ncert_line: h = ½gt² → 20 = ½ × 10 × t² → t² = 4 → t = 2 s.

questions.question: A body starts from rest and accelerates uniformly at 4 m/s². Distance covered in the 5th second is:

questions.question: The rms value of an AC voltage V = V₀ sinωt is:
questions.explanation: Correct: C. Vrms = V₀/√2. Similarly Irms = I₀/√2.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 12 Physics, Chapter 7 — Alternating Current, Section 7.2
questions.ncert_line: Vrms = V0/√2 = 0.707 V0. For household supply: 230 V rms means peak voltage = 230√2 ≈ 325 V.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A concave mirror of focal length 15 cm forms an image at infinity. The object is at:

questions.ncert_line: For concave mirror: image at infinity when object is at focus (u = f = 15 cm).

questions.ncert_line: Width ∝ 1/a. Halving a doubles the central maximum width.

questions.question: In hydrogen atom, the wavelength of emitted radiation when electron jumps from n=3 to n=2 is (Hα line):

questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 7 — System of Particles and Rotational Motion, Section 7.6
questions.ncert_line: τ = Iα → α = τ/I = 10/2 = 5 rad/s².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A 2 kg block slides down a frictionless incline of height 5 m. Its speed at the bottom is: (g = 10 m/s²)
questions.explanation: Correct: B. v = √(2gh) = √(100) = 10 m/s.
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 6 — Work Energy and Power, Section 6.3
questions.ncert_line: Energy conservation: mgh = ½mv² → v = √(2gh) = √(2×10×5) = √100 = 10 m/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A train 100 m long travelling at 72 km/h crosses a bridge 1 km long. Time to cross completely is:
