questions.ncert_line: Speed = 72 km/h = 20 m/s. Total distance = 1000 + 100 = 1100 m. Time = 1100/20 = 55 s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A block of mass 5 kg on a frictionless surface is pulled by a force of 15 N. The acceleration is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. a = F/m = 15/5 = 3 m/s².
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 5 — Laws of Motion, Section 5.2
questions.ncert_line: F = ma → a = F/m = 15/5 = 3 m/s².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A 2 kg ball moving at 10 m/s collides with a wall and bounces back at 8 m/s. Change in momentum is:
questions.explanation: Correct: B. |Δp| = m|v_f − v_i| = 2 × 18 = 36 kg m/s (taking original direction as positive).
questions.ncert_ref: Class 11 Physics, Chapter 6 — Work, Energy and Power, Section 6.6
questions.ncert_line: Δp = m(v_f − v_i) = 2 × (−8 − 10) = 2 × (−18) = −36 kg m/s. Magnitude = 36 kg m/s.

questions.explanation: Q=at−bt2 i=a−2bt{fori=0⇒t=a2b}i=a−2bt{fori=0⇒t=2ba} From joule's law of heating dH=i2Rdt H=∫0a/2b(a−2bt)2RdtH=(a−2bt)3R−3×2b∣0a2b=a3R6bH=−3×2b(a−2bt)3R02ba=6ba3R

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A black body is at a temperature of 5760 K. The energy of radiation emitted by the body at wavelength 250nm is U1, at wavelength 500nm is U2 and that at 1000nm is U3. Wien's constant, b=2.88×106nmK. Which of the following is correct?
questions.explanation: Maximum amount of emitted radiation corresponding to λm=bTλm=Tb λm=2.88×106nmK5760K=500nmλm=5760K2.88×106nmK=500nm From the graph U₁<U₂>U3U1<U₂>U₃

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A npn transistor is connected in common emitter configuration in a given amplifier. A load resistance of 800Ω is connected in the collector circuit and the voltage drop across it is 0.8 V₀.8 V. If the current amplification factor is 0.96 and the input resistance of the circuit is 192Ω, the voltage gain and the power gain of the amplifier will respectively be:
questions.explanation: Given α=0.96 so,β=α₁−α=0.960.04⇒β=24so,β=1−αα=0.040.96⇒β=24 Voltage gain for common emitter configuration Av=β⋅RLRP=24×800192=100Av=β⋅RPRL=24×192800=100 Power gain for common emitter configuration Pv=βAv=24×10^0=2400 Voltage gain for common base configuration Av=α⋅RLRP=0.96×800192=4Av=α⋅RPRL=0.96×192800=4 Power gain for common base configuration Pv=Avα=4×0.96=3.84 *In the question it is asked about common emitter configuration but we got above answer for common base configuration.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The intensity at the maximum in a Young's double slit experiment is I0. Distance between two slits is d=5λ, where λλ is the wavelength of light used in the experiment. What will be the intensity in front of one of the slits on the screen placed at a distance D=10 dD=10 d ?
questions.explanation: Path difference =S2P−S1P=D²+d²−D=D(1+12d2D2)−D=D(1+21D2d2)−D=D[1+d22D2−1]=d22D=D[1+2D2d2−1]=2Dd2Δx=d22×10d=d20=5λ20=λ4Δx=2×10dd2=20d=205λ=4λΔϕ=2πλ⋅λ4=π2Δϕ=λ2π⋅4λ=2π So, intensity at the desired point is I=I0cos2ϕ2=I0cos2π4=I02I=I0cos22ϕ=I0cos24π=2I0

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: System of Particles and Rotational Motion
questions.question: A uniform circular disc of radius 50 cm50 cm at rest is free to turn about an axis which is perpendicular to its plane and passes through its centre. It is subjected to a torque which produces a constant angular acceleration of 2.0rads−2. Its net acceleration in ms−2 at the end of 2.0 s 2.0 s is approximately :
questions.explanation: Particle at periphery will have both radial and tangential acceleration at=Rα=0.5×2=1m/s2ω=ω₀+αtω=0+2×2=4rad/secac=ω2R=(4)2×0.5=16×0.5=8m/s2atotal=ap2+ac2=12+82≈8m/s2 *In this question we have assumed the point to be located at periphery of the disc.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: System of Particles and Rotational Motion
questions.question: A disk and a sphere of same radius but different masses roll off on two inclined planes of the same altitude and length. Which one of the two objects gets to the bottom of the plane first ?

options.C: Both reach at the same time

questions.explanation: a=gsin⁡θ₁+K2R2a=1+R2K2gsinθ for disc;K2R2=12=0.5for disc;R2K2=21=0.5for sphere;K2R2=25=0.4for sphere;R2K2=52=0.4 a(sphere) >> a(disc) sphere reaches first

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The angle of incidence for a ray of light at a refracting surface of a prism is 45∘. The angle of prism is 60∘. If the ray suffers minimum deviation through the prism, the angle of minimum deviation and refractive index of the material of the prism respectively, are :

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: When an αα -particle of mass mm moving with velocity vv bombards on a heavy nucleus of charge Ze, its distance of closest approach from the nucleus depends on m as :
questions.explanation: At closest distance of approach, the kinetic energy of the particle will convert completely into electrostatic potential energy. ⇒12mv2=KQqd⇒d∝1m⇒21mv2=dKQq⇒d∝m₁

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle of mass 10g moves along a circle of radius 6.4cm with a constant tangential acceleration. What is the magnitude of this acceleration if the kinetic energy of the particle becomes equal to 8×10−4J by the end of the second revolution after the beginning of the motion?

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The molecules of a given mass of a gas have r.m.s. velocity of 200ms−1 at 27∘C and 1.0×105Nm−2 pressure. When the temperature and pressure of the gas are respectively, 127∘C and 0.05×105Nm−2, the r.m.s. velocity of its molecules in ms−1 is :

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A long straight wire of radius a carries a steady current I. The current is uniformly distributed over its cross-section. The ratio of the magnetic fields B and B', at radial distances a22a and 2a respectively, from the axis of the wire is :
questions.explanation: For points inside the wire B=μ0Ir2πR2(r≤R)B=2πR2μ0Ir(r≤R) For points outside the wire B=μ0I2πr(r≥R)B=2πrμ0I(r≥R) according to the question BB′=μ0I(a/2)2πa2μ0I2π(2a)=1:1B′B=2π(2a)μ0I2πa2μ0I(a/2)=1:1

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle moves so that its position vector is given by τ⃗=cos⁡ωtx^+sin⁡ωty^τ=cosωtx^+sinωty^. Where ωω is a constant. Which of the following is true ?

options.A: Velocity and acceleration both are perpendicular to τ⃗τ.
options.B: Velocity and acceleration both are parallel to τ⃗τ
options.C: Velocity is perpendicular to τ⃗τ and acceleration is directed towards the origin
options.D: Velocity is perpendicular to τ⃗τ and acceleration is directed away from the origin

questions.explanation: τ⃗=cos⁡ωtx^+sin⁡ωty^τ=cosωtx^+sinωty^ υ⃗=−ωsin⁡ωtx^+ωcos⁡ωty^υ=−ωsinωtx^+ωcosωty^ a⃗=−ω2cos⁡ωtx^+ωsin⁡ωty^=−ω2τ⃗a=−ω2cosωtx^+ωsinωty^=−ω2τ τ⃗.υ⃗=0τ.υ=0 hence τ⃗⊥υ⃗τ⊥υ a⃗a is directed towards the origin.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: What is the minimum velocity with which a body of mass m must enter a vertical loop of radius R so that it can complete the loop ?
questions.explanation: When minimum speed of body is 5gR, then no matter from where it enters the loop, it will complete full vertical loop.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: When a metallic surface is illuminated with radiation of wavelength λλ, the stopping potential is V. If the same surface is illuminated with radiation of wavelength 2λ, the stopping potential is V44V. The threshold wavelength for the metallic surface is :
questions.explanation: eV=hcλ−hcλ0eV=λhc−λ0hc...(i) eV/4=hc2λ−hcλ0eV/4=2λhc−λ0hc...(ii) From equation (i) and (ii) ⇒4=1λ−1λ012λ−1λ0⇒4=2λ1−λ01 On solving λ0=3λ

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A potentiometer wire is 100cm long and a constant potential difference is maintained across it. Two cells are connected in series first to support one another and then in opposite direction. The balance points are obtained at 50 cm50 cm and 10 cm10 cm from the positive end of the wire in the two cases. The ratio of emfs is :-

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A astronomical telescope has objective and eyepiece of focal lengths 40 cm and 4 cm respectively. To view an object 200 cm away from the objective, the lenses must be separated by a distance :-

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two non-mixing liquids of densities ρρ and nρ (n > 1) are put in a container. The height of each liquid is h. A solid cylinder of length L and density d is put in this container. The cylinder floats with its axis vertical and length pL(p < 1) in the denser liquid. The density d is equal to :-
questions.explanation: L A d g = (pL) A (nρρ)g + (1 – p) L A ρρ g ⇒d=(1–p)ρ+pnρ=[1+(n–1)p]ρ

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: To get output 1 for the following circuit, the correct choice for the input is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The ratio of escape velocity at earth (ve) to the escape velocity at a planet (vp) whose radius and mean density are twice as that of earth is :-
questions.explanation: Ve=2GMR=2GR⋅43πR3ρ∝RρVe=R2GM=R2G⋅34πR3ρ∝Rρ ∴∴ Ratio = 1 : 2√2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If the magnitude of sum of two vectors is equal to the magnitude of difference of the two vectors, the angle between these vectors is :-
questions.explanation: ∣Aˉ+Bˉ∣=∣Aˉ−Bˉ∣=0=90∘.Aˉ+Bˉ=Aˉ−Bˉ=0=90∘.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Given the value of Rydberg constant is 107m−1, the wave number of the last line of the Balmer series in hydrogen spectrum will be :-

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A body of mass 1kg begins to move under the action of a time dependent force Fˉ=(2ti^+3t2j^)N where i^ and j^ are unit vectors along xx and yy axis. What power will be developed by the force at the time tt ?
questions.explanation: Fˉ=2ti^+3t2j^ mdvˉdt=2ti^+3t2j^mdtdvˉ=2ti^+3t2j^ (m=1kg) ⇒∫0vˉdvˉ=∫0t(2ti^+3t2j^)dt⇒vˉ=t2i^+t3j^ Power =Fˉ.vˉ=(2t³+3t⁵)W

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An inductor 20mH, a capacitor 50μF and a resistor 40Ω are connected in series across a source of emf V=10 sin 340t. The power loss in A.C. circuit is :-

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If the velocity of a particle is v=At+Bt2, where A and B are constants, then the distance travelled by it between 1s and 2s is :-

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A long solenoid has 1000 turns. When a current of 4A flows through it, the magnetic flux linked with each turn of the solenoid is 4×10⁻³⁴×10⁻³ Wb. The self inductance of the solenoid is :
questions.explanation: Flux linked with each turn =4×10⁻³ Wb ∴Total flux linked=1000[4×10⁻³]Wb∴Total flux linked=1000[4×10⁻³]Wb ϕtotal=4⇒Li=4⇒L=1H

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A body of mass 10 kg moving with speed of 3 m/s collides with another stationary body of mass 5 kg. As a result, the two bodies stick together. The KE of composite mass will be
questions.explanation: Using momentum conservation: 10(3) = 15V, V = 2 m/s. KE = ½(15)(2²) = 30 J

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Select the false statement about collisions

options.A: In elastic collision, KE is not conserved during the collision
options.B: The coefficient of restitution for a collision between two steel balls lies between 0 and 1
options.C: The momentum of a ball colliding elastically with the floor is conserved
options.D: In an oblique elastic collision between two identical bodies with one of them at rest initially, the final velocities are perpendicular

questions.explanation: Momentum of a ball colliding elastically with floor is NOT conserved as floor exerts external impulse on the system.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A bullet of mass m moving with velocity v strikes a suspended wooden block of mass M. If the block rises to height h, the initial velocity of the bullet will be
questions.explanation: Using momentum conservation: mv = (m+M)v₁, v₁ = √(2gh), so v = (M+m)/m × √(2gh)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A ball is allowed to fall from a height of 10 m. If there is 40% loss of energy due to impact, then after one impact ball will go up by
questions.explanation: 60% energy retained. 0.6 × mg(10) = mgh, h = 6 m

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A bullet weighing 10 g and moving with a velocity 300 m/s strikes a 5 kg block of ice and drops dead. The ice block is kept on smooth surface. The speed of the block after the collision is
questions.explanation: Using momentum conservation: (10/1000)(300) = (5 + 10/1000)v, v = 0.6 m/s = 60 cm/s

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle of mass m moving eastward with speed v collides with another particle of the same mass moving northwards with same speed v. The two particles coalesce on collision. The new particle of mass 2m will move with velocity
questions.explanation: Using momentum conservation in x and y: resultant = v/√2 in North-East direction

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two perfectly elastic particles A and B of equal masses travelling along the line joing them with velocity 15 m/s and 10 m/s respectively, collide. Their velocities after the elastic collision will be (in m/s)
questions.explanation: In elastic collision between equal masses, velocities interchange: v₁ = 10 m/s, v₂ = 15 m/s

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two balls of equal mass undergo head on collision while each was moving with speed 6 m/s. If the coefficient of restitution is 1/3, the speed of each ball after impact will be

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A ball of mass M moving with speed v collides perfectly inelastically with another ball of mass m at rest. The magnitude of impulse imparted to the first ball is
questions.explanation: Impulse = change in momentum of first ball = Mv - M×[Mv/(M+m)] = Mmv/(M+m)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A force F = (3i + 4j) N acts on a particle moving in x-y plane. Starting from origin, the particle first goes along x-axis to the point (4,0) m and then parallel to y-axis to the point (4,3) m. The total work done by the force on the particle is
questions.explanation: Displacement = 4i + 3j. W = F·d = (3i+4j)·(4i+3j) = 12 + 12 = 24 J

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A bullet of mass 20 g leaves a rifle at initial speed 100 m/s and strikes a target at the same level with speed 50 m/s. The amount of work done by the resistance of air will be

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A knife of mass m is at a height x from a large wooden block. The knife is allowed to fall freely, strikes the block and comes to rest after penetrating distance y. The work done by the wooden block to stop the knife is
questions.explanation: By work-energy theorem: mg(x+y) + W_block = 0, so W_block = -mg(x+y)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A man is running on horizontal road with half the KE of a boy of half his mass. When man speeds up by 1 m/s, his KE becomes equal to KE of the boy. The original speed of the man is
questions.explanation: Setting up equations: ½(2m)V²/2 = ½(m)(v_boy)² and ½(2m)(V+1)² = ½(m)(v_boy)², solving gives V = √2 + 1 m/s

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle of mass m starts moving from origin along x-axis and its velocity varies with position x as v = k√x. The work done by force acting on it during first t seconds is
questions.explanation: From v = k√x: a = k²/2, x = ½at² = k²t²/4. W = F×x = m(k²/2)(k²t²/4) = mk⁴t²/8

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle is moving in a circular path of radius r under the action of a force F. If at an instant velocity of particle is v, and speed of particle is increasing, then
questions.explanation: Net force has both centripetal and tangential components. Angle between F and v is between 0° and 90°, so F·v > 0

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A block of mass 2 kg is released from the top of an inclined smooth surface. If spring constant is 100 N/m and block comes to rest after compressing the spring by 1 m, the distance travelled by block before it comes to rest is
questions.explanation: mgh sin45° = ½k(1)², solving h = 5 m

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If net force on a system is zero then

options.C: The acceleration of its constituent particle may be non-zero

questions.explanation: Due to internal forces, KE and acceleration of constituent particles may be non-zero even when net external force is zero.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Internal forces acting within a system of particles can alter

options.A: The linear momentum as well as the kinetic energy of the system
options.B: The linear momentum of the system, but not the kinetic energy
options.C: The kinetic energy of the system, but not the linear momentum
options.D: Neither linear momentum nor kinetic energy

questions.explanation: Internal forces can change KE but not total linear momentum as F_ext = 0.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In the figure, a particle is released from position A on a smooth track. When the particle reaches B, then normal reaction on it by the track is
questions.explanation: Using energy conservation from A(height 3h) to B(height 2h): v² = 2gh. At B: mg + N = mv²/h = m(2gh)/h, N = mg

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle of mass m is projected with speed u at angle θ with horizontal from ground. The work done by gravity on it during its upward motion is
questions.explanation: Height = u²sin²θ/2g. W = -mg × u²sin²θ/2g = -mu²sin²θ/2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle is moving along a vertical circle of radius R. At P, what will be the velocity of particle (assume critical condition at C)?
questions.explanation: At critical condition at C: v_c = √(gR). Using energy conservation from P to C with 60° angle: v = √(2gR)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle attached to end of string of length l is released from horizontal position. When vertically below O, string contacts nail N at distance h below O. For particle to swing completely around the nail,
questions.explanation: Using energy conservation: mgl = ½m(5g(l-h)), solving: h = 3l/5

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If F = 2x² - 3x - 2, then select the correct statement

options.A: x = -1/2 is the position of stable equilibrium
options.B: x = 2 is the position of stable equilibrium
options.C: x = -1/2 is the position of unstable equilibrium
options.D: x = 2 is the position of neutral equilibrium

questions.explanation: Setting F=0: x = 2 or x = -1/2. At x = -1/2, d²V/dx² > 0 → stable equilibrium

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: When a conservative force does positive work on a body, then the

options.C: Total mechanical energy of body increases
options.D: Total mechanical energy of body decreases

questions.explanation: F = -dU/dx, so when F does positive work, dU is negative → PE decreases

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The variation of force F acting on a body moving along x-axis varies with its position x. The body is in stable equilibrium state at
questions.explanation: Stable equilibrium is where F=0 and dF/dx < 0 (i.e., -d²U/dx² < 0), which corresponds to Q

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle in one dimensional potential field has U(x) = a/x² - b/x³. The position of equilibrium corresponds to x =
questions.explanation: F = -dU/dx = 0 at equilibrium: -2a/x³ + 3b/x⁴ = 0, solving x = 3b/2a

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The force required to row a boat at constant velocity is proportional to square of its speed. If a speed of v km/h requires 4 kW, how much power does a speed of 2v km/h require?
questions.explanation: F ∝ v², P = Fv ∝ v³. P₁/P₂ = v³/(2v)³ = 1/8, so P₂ = 32 kW

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A body of mass m is projected from ground with speed u at angle θ with horizontal. The power delivered by gravity at half of maximum height from ground is
questions.explanation: At half H_max, v_y = u sinθ/√2. P = -mg × v_y = -mgu sinθ/√2 (options 2 and 3 are equivalent)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle of mass m moves in circular path of radius r under constant power p. The angular acceleration of particle at time t is proportional to
questions.explanation: Pt = ½m(rω)², so ω² ∝ t. Using α = ω/t: α ∝ 1/√t

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The rate of doing work by force acting on a particle moving along x-axis depends on position x and equals 2x. The velocity of particle is given by
questions.explanation: P = F·v = m(v dv/dx)v = 2x, so mv² dv = 2x dx, mv³/3 = x², v = (3x²/m)^(1/3)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A small ball of mass m moving with speed v undergoes elastic head on collision with a stationary bob of identical mass of a simple pendulum of length L. The maximum height h to which the bob rises after collision is
questions.explanation: In elastic collision of equal masses, full velocity transfers. ½mv² = mgh → h = v²/2g

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two balls of masses m each moving at right angles with velocities 6 m/s and 8 m/s respectively. If collision is perfectly inelastic, the velocity of combined mass is
questions.explanation: Resultant momentum = m√(6²+8²) = 10m. v = 10m/2m = 5 m/s

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A sphere of mass m moving with constant velocity u hits another stationary sphere of same mass. If e is coefficient of restitution, ratio of velocities v₂/v₁ after collision is
questions.explanation: v₁ = u/2(1+e), v₂ = u/2(1-e), ratio v₂/v₁ = (1-e)/(1+e)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A neutron travelling with a velocity collides elastically head on with a nucleus of mass number A at rest. The fraction of total energy retained by neutron is
questions.explanation: v₁ = v(A-1)/(A+1). Fraction = v₁²/v² = ((A-1)/(A+1))²

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A small ball hits obliquely a smooth horizontal surface with speed u whose x and y components are indicated. If coefficient of restitution is 1/2, its x and y components vx and vy just after collision are respectively
questions.explanation: vy = e × uy = ½ × 4 = 2 m/s; vx = ux = 2 m/s (no friction on smooth surface)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An object of mass M₁ moving horizontally with speed u collides elastically with another object of mass M₂ at rest. Select correct statement

options.A: The momentum of system is conserved only in direction PQ
options.B: Momentum of M₁ is conserved in direction perpendicular to SR
options.C: Momentum of M₂ will change in direction normal to CR

questions.explanation: In oblique collision, momentum of M₁ is conserved perpendicular to the line of impact (SR)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A ball of mass m moving with speed u collides with a smooth horizontal surface at angle θ. The magnitude of impulse imparted to surface by ball [coefficient of restitution e]
questions.explanation: Normal component: u_y = -u sinθ, v_y = +eu sinθ. Impulse = m(v_y - u_y) = mu(1+e)sinθ

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A body of mass m falls from height h. If e is coefficient of restitution, the distance travelled before it comes to rest is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The PE of a 2 kg particle is given by V(x) = (x³/3 - x²/2) J. The total mechanical energy is 4 J. Maximum speed (in m/s) is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two particles of masses m₁, m₂ move with initial velocities u₁ and u₂. On collision, one particle gets excited absorbing energy ε. If final velocities are v₁ and v₂ then we must have
questions.explanation: Initial KE minus absorbed energy ε equals final KE: KE_i - ε = KE_f

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two similar springs P and Q have spring constants KP > KQ. They are stretched first by same amount (case a), then by same force (case b). Work done WP and WQ are related as
questions.explanation: Case a: W = ½kx², KP > KQ → WP > WQ. Case b: W = F²/2k, KP > KQ → WQ > WP

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle of mass m is driven by a machine delivering constant power k watts. If particle starts from rest, force on particle at time t is
questions.explanation: P = F·v = const k. v = √(2kt/m). F = ma = m dv/dt = √(mk/2) t^(-1/2)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A block of mass 10 kg moving in x direction with constant speed 10 m/s is subjected to retarding force F = 0.1x J/m during travel from x=20 m to x=30 m. Its final KE will be
questions.explanation: Initial KE = ½(10)(10²) = 500 J. W = -∫₂₀³⁰ 0.1x dx = -0.1[x²/2]₂₀³⁰ = -25 J. Final KE = 475 J

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A body of mass 4m lying at rest explodes into three pieces. Two pieces each of mass m move perpendicular to each other with equal speeds v. Total KE generated is
questions.explanation: Third piece has mass 2m and velocity v/√2 (by momentum conservation). Total KE = ½mv² + ½mv² + ½(2m)(v/√2)² = 3mv²/2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A uniform force (3i + j) N acts on particle of mass 2 kg displaced from position (2i + k) m to position (4i + 3j - k) m. Work done is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Potential energy of a particle is U = A/r² - B/r. For stable equilibrium, the distance is
questions.explanation: F = -dU/dr = 0: 2A/r³ - B/r² = 0, r = 2A/B

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two spheres A and B of masses m₁ and m₂ collide. A is at rest initially and B moving with velocity v along x-axis. After collision B has velocity v/2 perpendicular to original direction. Mass A moves after collision in the direction
questions.explanation: Momentum conservation: m₂v i = -m₂(v/2)j + m₁v_A. θ = tan⁻¹(v/2 / v) = tan⁻¹(1/2) to x-axis

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A stone dropped from height h hits the ground with momentum P. If same stone is dropped from height 100% more, momentum when it hits the ground will change by

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A car starts from rest with constant power P₀. The instaneous velocity is proportional to
questions.explanation: W = P₀t = ½mv², so v² ∝ t, v ∝ t^(1/2)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The potential energy of a system increases if work is done

options.A: Upon the system by a conservative force
options.B: Upon the system by a non-conservative force
options.C: By the system against a conservative force
options.D: By the system against a non-conservative force

questions.explanation: dU = -∫F_c·dx. When system does work against conservative force, U increases

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An engine pumps water through a hose pipe. Water leaves with velocity 2 m/s. Mass per unit length of water in pipe is 100 kg/m. Power of the engine is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle of mass M starting from rest undergoes uniform acceleration. If speed acquired in time T is V, the power delivered to particle is
questions.explanation: W = ½MV². P = W/T = MV²/2T

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An engine pumps water continuously. Water leaves hose with velocity v and m is mass per unit length of water jet. Rate at which KE is imparted to water is
questions.explanation: Rate of KE = ½(dm/dt)v² = ½(mv)v² = ½mv³

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A body of mass 1 kg is thrown upwards with velocity 20 m/s. It comes to rest after attaing height 18 m. Energy lost due to air friction is (g=10 m/s²)
questions.explanation: W_friction + W_gravity = ΔKE = 0 - ½(1)(20²). W_f - mg(18) = -200. W_f = 200 - 180 = 20 J

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A block of mass M is attached to lower end of vertical spring of force constant k. Released from rest with spring unstretched. Maximum extension in spring will be
questions.explanation: Using energy conservation: Mgx = ½kx², x_max = 2Mg/k

questions.question: Water falls from height 60 m at rate 15 kg/s to operate turbine. Losses due to friction are 10%. Power generated by turbine (g=10 m/s²) is
questions.explanation: Power input = (dm/dt)gh = 15×60×10 = 9000 W. After 10% loss = 9000×0.9 = 8100 W = 8.1 kW

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A shell of mass 200 g is ejected from gun of mass 4 kg by explosion generating 1.05 kJ of energy. Initial velocity of shell is
questions.explanation: Momentum conservation: 0 = 0.2v + 4v₁, v₁ = -0.05v. Energy: ½(0.2)v² + ½(4)(0.05v)² = 10⁵⁰, solving v = 100 m/s

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A vertical spring of force constant K fixed on table. Ball of mass m falls from height h above spring, spring compressed by distance d. Net work done in process is
questions.explanation: W = W_gravity + W_spring = mg(h+d) - ½Kd²

questions.explanation: PE gained = mgh = 2×10 = 200 J. Work against friction = 300 - 200 = 100 J

questions.subject: Physics

questions.explanation: Momentum conservation: 0 = 18(6) + 12v, v = -9 m/s. KE = ½×12×9² = 486 J

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A force F acting on object varies with distance x. Work done in moving from x=0 to x=6 m is
questions.explanation: W = area under F-x curve = 3×3 + ½×3 = 9 + 4.5 = 13.5 J

questions.explanation: W = ∫₀²(x + x³)dx = [x²/2 + x⁴/4]₀² = 2 + 4 = 6 J

questions.explanation: Fs cosθ = 25, 5×10×cosθ = 25, cosθ = 1/2, θ = 60°

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A 1 kg stationary bomb explodes into three parts in ratio 1:3. Parts of equal mass move perpendicularly with velocity 30 m/s. Velocity of bigger part will be
questions.explanation: Momentum conservation: resultant of two m/5 parts = m/5 × 30√2. Bigger part (3m/5) has velocity = 30√2/3 = 10√2 m/s

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If KE of a body is increased by 300%, percentage change in momentum will be
questions.explanation: New KE = 4K. P = √(2mK). New P = √(8mK) = 2P. Change = 100%

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two particles m₁ > m₂ with same momentum have different KE E₁ and E₂ respectively. Then
questions.explanation: E = P²/2m. Same P, so E ∝ 1/m. m₁ > m₂ → E₁ < E₂

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A body dropped from height h hits ground with velocity 3 m/s. Another body of same mass thrown from same height with 4 m/s initial velocity. Final velocity with which it strikes ground is
questions.explanation: v² = u² + 2gh. First: 9 = 0 + 2gh → gh=4.5. Second: v² = 16 + 9 = 25, v = 5 m/s

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle with total energy E is moving in potential energy region U(x). Motion of particle is restricted to the region when
questions.explanation: KE = E - U(x) ≥ 0, so U(x) ≤ E for motion to be possible

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: KE acquired by mass m travelling distance d from rest under constant force is directly proportional to
questions.explanation: KE = Fd (work done), independent of mass

questions.question: A simple pendulum bob of mass m oscillates such that PB is H. If g is acceleration due to gravity, velocity of bob as it passes through B is
questions.explanation: ½mv² = mgH, v = √(2gH)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A car moving at 40 km/h can be stopped by brakes after at least 2 m. If same car is moving at 80 km/h, minimum stopping distance is
questions.explanation: s ∝ v² (same braking force). s₂ = s₁ × (80/40)² = 2 × 4 = 8 m

questions.subject: Physics

questions.explanation: mgl(1-cosθ) = ½mv², v = √(2gl(1-cosθ))

questions.question: Stone tied to string of length l whirled in vertical circle. At lowest position, speed is u. Magnitude of change in velocity as it reaches horizontal position is
questions.explanation: At horizontal: v'² = u² - 2gl. v_horizontal = √(u²-2gl). Initial velocity is horizontal. Δv = √(u² + v'² - 2u·v'·cos90°) = √(u² + u²-2gl) = √(2u²-2gl) = √(2(u²-gl))

questions.question: PE between two atoms is U(x) = a/x¹² - b/x⁶. Atom is in stable equilibrium when

questions.explanation: In elastic collision between equal masses, velocities interchange. Second particle gets v₁.

questions.explanation: m(3) = 3m × v, v = 1 km/h

questions.subject: Physics

questions.explanation: Momentum: 2 = v₁ + 2v₂. Restitution: 0.5 = (v₂-v₁)/2. Solving: v₁=0, v₂=1 m/s

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Metal ball of 2 kg moving at 36 km/h has head on collision with stationary ball of 3 kg. Both move together after collision. Loss in KE is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two springs A and B: KA = 2KB. Stretched by equal forces. If energy stored in A is EA, energy stored in B is
questions.explanation: U = F²/2k. UB/UA = KA/KB = 2, so UB = 2EA

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion: Internal forces can change the kinetic energy but not the momentum of the system. Reason: The net internal force on a system is always zero.

options.A: Both A and R are true, R is correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true, R is not correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true, R is false
options.D: A is false, R is true

questions.explanation: Internal forces can do work (change KE) but cannot change total momentum since net internal force is zero.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion: When a body moves in a circle the work done by centripetal force is always zero. Reason: Centripetal force is perpendicular to displacement at every instant.

options.A: Both A and R are true, R is correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true, R is not correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true, R is false
options.D: A is false, R is true

questions.explanation: Centripetal force ⊥ velocity, so W = F·v = 0

questions.question: The moment of inertia of a body depends on

questions.chapter: System of Particles and Rotational Motion

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: System of Particles and Rotational Motion

questions.question: Circular disc to maximize MI about geometrical axis using iron and aluminium. This is possible with

options.A: Aluminium at interior and iron surrounding it
options.B: Iron at interior and aluminium surrounding it
options.D: Iron at external surfaces with aluminium interior

questions.explanation: Higher density (iron) placed farther from axis maximizes I = Σmr²

questions.subject: Physics

questions.question: Two point masses m and 3m at distance r. MI about axis through CM perpendicular to line joing them is
questions.explanation: CM at 3r/4 from m. I = m(3r/4)² + 3m(r/4)² = 9mr²/16 + 3mr²/16 = 3mr²/4

questions.chapter: System of Particles and Rotational Motion

questions.explanation: L = mv × r⊥. r⊥ (perpendicular distance from O to line of motion) is constant.

questions.chapter: System of Particles and Rotational Motion

questions.explanation: Man walks 6 m on boat. 140x = 60(6-x), x = 1.8 m (boat moves back). Net displacement = 6-1.8 = 4.2 m. Final distance = 20-4.2 = 15.8 m

questions.explanation: Angular momentum conservation: (2/5)mr²ω₀ = (2/5)m(r/η)²ω', ω' = η²ω₀

questions.chapter: System of Particles and Rotational Motion

questions.explanation: l = MR²/4. l' = MR²/2 + MR² = 3MR²/2 = 6l

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: System of Particles and Rotational Motion
questions.question: Two discs of same mass and thickness with densities 17 g/cm³ and 51 g/cm³. Ratio of their MI about central axes is
questions.explanation: I = πr⁴tρ/2. For same mass: m = πr²tρ → r²∝1/ρ. I ∝ r²ρ⁻¹ ∝ 1/ρ². Wait: I = mr²/2 ∝ r². r₁/r₂ = √(ρ₂/ρ₁) = √3. I₁/I₂ = 3

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: System of Particles and Rotational Motion
questions.question: From disc of radius R and mass 9M, small disc of radius R/3 removed off-centre. MI of remaing disc about axis through centre perpendicular to plane is
questions.explanation: I₁ = 9MR²/2. I₂ = M(R/3)²/2 + M(2R/3)² = MR²/2. I = I₁-I₂ = 4MR²

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: System of Particles and Rotational Motion
questions.question: Thin uniform wire of mass m and length l bent into circle. MI about axis through one end perpendicular to plane is
questions.explanation: r = l/2π. I_cm = mr². I = I_cm + mr² = 2mr² = 2m(l/2π)² = ml²/2π²

questions.explanation: Conservation of L: mk₁²ω₁ = mk₂²ω₂, k₁/k₂ = √(ω₂/ω₁)

Taking the thermal conductivity of ice as K, and its specific latent heat of fusion as L, the rate of increase of the thickness of ice layer, at this instant, would be given by
questions.explanation: Assume at instant thickness of ice is x. And time taken to form additional thickness (dx) is dt. mL=xKA[26−0]dt (Adx)pL=xKA(26)dt⇒dtdx=xρL26 K

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two bullets are fired horizontally and simultaneously towards each other from roof tops of two buildings 100 m apart and of same height of 200 m, with the same velocity of 25 m/s. When and where will the two bullets collide? (g=10 m/s2)

options.B: After 2 s at a height of 180 m
options.C: After 2 s at a height of 20 m
options.D: After 4 s at a height of 120 m

questions.explanation: Let bullets collide at time t x1+x²=100 m 25t+25t=100 t=2 s y=21gt2=21×10×22 =20m h=200−20=180 m Hence bullets will collide after 2 s at height 180 m above the ground.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The stress-strain curves are drawn for two different materials X and Y. It is observed that the ultimate strength point and the fracture point are close to each other for material X but are far apart for material Y. We can say that materials X and Y are likely to be (respectively),
questions.explanation: As given that fracture point and ultimate strength point is close for material X, hence X is brittle in nature and both points are far apart for material Y hence it is ductile. i.e X is brittle and Y is ductile in nature.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A body of mass m is kept on a rough horizontal surface (coefficient of friction =μ). A horizontal force is applied on the body, but it does not move. The resultant of normal reaction and the frictional force acting on the object is given by F, where F is,
questions.explanation: Since body does not move hence it is in equilibrium. fr= frictional force which is less than or equal to limiting friction. Now N=mg Hence F=N+fr ∣F∣≤(mg)2+(μmg)2 ∣F∣≤mg1+μ2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle of mass 5m at rest suddenly breaks on its own into three fragments. Two fragments of mass m each move along mutually perpendicular direction with speed v each. The energy released during the process is,
questions.explanation: From conservation of linear momentum. 0=mvj^+mvi^+3mv1 v1=−3v(i^+j^) v1=32v KEi=0 KEf=21mv2+21(3m)(32)2v2 =mv2+3mv2=34mv2 ΔKE=KEf−KEi=34mv2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An object of mass 500 g, initially at rest, is acted upon by a variable force whose X-component varies with X in the manner shown. The velocities of the object at the points x=8 m and X = 12 m, would have the respective values of (nearly) Shutterstock
questions.explanation: From work-energy theorem AK = work = area under F - x graph From x=0 to x=8m 21mv2=100+30 v²=520 v=520=23 m/s From x=0 to x=12m 21mv2=100+30−47.5+20 v=410 v=20.6 m/s Hence appropriate option is 23 m/s and 20.6 m/s.

questions.explanation: Since v=1+R2K22gh v²=1+212gh R2K2=21 for solid cylinder 2gh=42×23 h=1012=1.2m Now x=sin 30∘h=211.2=2.4m x=2.4m

questions.explanation: Sol. Vescape = 11200m / s Say at temperature T it attains V escape So1√(3kBT/mO2) = 11200m / s On solving, T = 8.360×104K

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The efficiency of an ideal heat engine working between the freezing point and boiling point of water, is
questions.explanation: Sol. Efficiency of ideal heat engine, η = (1 - T2/T1) T2 : Sink temperature T1 : Source temperature %η = (1 - T2/T1)×100 = (1 - 273/373)×100 = (100/373)×100 = 26.8%

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A set of 'n' equal resistors, of value 'R' each, are connected in series to a battery of emf 'E' and internal resistance 'R'. The current drawn is 1. Now, the 'n' resistors are connected in parallel to the same battery. Then the current drawn from battery becomes 10 1. The value of 'n' is
questions.explanation: Sol. 1 = E/(nR + R) 101 = E/(R/n + R) Dividing (ii) by (i), 10 = ((n + 1)R)/((1/n + 1)R) After solving the equation, n = 10

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Unpolarised light is incident from air on a plane surface of a material of refractive index 'μ'. At a particular angle of incidence 'i', it is found that the reflected and refracted rays are perpendicular to each other. Which of the following options is correct for this situation?

options.B: Reflected light is polarised with its electric vector perpendicular to the plane of incidence
options.C: Reflected light is polarised with its electric vector parallel to the plane of incidence

questions.explanation: Sol. When reflected light rays and refracted rays are perpendicular, reflected light is polarised with electric field vector perpendicular to the plane of incidence. Also, tan i = μ (Brewster angle)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In Young's double slit experiment the separation d between the slits is 2mm, the wavelength λ of the light used is 5896 Å and distance D between the screen and slits is 100cm. It is found that the angular width of the fringes is 0.20°. To increase the fringe angular width to 0.21° (with same λ and D) the separation between the slits needs to be changed to
questions.explanation: Sol. Angular width = λ/d 0.20° = λ/2 mm …(i) 0.21° = λ/d …(ii) Dividing we get, 0.20/0.21 = d/2 mm ∴ d = 1.9 mm

options.B: Large focal length and small diameter
options.C: Small focal length and large diameter
options.D: Small focal length and small diameter

questions.explanation: Sol. For telescope, angular magnification = f0/fE So, focal length of objective lens should be large. Angular resolution = D/1.22λ should be large. So, objective should have large focal length (f0) and large diameter D.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An electron of mass m with an initial velocity V→ = V0 iˆ (V0 > 0) enters an electric field E→ = –E0 iˆ (E0 = constant > 0) at t = 0. If λ0 is its de-Broglie wavelength initially, then its de-Broglie wavelength at time t is
questions.explanation: Sol. Initial de-Broglie wavelength λ0 = h/mV0 Acceleration of electron = eE0/m Velocity after time ‘t’ V = V0 + (eE0/m)t So, λ = h/mV = h/m(V0 + (eE0/m)t) = h/mV0(1 + (eE0/mV0)t) = λ0/(1 + (eE0/mV0)t)

options.A: Does not affect resistance of p-n junction
options.D: Affects the overall V - I characteristics of p-n junction

questions.explanation: Sol. Due to heating, number of electron-hole pairs will increase, so overall resistance of diode will change. Due to which forward biasing and reversed biasing both are changed.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An em wave is propagating in a medium with a velocity ν→ = νi→. The instaneous oscillating electric field of this em wave is along +y axis. Then the direction of oscillating magnetic field of the em wave will be along
questions.explanation: Sol. E→ × B→ = V→ (E→) × (B→) = Vi→ So, B→ = Bk→ Direction of propagation is along +z direction.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The refractive index of the material of a prism is √2 and the angle of the prism is 30°. One of the two refracting surfaces of the prism is made a mirror inwards, by silver coating. A beam of monochromatic light entering the prism from the other face will retrace its path (after reflection from the silvered surface) if its angle of incidence on the prism is
questions.explanation: Sol. For retracing its path, light ray should be normally incident on silvered face. Applying Snell's law at M, sin i/ sin 30° = √2/1 ⇒ sin i = √2 × 1/2 sin i = 1/√2 i.e. i = 45°

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An object is placed at a distance of 40cm from a concave mirror of focal length 15cm. If the object is displaced through a distance of 20cm towards the mirror, the displacement of the image will be
questions.explanation: Sol. 1/f = 1/v1 + 1/u1 1/-15 = 1/v1 + 1/(-40) ⇒ 1/v1 = -1/15 + 1/40 v1 = -24cm When object is displaced by 20cm towards mirror. Now, u2 = -20 1/f = 1/v² + 1/u2 1/-15 = 1/v² + 1/(-20) 1/v² = 1/20 - 1/15 v² = -60cm So, image shifts away from mirror by = 60 - 24 = 36cm

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The magnetic potential energy stored in a certain inductor is 25mJ, when the current in the inductor is 60mA. This inductor is of inductance

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An electron falls from rest through a vertical distance h in a uniform and vertically upward directed electric field E. The direction of electric field is now reversed, keeping its magnitude the same. A proton is allowed to fall from rest in it through the same vertical distance h. The time of fall of the electron, in comparison to the time of fall of the proton is
questions.explanation: Sol. h = 1/2 (eE/m) t² ∴ t = √(2hm/eE) ∴ t ∝ √m as 'e' is same for electron and proton. Electron has smaller mass so it will take smaller time.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The electrostatic force between the metal plates of an isolated parallel plate capacitor C having a charge Q and area A, is

options.A: Proportional to the square root of the distance between the plates
options.B: Linearly proportional to the distance between the plates
options.C: Independent of the distance between the plates
options.D: Inversely proportional to the distance between the plates

questions.explanation: Sol. For isolated capacitor Q = Constant Fplate = Q2/(2Aε0) F is Independent of the distance between plates.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A pendulum is hung from the roof of a sufficiently high building and is moving freely to and fro like a simple harmonic oscillator. The acceleration of the bob of the pendulum is 20m/s2 at a distance of 5m from the mean position. The time period of oscillation is
questions.explanation: Sol. |a| = ω2y ⇒ 20 = ω2(5) ⇒ ω = 2rad/s T = 2π/ω = 2π/2 = πs

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A metallic rod of mass per unit length 0.5 kg m–1 is lying horizontally on a smooth inclined plane which makes an angle of 30° with the horizontal. The rod is not allowed to slide down by flowing a current through it when a magnetic field of induction 0.25 T is acting on it in the vertical direction. The current flowing in the rod to keep it stationary is
questions.explanation: Sol. For equilibrium, mg sin30° = I/B cos30° I = (mg/l) (tan30°/B) I = (0.5 × 9.8)/(0.25 × √3) = 11.32 A

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A thin diamagnetic rod is placed vertically between the poles of an electromagnet. When the current in the electromagnet is switched on, then the diamagnetic rod is pushed up, out of the horizontal magnetic field. Hence the rod gains gravitational potential energy. The work required to do this comes from

options.A: The lattice structure of the material of the rod
options.D: The induced electric field due to the changing magnetic field

questions.explanation: Sol. Energy of current source will be converted into potential energy of the rod.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An inductor 20 mH, a capacitor 100 μF and a resistor 50 Ω are connected in series across a source of emf, V = 10 sin 314 t. The power loss in the circuit is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Current sensitivity of a moving coil galvanometer is 5 div/mA and its voltage sensitivity (angular deflection per unit voltage applied) is 20 div/V. The resistance of the galvanometer is
questions.explanation: Sol. Current sensitivity = NBA/C = IS Voltage sensitivity = NBA/CRG = VS So, resistance of galvanometer = IS/VS = (5 × 10⁻3)/(20) = 5000/20 = 250 Ω

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Three objects, A : (a solid sphere), B : (a thin circular disk) and C : (a circular ring), each have the same mass M and radius R. They all spin with the same angular speed ω about their own symmetry axes. The amounts of work (W) required to bring them to rest, would satisfy the relation
questions.explanation: Sol. Work done required to bring them rest ΔW = ΔKE ΔW = 1/2 Iω2 ΔW ∝ I for same ω WA : WB : WC = 2/5 MR² : 1/2 MR² : MR² = 2/5 : 1/2 : 1 = 4 : 5 : 10 ⇒ WC > WB > WA

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A moving block having mass m, collides with another stationary block having mass 4m. The lighter block comes to rest after collision. When the initial velocity of the lighter block is v, then the value of coefficient of restitution (e) will be
questions.explanation: Sol. According to law of conservation of linear momentum, mv + 4m × 0 = 4mv' + 0 v' = v/4 e = (Relative velocity of separation)/(Relative velocity of approach) = (v'/4)/(v) e = 1/4 = 0.25

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which one of the following statements is incorrect?

options.A: Frictional force opposes the relative motion.
options.B: Limiting value of static friction is directly proportional to normal reaction.
options.C: Rolling friction is smaller than sliding friction.
options.D: Coefficient of sliding friction has dimensions of length.

questions.explanation: Sol. Coefficient of sliding friction has no dimension. f = μsN ⇒ μs = f/N

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A toy car with charge q moves on a frictionless horizontal plane surface under the influence of a uniform electric field E→ Due to the force qE→ its velocity increases from 0 to 6m/s in one second duration. At that instant the direction of the field is reversed. The car continues to move for two more seconds under the influence of this field. The average velocity and the average speed of the toy car between 0 to 3 seconds are respectively
questions.explanation: Please refer to your NCERT textbook for a detailed explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A student measured the diameter of a small steel ball using a screw gauge of least count 0.001 cm. The main scale reading is 5 mm and zero of circular scale division coincides with 25 divisions above the reference level. If screw gauge has a zero error of -0.004cm, the correct diameter of the ball is
questions.explanation: Sol. Diameter of the ball = MSR + CSR × (Least count) - Zero error = 0.5cm + 25 × 0.001 - (-0.004) = 0.5 + 0.025 + 0.004 = 0.529cm

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A solid sphere is rotating freely about its symmetry axis in free space. The radius of the sphere is increased keeping its mass same. Which of the following physical quanties would remain constant for the sphere?
questions.explanation: Sol. τex = 0 So, dL/dt = 0 i.e. L = constant So angular momentum remains constant.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If the mass of the Sun were ten times smaller and the universal gravitational constant were ten times larger in magnitude, which of the following is not correct?

options.A: Time period of a simple pendulum on the Earth would decrease
options.B: Walking on the ground would become more difficult
options.D: 'g' on the Earth will not change

questions.explanation: Sol. If Universal Gravitational constant becomes ten times, then G' = 10 G So, acceleration due to gravity increases. i.e. (4) is wrong option.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A solid sphere is in rolling motion. In rolling motion a body posses translational kinetic energy (KL) as well as rotational kinetic energy (Kr) simultaneously. The ratio KL : (KL + Kr) for the sphere is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A small sphere of radius 'r' falls from rest in a viscous liquid. As a result, heat is produced due to viscous force. The rate of production of heat when the sphere attains its terminal velocity, is proportional to
questions.explanation: Sol. Power = 6πηrVT·VT = 6πηrVT2 VT ∝ r² ⇒ Power ∝ r5

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The power radiated by a black body is P and it radiates maximum energy at wavelength, λ0. If the temperature of the black body is now changed so that it radiates maximum energy at wavelength 3/4 λ0, the power radiated by it becomes nP. The value of n is
questions.explanation: Sol. We know, λmax T = constant (Wien's law) So, λmax1 T1 = λmax2 T2 ⇒ λ0 T = 3λ0/4 T' ⇒ T' = 4/3 T So, P2/P1 = (T'/T)4 = (4/3)4 = 256/81

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two wires are made of the same material and have the same volume. The first wire has cross-sectional area A and the second wire has cross-sectional area 3A. If the length of the first wire is increased by Δl on applying a force F, how much force is need to stretch the second wire by the same amount?
questions.explanation: Sol. For wire 1, Δl = (F/AY)3l...(i) For wire 2, Stress = F'/3A = Y × Strain = Y × (Δl/l) ⇒ Δl = (F'/3AY) l...(ii) From equation (i) & (ii), (F/AY)3l = (F'/3AY)l ⇒ F' = 9F

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A sample of 0.1g of water at 100°C and normal pressure (1.013×105Nm-2) requires 54 cal of heat energy to convert to steam at 100°C. If the volume of the steam produced is 167.1 cc, the change in internal energy of the sample, is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The correct order of N-compounds in its decreasing order of oxidation states is
questions.explanation: Sol. HNO₃, NO, N₂, NH4Cl Hence, the correct option is (3).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which one of the following elements is unable to form MF63- ion?
questions.explanation: Sol. : 'B' has no vacant d- orbitals in its valence shell, so it can't extend its covalency beyond 4. i.e. 'B' cannot form the ion like MF63(-) i.e. BF63(-). Hence, the correct option is (1).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A uniform rope of length L and mass m1 hangs vertically from a rigid support. A block of mass m2 is attached to the free end of the rope. A transverse pulse of wavelength λ1 is produced at the lower end of the rope. The wavelength of the pulse when it reaches the top of the rope is λ2. The ratio λ2/λ1 is :
questions.explanation: T1=m2g T2=(m1+m²)g Velocity ∝T λ∝T λ1λ2=T1T2λ1=T2T1 ⇒λ2λ1=m1+m2⇒λ1λ2=m2m1+m²

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Coefficient of linear expansion of brass and steel rods are α1 and α2. Lengths of brass and steel rods are ℓ1 and ℓ2 respectively. If (ℓ2−ℓ1) is maintained same at all temperatures, which one of the following relations holds good?
questions.explanation: Change in length for both rods should be same

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A gas is compressed isothermally to half its initial volume. The same gas is compressed separately through an adiabatic process until its volume is again reduced to half. Then :-

options.A: Compressing the gas isothermally will require more work to be done.
options.B: Compressing the gas through adiabatic process will require more work to be done.
options.C: Compressing the gas isothermally or adiabatically will require the same amount of work.
options.D: Which of the case (whether compression through isothermal or through adiabatic process) requires more work will depend upon the atomicity of the gas.

questions.explanation: Wext= negative of area with volume- axis W(adiabatic) >> W(isothermal)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A piece of ice falls from a height h so that it melts completely. Only one-quarter of the heat produced is absorbed by the ice and all energy of ice gets converted into heat during its fall. The value of h is : [Latent heat of ice is 3.4 × 10^5 J/kg and g = 10 N/kg]
questions.explanation: mgh4=mL4mgh=mL

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A small signal voltage V(t)=V0 sin ωt is applied across an ideal capacitor C :

options.A: Current I(t), lags voltage V(t) by 90∘
options.B: Over a full cycle the capacitor C does not consume any energy from the voltage source.
options.C: Current I(t) is in phase with voltage V(t)
options.D: Current I(t) leads voltage V(t) by 180∘

questions.explanation: Power =Vrms⋅Irmscos⁡ϕ=Vrms⋅Irmscosϕ

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A car is negotiating a curved road of radius R. The road is banked at an angle θθ. the coefficient of friction between the tyres of the car and the road is μs. The maximum safe velocity on this road is :
questions.explanation: v2Rg(μs+tan⁡θ1−μstan⁡θ)Rgv2(1−μstanθμs+tanθ)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
questions.topic: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
questions.question: Wavelength of a 1 keV photon is 1.24×10−9 m1.24×10−9 m. What is the frequency of 1 MeV photon?
questions.explanation: The energy of a photon is inversely proportional to its wavelength. By comparing the given ratio of energies (1 keV to 1 MeV) and using the relation f=c/λ, the frequency is calculated to be 2.4×1020Hz.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
questions.topic: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
questions.question: Ultraviolet radiations of 6.2eV fall on an aluminium surface (work function 4.2eV). The kinetic energy (in joule) of the fastest electron emitted is approximately
questions.explanation: The maximum kinetic energy is the incident energy minus the work function (6.2 eV - 4.2 eV = 2 eV). Converting 2 eV to joules by multiplying by 1.6×10−19 gives 3.2×10−19 J3.2×10−19 J.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
questions.topic: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
questions.question: The work function of a metallic surface is 5.01eV. The photo electrons are emitted when light of wavelength 2000A˚ falls on it. The potential difference applied to stop the fastest photo electrons is [h=4.14×10−15eVs]
questions.explanation: The stopping potential is the maximum kinetic energy of the photoelectrons in eV, which is calculated as hcλ−w0λhc−w0. Solving this with the given values yields 1.2 eV, so the stopping potential is 1.2 V.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
questions.topic: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
questions.question: A radio transmitter operates at a frequency of 880kHz and a power of 10kW. The number of photons emitted per second is
questions.explanation: The number of photons emitted per second is the total power divided by the energy per photon (hf). Calculating 10×103h×880×10^310×10^3 gives approximately 1.72×10^31.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
questions.topic: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
questions.question: Assuming photoemission to take place, the factor by which the maximum velocity of the emitted photo electrons changes when the wavelength of the incident radiation is increased four times, is (assuming work function to be negligible in comparison to hc/λ)
questions.explanation: The kinetic energy is hcλλhc. Since v²∝1λv2∝λ1, if the wavelength is increased four times, the velocity becomes half.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two waves having intensities in the ratio of 9 : 1 produce interference. The ratio of maximum to minimum intensity is equal to
questions.explanation: The ratio of maximum to minimum intensity is given by (I1+I₂)2(I1−I₂)2(I1+I₂)2. With intensity ratio 9:1, the amplitude ratio is 3:1, resulting in a maximum to minimum intensity ratio of 4:1.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If the light is polarised by reflection, then the angle between reflected and refracted light is
questions.explanation: According to Brewster's law, when light is incident at the polarizing angle, the reflected and refracted rays are perpendicular to each other, making an angle of 90° or π/2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The fringe width in Young's double-slit experiment can be increased if we decrease

options.B: Distance between the source and the screen

questions.explanation: Fringe width (ββ) is given by λDddλD. It is inversely proportional to the slit separation dd, so decreasing dd increases the fringe width.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In case of Young's experiment

options.A: There are two virtual sources of light from same monochromatic source of light
options.B: Both the slits get light from a single monochromatic source of light
options.C: Two separate monochromatic sources of light of same wavelength are used

questions.explanation: For interference fringes to be observed, the sources must be coherent. This is achieved in Young's experiment by both slits receiving light from the same single monochromatic source.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If light from a galaxy observed on the earth's surface has a red shift, then

options.A: Galaxy is stationary w.r.t. the earth
options.C: Galaxy is receding from the earth

questions.explanation: A red shift means the observed wavelength is longer (shifted towards the red end of the spectrum) than the emitted wavelength. This occurs when the source of light is moving away from the observer.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Digital movie projectors work on the principle of

options.D: Total internal reflection from optical fibres

questions.explanation: Digital movie projectors use Digital Light Processing (DLP) technology, which relies on an array of tiny mirrors (micromirrors) to reflect light and create the image.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A convex mirror is used to form an image of a real object. Then mark the wrong statement

options.A: The image lies between the pole and focus
options.B: The image is diminished in size

questions.explanation: A convex mirror always forms a virtual, erect, and diminished image for a real object. It cannot form a real image of a real object.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: When a light ray from a rarer medium is refracted into a denser medium, its
questions.explanation: By definition, light slows down in a denser medium. Since frequency remains constant, a decrease in velocity (v=fλ) also leads to a decrease in wavelength.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Numerically 1 gauss = x tesla, then x is
questions.explanation: 1 gauss = 10⁻4 T

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An electron having a charge e moves with a velocity v in positive x direction. A magnetic field acts on it in positive y direction. The force on the electron acts in (where outward direction is taken as positive z-axis).
questions.explanation: F = -e (v × B) So using right hand thumb rule, F will be in –z direction.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If a proton enters perpendicularly a magnetic field with velocity v, then time period of revolution is T. If proton enters with velocity 2v, then time period will be
questions.explanation: T = 2πm/qB, independent of v.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A proton, a deutron and an α-particle accelerated through the same potential difference enter a region of uniform magnetic field, moving at right angles to it. What is the ratio of their kinetic energy?
questions.explanation: K.E. = qV, V is same for all. K.E. ∝ q. So K.E.p : K.E.d : K.E.α = 1 : 1 : 2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle of mass m carrying charge q is accelerated by a potential difference V. It enters perpendicularly in a region of uniform magnetic field B and executes circular arc of radius R, then m/q equals
questions.explanation: r = mv/qB = √(2mqV)/qB r2 = (2mqV)/(q2B2) => m/q = B2r2/2V

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The net charge in a current carrying wire is zero still magnetic field exerts a force on it, because a magnetic field exerts force on
questions.explanation: F = q (v × B). Moving charges are only electrons and magnetic field exert force on moving charges only.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If a charged particle enters perpendicularly in the uniform magnetic field then

options.A: Energy remains constant but momentum changes
options.B: Energy and momentum both remains constant
options.C: Momentum remains constant but energy changes
options.D: Neither energy nor momentum remains constant

questions.explanation: Work done by magnetic force will always be zero as F ⟂ v, so using work energy theorem, W = 0, so K = constant. P changes as direction of velocity changes.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The motion of a charged particle can be used to distinguish between a magnetic field and electric field in a certain region by firing the charge
questions.explanation: To distinguish between E and B fields, one can observe the path when fired from opposite directions.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The correct expression for Lorentz force is
questions.explanation: Refer to NCERT for the complete explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A charged particle moves in a gravity free space without change in velocity. Which of the following is not possible in that space?
questions.explanation: If E ≠ 0 and B = 0, E will definitely deflect / increase / decrease velocity of charge. If both E and B are non zero, they may balance each other.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A wire is bent in the form of an equilateral triangle of side 100 cm and carries a current of 2 A. It is placed in a magnetic field of induction 2.0 T directed perpendicular into the plane of paper. The direction and magnitude of magnetic force acting on each side of the triangle will be

options.A: 2 N, normal to the side towards centre of the triangle
options.B: 2 N, normal to the side away from the centre of the triangle
options.C: 4 N, normal to the side towards centre of the triangle
options.D: 4 N, normal to the side away from the centre of the triangle

questions.explanation: F = i(l × B) F = 2(1)(2) = 4 N, normal to side towards centre of triangle.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The oscillator frequency of a cyclotron is 10 MHz. What should be the operating magnetic field for accelerating proton?

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A negative charge is coming towards the observer. The direction of the magnetic field produced by it will be (as seen by observer)

options.C: In the direction of motion of charge
options.D: In the direction opposite to the motion of charge

questions.explanation: B will be clockwise as seen by observer.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: When equal current is passed through two coils, equal magnetic field is produced at their centres. If the ratio of number of turns in the coils is 8 : 15, then the ratio of their radii will be

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The radius of a circular current carrying coil is R. At what distance from the centre of the coil on its axis, the intensity of magnetic field will be 1/2√2 times that at the centre?

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which one of the following graphs shows the variation of magnetic induction B with distance r from a long wire carrying a current?
questions.explanation: B = μ0i/2πr ⇒ B ∝ 1/r

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two straight long conductors AOB and COD are perpendicular to each other and carry currents i1 and i2. The magnitude of the magnetic induction at a point P at a distance d from the point O in a direction perpendicular to the plane ABCD is
questions.explanation: B1 = μ0i1/2πd, B2 = μ0i2/2πd, they are perpendicular. B = √(B12 + B22) = μ0/2πd √(i12 + i22)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A current of i ampere is flowing in an equilateral triangle of side a. The magnetic induction at the centroid will be

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A square frame of side l carries a current i. The magnetic field at its centre is B. The same current is passed through a circular coil having the same perimeter as the square. The field at the centre of the circular coil is B'. The ratio of B'/B is
questions.explanation: Please refer to your NCERT textbook for a detailed explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The magnetic induction at the point O, if the wire carries a current i, is
questions.explanation: B due to straight wires at O = (μ0i/4πR) * 2 * sin90? Actually for infinite wire, B = μ0i/2πR, but here they are semi-infinite, so B_straight = μ0i/4πR each, in opposite directions. The net from the two straight wires is μ0i/2πR in one direction. B_semicircle = μ0i/4R. The two are perpendicular. So B_net = √ = μ0i/4R √ = μ0i/4R √(4/π2 + 1) = μ0i/4R √((4+π2)/π2) = μ0i/4πR √(4+π2). This matches option (3).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The magnetic field intensity at the point O of a loop with current i, whose shape is illustrated below is
questions.explanation: Please refer to your NCERT textbook for a detailed explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If an electron revolves around a nucleus in a circular orbit of radius R with frequency n, then the magnetic field produced at the centre of the nucleus will be
questions.explanation: Current i = q/T = e * n B at centre of circular loop = μ0i/2R = μ0ne/2R

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A thin disc of radius R and mass M has charge q uniformly distributed on it. It rotates with angular velocity ω. The ratio of magnetic moment and angular momentum for the disc is
questions.explanation: Standard result: M/L = q/2m

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The number of turns per unit length of a long solenoid is 10. If its average radius is 5 cm and it carries a current of 10 A, then the ratio of flux densities obtained at the centre and at the end on the axis will be
questions.explanation: B_centre = μ0nI, B_end = (1/2)μ0nI B_centre : B_end = 2 : 1

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In a toroid the number of turns per unit length is 1000 and current through it is 1/4π ampere. The magnetic field produced inside (in weber/m2) will be

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A conducting circular loop of radius r carries a constant current i. It is placed in uniform magnetic field B, such that B is perpendicular to the plane of the loop. The net magnetic force acting on the loop is
questions.explanation: Net force on a closed loop in uniform magnetic field will always be zero.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A circular loop of radius R is kept in a uniform magnetic field pointing perpendicular into the plane of paper. When a current I flows in the loop, the tension produced in the loop is
questions.explanation: Consider a small element. The magnetic force on the element is dF = I dl B, acting radially outward. The tension T in the loop balances this force. 2T sin(dθ/2) = I (R dθ) B. For small angles, sin(dθ/2) ≈ dθ/2. So T dθ = I R B dθ ⇒ T = I B R.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If two straight current carrying wires are kept perpendicular to each other almost touching, then the wires
questions.explanation: Due to wire AB, wire CD will experience torque due to which it will become parallel to AB.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Connecting wires carrying currents in opposite directions are twisted together in using electrical appliances in order to reduce
questions.explanation: Twisting cancels the magnetic field produced by the currents.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A rectangular loop carrying a current i is situated near a long straight wire such that the wire is parallel to one of the sides of the loop and is in the plane of the loop. If a steady current i is established in the wire, the loop will

options.A: Rotate about an axis parallel to the wire

questions.explanation: Force on the two parallel sides will be attractive and repulsive respectively. The net force will be away from the wire.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A square loop of side l is kept in a uniform magnetic field B such that its plane makes an angle α with B. The loop carries a current i. The torque experienced by the loop in this position is
questions.explanation: Torque = M × B, M = iA = i l2. The angle between M and B is 90-α. So τ = i l2 B sin(90-α) = i l2 B cos α

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The effective radius of a circular coil is R and number of turns is N. The current through it is i ampere. The work done in rotating the coil by angle of 180° in an external magnetic field B will be (initially plane of coil is perpendicular to magnetic field)
questions.explanation: W = -MB(cos180 - cos0) = -MB(-1 - 1) = 2MB = 2 * (NiπR2) * B = 2πNiR2B

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The current sensitivity of a moving coil galvanometer increases by 20% when its resistance is doubled. Calculate, by what factor does the voltage sensitivity change?
questions.explanation: Please refer to your NCERT textbook for a detailed explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A charged particle enters a uniform magnetic field perpendicular to it. The magnetic field

options.A: Increases the speed of the particle
options.B: Decreases the kinetic energy of the particle
options.C: Changes the direction of motion of the particle

questions.explanation: Magnetic force F ⟂ V, so no work is done by magnetic field so speed and kinetic energy cannot be changed by magnetic field but it can deflect the particle.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A proton and an alpha particle enter the same magnetic field which is perpendicular to their velocity. If they have same kinetic energy then ratio of radii of their circular path is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In which of the following situations, the magnetic field can accelerate a charge particle at rest? I. When the magnetic field is uniform with respect to time as well as position II. When the magnetic field is time varying but uniform w.r.t. position III. When the magnetic field is time independent but position dependent
questions.explanation: When magnetic field is time varying, an induced electric field is produced which can accelerate charge.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A proton moving with a constant velocity, passes through a region of space without change in its velocity. If E & B represent the electric and magnetic fields respectively, this region may have
questions.explanation: For no change in velocity, net force = 0. This can happen if E=0 and the particle moves parallel to B (so no magnetic force). If E≠0, then velocity must be perpendicular to E and B such that qE = qvB. This is possible. Option (1) is possible (B field parallel to velocity). Option (2) would cause acceleration. Option (3) would cause force only from E. Option (4) would have both forces. So the most general answer is (1) as it is a subset of possible conditions.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle of charge per unit mass α is released from origin with a velocity v = v0 î in a uniform magnetic field B = -B0 k̂. If the particle passes through (0, y, 0) then y is equal to
questions.explanation: q/m = α. The particle will move in a circle. The point (0,y,0) is at a distance y from origin. The radius of the circle is r = v0/(B0α). The particle will reach (0,y,0) when it has covered half the circle, so y = 2r = 2v0/(B0α).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A proton is accelerating in a cyclotron where the applied magnetic field is 2 T. If the potential gap is effectively 100 kV then how much revolutions the proton has to make between the “dees” to acquire a kinetic energy of 20 MeV?
questions.explanation: Energy gained per revolution = 2 * 100 keV = 200 keV = 0.2 MeV Number of revolutions = 20 MeV / 0.2 MeV = 100

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A current i ampere flows in a circular arc of wire which subtends an angle 3π/2 radian at its center, whose radius is R. The magnetic field B at its center is
questions.explanation: B = (μ0i/4πR) * θ (in radians) θ = 3π/2 B = (μ0i/4πR) * (3π/2) = 3μ0i/8R

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: At what distance on the axis, from the centre of a circular current carrying coil of radius r, the magnetic field becomes 1/8th of the magnetic field at centre?

questions.explanation: Please refer to your NCERT textbook for a detailed explanation of this concept.

questions.subject: Physics
