questions.question: A resistance wire connected in the left gap of a metre bridge balances a 10 Ω resistance in the right gap at a point which divides the bridge wire in the ratio 3:2. If the length of the resistance wire is 1.5 m, then the length of 1 Ω of the resistance wire is
questions.explanation: At balance: R/10 = l₁/l₂ = 3/2 ⇒ R = 15 Ω. Length for 15 Ω = 1.5 m. Length for 1 Ω = 1.5/15 = 0.1 m = 1.0 × 10⁻¹ m.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A potentiometer wire of length L and a resistance r are connected in series with a battery of emf E₀ and a resistance r₁. An unknown emf is balanced at a length l of the potentiometer wire. The emf E will be given by
questions.explanation: Current I = E₀/(r + r₁). Potential gradient = I × r/L = E₀r/(r + r₁)L. Unknown emf E = potential gradient × l = (E₀r)/(r + r₁) × l/L.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The resistances in the two arms of the meter bridge are 5 Ω and R Ω respectively. When the resistance R is shunted with an equal resistance, the new balance point is at 1.6l₁. The resistance R is
questions.explanation: Initially: 5/R = l₁/(100 - l₁). After shunting R with R, new resistance = R/2. Then: 5/(R/2) = 1.6l₁/(100 - 1.6l₁) ⇒ 10/R = 1.6l₁/(100 - 1.6l₁). From first equation, l₁ = 500/(R + 5). Substituting and solving gives R = 15 Ω.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A potentiometer circuit has been set up for finding the internal resistance of a given cell. The main battery has emf 2.0 V and negligible internal resistance. The potentiometer wire is 4 m long. When the resistance connected across the given cell has values of (i) infinity, (ii) 9.5 Ω, the balancing lengths are 3 m and 2.85 m respectively. The value of internal resistance of the cell is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The resistances of the four arms P, Q, R and S in a Wheatstone bridge are 10 Ω, 30 Ω, 30 Ω and 90 Ω respectively. The emf and internal resistance of the cell are 7 V and 5 Ω respectively. If the galvanometer resistance is 50 Ω, the current drawn from the cell will be
questions.explanation: Bridge is balanced (P/Q = 10/30 = 1/3, R/S = 30/90 = 1/3). Equivalent resistance = (P+Q)||(R+S) = 40||120 = 30 Ω. Total resistance = 30 + 5 = 35 Ω. Current = 7/35 = 0.2 A.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A thick current carrying cable of radius R carries current I uniformly distributed across its cross-section. The variation of magnetic field B due to the cable with the distance r from the axis of the cable is represented by

options.A: B ∝ r for r < R, B ∝ 1/r for r > R
options.B: B ∝ 1/r for r < R, B ∝ r for r > R
options.C: B ∝ r² for r < R, B ∝ 1/r² for r > R
options.D: B constant for r < R, B ∝ 1/r for r > R

questions.explanation: Inside: B = (μ₀I/2πR²)r. Outside: B = μ₀I/2πr.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two toroids 1 and 2 have total number of turns 200 and 100 respectively with average radii 40 cm and 20 cm respectively. If they carry same current i, the ratio of the magnetic fields along the two loops is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A long wire carrying a steady current is bent into a circular loop of one turn. The magnetic field at the centre of the loop is B. It is then bent into a circular coil of n turns. The magnetic field at the centre of this coil of n turns will be
questions.explanation: For one turn: B = μ₀I/2R. For n turns: length constant ⇒ 2πR = n × 2πR' ⇒ R' = R/n. B' = μ₀nI/2R' = μ₀nI/2(R/n) = n²(μ₀I/2R) = n²B.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An electron moving in a circular orbit of radius r makes n rotations per second. The magnetic field produced at the centre has magnitude
questions.explanation: Current I = e × frequency = ne. Magnetic field at centre of circular loop = μ₀I/2r = μ₀ne/2r.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two identical long conducting wires AOB and COD are placed at right angle to each other, with O as common point. The wires carry I₁ and I₂ currents respectively. Point P is at distance d from O along direction perpendicular to the plane containing the wires. The magnetic field at P will be
questions.explanation: Fields due to perpendicular wires are perpendicular. B₁ = μ₀I₁/2πd, B₂ = μ₀I₂/2πd. Net B = √(B₁² + B₂²) = μ₀/2πd × √(I₁² + I₂²).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two similar coils of radius R are lying concentrically with their planes at right angles to each other. The currents flowing in them are I and 2I respectively. The resultant magnetic field induction at the centre will be
questions.explanation: B₁ = μ₀I/2R, B₂ = μ₀(2I)/2R = 2B₁. Fields perpendicular, so B = √(B₁² + B₂²) = √(B₁² + 4B₁²) = √5 B₁ = √5 μ₀I/2R.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two wires are held perpendicular to the plane of paper and are 5 cm apart. They carry currents of 2.5 A and 5 A in same direction. Then, the magnetic field strength B at a point midway between the wires will be
questions.explanation: At midpoint, fields are opposite. B₁ = μ₀(2.5)/2π(0.025) = μ₀/2π × 100, B₂ = μ₀(5)/2π(0.025) = μ₀/2π × 200. Net B = B₂ - B₁ = μ₀/2π × 100 = μ₀/2π T? Wait, careful: r = 2.5 cm = 0.025 m. B₁ = μ₀×2.5/(2π×0.025) = μ₀×100/2π, B₂ = μ₀×5/(2π×0.025) = μ₀×200/2π. Net = μ₀×100/2π = (μ₀/2π)×100. But options are in μ₀/2π, so factor 100 missing. Possibly they used cm inconsistently.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two circular coils 1 and 2 are made from the same wire but the radius of the 1st coil is twice that of the 2nd coil. What is the ratio of potential difference applied across them so that the magnetic field at their centres is the same?
questions.explanation: B = μ₀I/2r = μ₀(V/R)/2r. R ∝ length ∝ r, so R ∝ r. Thus B ∝ V/r². For same B, V₁/r₁² = V₂/r₂² ⇒ V₁/V₂ = (r₁/r₂)² = 4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A long solenoid carrying a current produces a magnetic field B along its axis. If the current is doubled and the number of turns per cm is halved, the new value of the magnetic field is
questions.explanation: B = μ₀nI. New n' = n/2, I' = 2I. So B' = μ₀(n/2)(2I) = μ₀nI = B.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The magnetic field of a given length of wire carrying a current for a single turn circular coil at centre is B, then its value for two turns for the same wire when same current passing through it is
questions.explanation: For one turn: B = μ₀I/2r, length L = 2πr. For two turns: each turn radius r' = L/(4π) = r/2. B' = μ₀(2I)/2r' = μ₀I/r' = μ₀I/(r/2) = 2μ₀I/r = 4(μ₀I/2r) = 4B.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Magnetic field due to 0.1 A current flowing through a circular coil of radius 0.1 m and 1000 turns at the centre of the coil is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If a long hollow copper pipe carries a current, then magnetic field is produced
questions.explanation: Inside hollow pipe, enclosed current = 0, so B = 0. Outside, B = μ₀I/2πr.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A coil of one turn is made of a wire of certain length and then from the same length a coil of two turns is made. If the same current is passed in both cases, the ratio of the magnetic induction at their centres will be
questions.explanation: For one turn: r₁ = L/2π, B₁ = μ₀I/2r₁ = μ₀Iπ/L. For two turns: each turn radius r₂ = L/4π, B₂ = μ₀(2I)/2r₂ = μ₀I/r₂ = μ₀I × 4π/L = 4μ₀Iπ/L. So B₁/B₂ = 1/4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The magnetic field dB due to a small element at a distance r and carrying current i is given by
questions.explanation: Biot-Savart law: dB = (μ₀/4π) i (dl × r̂)/r².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: At what distance from a long straight wire carrying a current of 12 A will the magnetic field be equal to 3 × 10⁻⁵ Wb/m²?

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A straight wire of diameter 0.5 mm carrying a current of 1 A is replaced by another wire of 1 mm diameter carrying same current. The strength of magnetic field far away is
questions.explanation: B = μ₀I/2πr depends on distance from wire, not on wire diameter.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The magnetic field at a distance r from a long wire carrying current i is 0.4 T. The magnetic field at a distance 2r is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The magnetic induction at a point P which is at a distance of 4 cm from a long current carrying wire is 10⁻³ T. The field at a distance 12 cm from the current will be

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Tesla is the unit of
questions.explanation: Tesla is SI unit of magnetic induction (B).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Ionised hydrogen atoms and α-particles with same momenta enter perpendicular to a constant magnetic field B. The ratio of their radii of their paths r_H : r_α will be
questions.explanation: r = p/qB. For same p, r ∝ 1/q. q_H = e, q_α = 2e. So r_H/r_α = q_α/q_H = 2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An electron is moving in a circular path under the influence of a transverse magnetic field of 3.57 × 10⁻² T. If the value of e/m is 1.76 × 10¹¹ C/kg, the frequency of revolution of the electron is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An alternating electric field of frequency ν is applied across the dees (radius = R) of a cyclotron that is being used to accelerate protons (mass = m). The operating magnetic field B used in the cyclotron is given by
questions.explanation: Cyclotron frequency ν = qB/2πm ⇒ B = 2πmν/e.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A uniform electric field and a uniform magnetic field are acting along the same direction. If an electron is projected in the region such that its velocity is pointed along the direction of fields, then the electron
questions.explanation: Magnetic force = 0 (v ∥ B). Electric force opposes motion (electron charge negative), so speed decreases.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A beam of cathode rays is subjected to crossed electric (E) and magnetic fields (B). The fields are adjusted such that the beam is not deflected. The specific charge of the cathode rays is given by
questions.explanation: For no deflection: v = E/B. Also ½mv² = eV ⇒ v² = 2eV/m. Equating: E²/B² = 2eV/m ⇒ e/m = E²/2VB².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle of mass m, charge q and kinetic energy T enters a transverse uniform magnetic field of induction B. After 3 s, the kinetic energy of the particle will be
questions.explanation: Magnetic field does no work, so kinetic energy remains constant.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A beam of electrons passes undeflected through mutually perpendicular electric and magnetic fields. If the electric field is switched OFF and the same magnetic field is maintained, the electrons move
questions.explanation: With only magnetic field perpendicular to velocity, electrons move in circular path.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Under the influence of a uniform magnetic field a charged particle is moving in a circle of radius R with constant speed v. The time period of the motion

options.A: depends on v and not on R
options.B: depends on both R and v
options.C: is independent of both R and v
options.D: depends on R and not on v

questions.explanation: T = 2πm/qB, independent of R and v.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: When a charged particle moving with velocity v is subjected to a magnetic field of induction B, the force on it is non-zero. This implies that

options.A: angle between v and B is necessarily 90°
options.B: angle between v and B can have any value other than 90°
options.C: angle between v and B can have any value other than 0° and 180°
options.D: angle between v and B is either 0° or 180°

questions.explanation: F = qvB sin θ. For non-zero force, sin θ ≠ 0 ⇒ θ ≠ 0°, 180°. Best Choice Questions - Calculation-then-Eliminate Format 100% Unique Questions - No Repeats from Previous Sets

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A metallic rod of mass per unit length 0.5 kg/m is lying horizontally on a smooth inclined plane which makes an angle of 30° with the horizontal. The rod is not allowed to slide down by flowing a current through it when a magnetic field of induction 0.25 T is acting on it in the vertical direction. The current flowing in the rod to keep it stationary is
questions.explanation: For equilibrium, mg sin θ = BIL cos θ. I = (m/L)g tan θ/B = 0.5 × 9.8 × tan 30°/0.25 = 0.5 × 9.8 × 0.577/0.25 = 11.32 A.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An arrangement of three parallel straight wires placed perpendicular to plane of paper carrying same current I along the same direction is shown in figure. Magnitude of force per unit length on the middle wire B is given by
questions.explanation: Force on B due to A and C are equal and perpendicular. F₁ = F₂ = μ₀I²/2πd. Resultant = √(F₁² + F₂²) = √2 × μ₀I²/2πd = μ₀I²/√2πd.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A square loop ABCD carrying a current i is placed near and coplanar with a long straight conductor XY carrying a current I. The net force on the loop will be
questions.explanation: Force on parallel arms: F_AB = μ₀IiL/2π(L/2) = μ₀Ii/π, F_CD = μ₀IiL/2π(3L/2) = μ₀Ii/3π. Net = F_AB - F_CD = μ₀Ii(1 - 1/3)/π = 2μ₀Ii/3π.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A rectangular coil of length 0.12 m and width 0.1 m having 50 turns of wire is suspended vertically in a uniform magnetic field of strength 0.2 Wb/m². The coil carries a current of 2 A. If the plane of the coil is inclined at an angle of 30° with the direction of the field, the torque required to keep the coil in stable equilibrium will be

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A current loop in a magnetic field

options.A: experiences torque whether field is uniform or non-uniform in all orientations
options.B: can be in equilibrium in one orientation
options.C: can be in equilibrium in two orientations, both unstable
options.D: can be in equilibrium in two orientations, one stable and one unstable

questions.explanation: For parallel alignment (θ = 0°) - stable equilibrium, for antiparallel (θ = 180°) - unstable equilibrium.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A current carrying closed loop in the form of a right angled isosceles ΔABC is placed in a uniform magnetic field acting along AB. If the magnetic force on the arm BC is F, the force on the arm AC is
questions.explanation: Net force on closed loop in uniform magnetic field is zero. So F_AB + F_BC + F_CA = 0. F_AB = 0 (parallel to field). Thus F_CA = -F_BC = -F.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A square current carrying loop is suspended in a uniform magnetic field acting in the plane of the loop. If the force on one arm of the loop is F, the net force on the remaining three arms of the loop is
questions.explanation: Net force on closed loop = 0. So sum of forces on all four arms = 0. Force on remaining three arms = -F.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A coil in the shape of an equilateral triangle of side l is suspended between the pole pieces of a permanent magnet such that B is in plane of the coil. If due to a current i in the triangle a torque τ acts on it, the side l of the triangle is
questions.explanation: Area A = √3 l²/4, τ = BiA sin 90° = Bi(√3 l²/4). So l² = 4τ/√3 Bi ⇒ l = 2√(τ/√3 Bi).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Current is flowing in a coil of area A and number of turns N, then magnetic moment of the coil M is equal to
questions.explanation: Magnetic moment M = NIA.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two long parallel wires are at a distance of 1 m. Both of them carry 1 A of current. The force of attraction per unit length between the two wires is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A straight wire of length 0.5 m and carrying a current of 1.2 A is placed in uniform magnetic field of induction 2 T. The magnetic field is perpendicular to the length of the wire. The force on the wire is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A current carrying coil is subjected to a uniform magnetic field. The coil will orient so that its plane becomes
questions.explanation: Coil orients to make its magnetic moment parallel to field, which means plane of coil becomes perpendicular to field? Wait, magnetic moment is perpendicular to plane of coil. For moment parallel to field, plane is perpendicular to field. Options say "plane becomes parallel" which is incorrect. But given answer is (c) parallel.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Current sensitivity of a moving coil galvanometer is 5 div/mA and its voltage sensitivity is 20 div/V. The resistance of the galvanometer is
questions.explanation: Current sensitivity I_s = θ/I, Voltage sensitivity V_s = θ/V = θ/IR = I_s/R. So R = I_s/V_s = 5 × 10⁻³ A⁻¹/20 V⁻¹ = 250 Ω.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In an ammeter, 0.2% of main current passes through the galvanometer. If resistance of galvanometer is G, the resistance of ammeter will be
questions.explanation: I_g = 0.2% of I = I/500. Shunt resistance S = I_g G/(I - I_g) = (I/500)G/(499I/500) = G/499. Ammeter resistance = G∥S = (G × G/499)/(G + G/499) = (G²/499)/(500G/499) = G/500.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A millivoltmeter of 25 mV range is to be converted into an ammeter of 25 A range. The value (in ohm) of necessary shunt will be
questions.explanation: I_g = 25 mV/G. For ammeter, S = I_g G/(I - I_g) ≈ (25 mV/G) × G/25 = 25 mV/25 = 1 mV = 0.001 Ω.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The resistance of an ammeter is 13 Ω and its scale is graduated for a current upto 100 A. After an additional shunt has been connected to this ammeter, it becomes possible to measure currents upto 750 A. The value of shunt resistance is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A uniform conducting wire of length 12a and resistance R is wound up as a current carrying coil in the shape of an equilateral triangle of side a. The magnetic dipole moment of the coil is
questions.explanation: Number of turns n = 12a/(3a) = 4. Area of triangle = (√3/4)a². Magnetic moment M = nIA = 4 × I × (√3/4)a² = √3 Ia².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A wire of length L metre carrying a current of I ampere is bent in the form of a circle. Its magnetic moment is
questions.explanation: L = 2πR ⇒ R = L/2π. Area = πR² = π(L/2π)² = L²/4π. Magnetic moment = IA = I × L²/4π.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A 250-turn rectangular coil of length 2.1 cm and width 1.25 cm carries a current of 85 μA and subjected to a magnetic field of strength 0.85 T. Work done for rotating the coil by 180° against the torque is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A bar magnet is hung by a thin cotton thread in a uniform horizontal magnetic field and is in equilibrium state. The energy required to rotate it by 60° is W. Now the torque required to keep the magnet in this new position is
questions.explanation: W = MB(cos 0° - cos 60°) = MB(1 - 1/2) = MB/2 ⇒ MB = 2W. Torque τ = MB sin 60° = 2W × √3/2 = √3 W.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A bar magnet of length l and magnetic dipole moment M is bent in the form of an arc as shown in figure. The new magnetic dipole moment will be
questions.explanation: Original pole strength m = M/l. After bending into arc of radius r, l = (π/3)r ⇒ r = 3l/π. New magnetic moment M' = m × r = (M/l) × (3l/π) = 3M/π.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A compass needle which is allowed to move in a horizontal plane is taken to a geomagnetic pole. It

options.A: will become rigid showing no movement
options.C: will stay in North-South direction only
options.D: will stay in East-West direction only

questions.explanation: At geomagnetic poles, horizontal component of Earth's field is zero, so needle aligns with vertical field? Actually at poles, needle will dip vertically, not stay horizontal.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A bar magnet having a magnetic moment of 2 × 10⁴ J/T is free to rotate in a horizontal plane. A horizontal magnetic field B = 6 × 10⁻⁴ T exists in the space. The work done in taking the magnet slowly from a direction parallel to the field to a direction 60° from the field is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A charged particle (charge q) is moving in a circle of radius R with uniform speed v. The associated magnetic moment μ is given by
questions.explanation: Current I = q/T = qv/2πR. Magnetic moment = IA = (qv/2πR) × πR² = qvR/2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A bar magnet of magnetic moment M is placed in a magnetic field of induction B. The torque exerted on it is
questions.explanation: Torque τ = M × B.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The relations amongst the three elements earth's magnetic field, namely horizontal component H, vertical component V and dip δ are (B_E = total magnetic field)

options.A: V = B_E tan δ, H = B_E
options.B: V = B_E sin δ, H = B_E cos δ
options.C: V = B_E cos δ, H = B_E sin δ
options.D: V = B_E, H = B_E tan δ

questions.explanation: From diagram, H = B_E cos δ, V = B_E sin δ.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: At a point A on the earth's surface the angle of dip δ = +25°. At a point B on the earth's surface the angle of dip δ = -25°. We can interpret that

options.A: A is in southern hemisphere, B in northern
options.B: A is in northern hemisphere, B in southern

questions.explanation: Dip angle is positive in northern hemisphere, negative in southern hemisphere.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If θ₁ and θ₂ be the apparent angles of dip observed in two vertical planes at right angles to each other, then the true angle of dip θ is given by

options.A: cot² θ = cot² θ₁ + cot² θ₂
options.B: tan² θ = tan² θ₁ + tan² θ₂
options.C: cot² θ = cot² θ₁ - cot² θ₂
options.D: tan² θ = tan² θ₁ - tan² θ₂

questions.explanation: From relation between apparent and true dip: cot² θ = cot² θ₁ + cot² θ₂.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A vibration magnetometer placed in magnetic meridian has a small bar magnet. The magnet executes oscillations with a time period of 2 s in the earth's horizontal magnetic field of 24 μT. When a horizontal field of 18 μT is produced opposite to the earth's field by placing a current carrying wire, the new time period of magnet will be

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two bar magnets having same geometry with magnetic moments M and 2M are firstly placed with similar poles on same side, period is T₁. When polarity of one magnet is reversed, period becomes T₂. Then
questions.explanation: T ∝ 1/√M. First case: M₁ = M + 2M = 3M. Second case: M₂ = 2M - M = M. So T₁/T₂ = √(M/3M) = 1/√3 ⇒ T₁ < T₂.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Due to the earth's magnetic field, charged cosmic ray particles

options.C: require less KE to reach equator than poles
options.D: require greater KE to reach equator than poles

questions.explanation: At equator, particles experience maximum magnetic deflection, so need higher energy to reach.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A bar magnet is oscillating in the earth's magnetic field with a period T. If its mass is quadrupled, the new period will be
questions.explanation: T ∝ √(I/M). I ∝ mass. So mass quadrupled ⇒ I quadrupled ⇒ T' = √4 × T = 2T.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An iron rod of susceptibility 599 is subjected to a magnetising field of 1200 A/m. The permeability of the material of the rod is (μ₀ = 4π × 10⁻⁷ Tm/A)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The magnetic susceptibility is negative for
questions.explanation: Diamagnetic materials have negative susceptibility.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If a diamagnetic substance is brought near the North or the South pole of a bar magnet, it is

options.B: repelled by North and attracted by South
options.C: attracted by North and repelled by South

questions.explanation: Diamagnetic materials are repelled by both poles.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Nickel shows ferromagnetic property at room temperature. If the temperature is increased beyond Curie temperature, then it will show
questions.explanation: Above Curie temperature, ferromagnetic materials become paramagnetic.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Above Curie temperature
questions.explanation: Curie temperature is the transition point from ferromagnetic to paramagnetic behavior.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If the magnetic dipole moment of an atom of diamagnetic material, paramagnetic material and ferromagnetic material are denoted by μ_d, μ_p and μ_f respectively, then
questions.explanation: Diamagnetic atoms have no permanent dipole moment. Paramagnetic atoms have permanent dipole moment.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: According to Curie's law, the magnetic susceptibility of a paramagnetic substance at an absolute temperature T is proportional to
questions.explanation: Curie's law: χ ∝ 1/T.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A diamagnetic material in a magnetic field moves

options.B: from weaker to stronger parts of the field
options.C: from stronger to weaker parts of the field

questions.explanation: Diamagnetic materials are repelled from stronger field regions.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In which type of material the magnetic susceptibility does not depend on temperature?
questions.explanation: Diamagnetic susceptibility is independent of temperature.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A diamagnetic substance is brought near a strong magnet, then it is

options.C: repelled by North and attracted by South
options.D: attracted by North and repelled by South

questions.explanation: Diamagnetic substances are repelled by magnets.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: For protecting a sensitive equipment from the external magnetic field, it should be
questions.explanation: Iron being ferromagnetic provides magnetic shielding.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The magnetic potential energy stored in a certain inductor is 25 mJ, when the current in the inductor is 60 mA. This inductor is of inductance

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A long solenoid has 1000 turns. When a current of 4 A flows through it, the magnetic flux linked with each turn of the solenoid is 4 × 10⁻³ Wb. The self-inductance of the solenoid is
questions.explanation: Total flux = Nφ = 1000 × 4 × 10⁻³ = 4 Wb. L = total flux/I = 4/4 = 1 H.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A long solenoid has 500 turns. When a current of 2 A is passed through it, the resulting magnetic flux linked with each turn of the solenoid is 4 × 10⁻³ Wb. The self-inductance of the solenoid is
questions.explanation: Total flux = Nφ = 500 × 4 × 10⁻³ = 2 Wb. L = total flux/I = 2/2 = 1 H.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two coils of self-inductances 2 mH and 8 mH are placed so close together that the effective flux in one coil is completely linked with the other. The mutual inductance between these coils is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In an inductor of self-inductance L = 2 mH, current changes with time according to relation i = t²e⁻ᵗ. At what time emf is zero?
questions.explanation: e = -L di/dt. di/dt = 2te⁻ᵗ - t²e⁻ᵗ = te⁻ᵗ(2 - t). For e = 0, di/dt = 0 ⇒ t(2 - t) = 0 ⇒ t = 0 or t = 2 s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two coils have a mutual inductance of 0.005 H. The current changes in the first coil according to equation i = i₀ sin ωt, i₀ = 10 A and ω = 100π rad/s. The maximum value of emf in the second coil is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A varying current in a coil changes from 10 A to zero in 0.5 s. If the average emf induced in the coil is 220 V, the self-inductance of the coil is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If N is the number of turns in a coil, the value of self-inductance varies as
questions.explanation: L ∝ N².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: What is the self-inductance of a coil which produces 5 V when the current changes from 3 A to 2 A in one millisecond?

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A 100 mH coil carries a current of 1 A. Energy stored in its magnetic field is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If the number of turns per unit length of a coil of solenoid is doubled, the self-inductance of the solenoid will
questions.explanation: L ∝ n², so doubling n makes L four times.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The current in self-inductance L = 40 mH is to be increased uniformly from 1 A to 11 A in 4 millisecond. The emf induced in inductor during the process is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An inductor may store energy in
questions.explanation: Inductor stores energy in magnetic field.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In the circuit of figure, the bulb will become suddenly bright, if
questions.explanation: When contact is broken, induced emf causes current to flow momentarily, making bulb bright.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Energy in a current carrying coil is stored in the form of
questions.explanation: Energy stored in inductor is in magnetic field.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An inductor of inductance L, a capacitor of capacitance C and a resistor of resistance R are connected in series to an AC source of potential difference V volts as shown in figure. Potential difference across L, C and R is 40 V, 10 V and 40 V respectively. The amplitude of current flowing through LCR series circuit is 10√2 A. The impedance of the circuit is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A series LCR circuit containing 5.0 H inductor, 80 μF capacitor and 40 Ω resistor is connected to 230 V variable frequency AC source. The angular frequencies of the source at which power transferred to the circuit is half the power at the resonant angular frequency are likely to be
questions.explanation: ω₀ = 1/√(LC) = 1/√(5 × 80 × 10⁻⁶) = 1/√(4 × 10⁻⁴) = 1/0.02 = 50 rad/s. Δω = R/2L = 40/(2 × 5) = 4 rad/s. Half-power frequencies = ω₀ ± Δω = 46 rad/s and 54 rad/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A series LCR circuit is connected to an AC voltage source. When L is removed from the circuit, the phase difference between current and voltage is π/3. If instead C is removed from the circuit, the phase difference is again π/3 between current and voltage. The power factor of the circuit is
questions.explanation: When L removed, tan π/3 = X_C/R ⇒ X_C = √3 R. When C removed, tan π/3 = X_L/R ⇒ X_L = √3 R. So X_L = X_C, circuit is resonant, power factor = 1.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A 40 μF capacitor is connected to a 200 V, 50 Hz AC supply. The rms value of the current in the circuit is nearly

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In an electromagnetic wave in free space the root mean square value of the electric field is E_rms = 6 V/m. The peak value of the magnetic field is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A small signal voltage V(t) = V₀ sin ωt is applied across an ideal capacitor C

options.A: over a full cycle the capacitor C does not consume any energy from the voltage source
options.B: current I(t) is in phase with voltage V(t)
options.C: current I(t) leads voltage V(t) by 180°
options.D: current I(t) lags voltage V(t) by 90°

questions.explanation: In pure capacitor, average power over cycle is zero.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An inductor 20 mH, a capacitor 50 μF and a resistor 40 Ω are connected in series across a source of emf V = 10 sin 340t. The power loss in AC circuit is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The potential differences across the resistance, capacitance and inductance are 80 V, 40 V and 100 V respectively in an LCR circuit. The power factor of this circuit is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A 100 Ω resistance and a capacitor of 100 Ω reactance are connected in series across a 220 V source. When the capacitor is 50% charged, the peak value of the displacement current is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A resistance R draws power P when connected to an AC source. If an inductance is now placed in series with the resistance, such that the impedance of the circuit becomes Z, the power drawn will be
questions.explanation: Initially P = V²/R. After adding L, P' = V²R/Z² = (V²/R) × (R²/Z²) = P × (R/Z)².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A coil of self-inductance L is connected in series with a bulb B and an AC source. Brightness of the bulb decreases when

options.A: frequency of the AC source is decreased
options.B: number of turns in the coil is reduced
options.C: a capacitance of reactance X_C = X_L is included in the same circuit
options.D: an iron rod is inserted in the coil

questions.explanation: Inserting iron rod increases L, increases inductive reactance, decreases current, bulb dims.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In an electrical circuit, R, L, C and an AC voltage source are all connected in series. When L is removed from the circuit, the phase difference between the voltage and the current in the circuit is π/3. If instead C is removed from the circuit, the phase difference is again π/3. The power factor of the circuit is
questions.explanation: When L removed, tan π/3 = X_C/R ⇒ X_C = √3 R. When C removed, tan π/3 = X_L/R ⇒ X_L = √3 R. So X_L = X_C, circuit resonant, power factor = 1.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In an AC circuit an alternating voltage e = 200√2 sin 100t volt is connected to a capacitor of capacity 1 μF. The rms value of the current in the circuit is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In the given circuit, the reading of voltmeter V₁ and V₂ are 300 V each. The reading of the voltmeter V₃ and ammeter A are respectively
questions.explanation: Since V_L = V_C, circuit is resonant. V = V_R = 220 V. I = V/R = 220/100 = 2.2 A.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle moves from a point (−2i^+5j^)(−2i^+5j^​) to (4j^+3k^)(4j^​+3k^) when a force of (4i^+3j^)(4i^+3j^​) N is applied. How much work has been done by the force?
questions.explanation: Displacement = r₂ - r₁ = (4ĵ + 3k̂) - (-2î + 5ĵ) = 2î - ĵ + 3k̂. Work = F·s = (4î + 3ĵ)·(2î - ĵ + 3k̂) = 8 - 3 + 0 = 5 J.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two similar springs P and Q have spring constants K_P and K_Q, such that K_P > K_Q. They are stretched, first by the same amount (case a), then by the same force (case b). The work done by the springs W_P and W_Q are related as, in case (a) and case (b), respectively
questions.explanation: Case (a): Same elongation x, W = ½Kx², so W ∝ K, thus W_P > W_Q. Case (b): Same force F, elongation x = F/K, W = ½K(F/K)² = F²/2K, so W ∝ 1/K, thus W_P < W_Q.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A block of mass 10 kg, moving in x-direction with a constant speed of 10 m/s, is subjected to a retarding force F = 0.1x J/m during its travel from x = 20 m to 30 m. Its final KE will be

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two particles of masses m₁, m₂ move with initial velocities u₁ and u₂. On collision, one of the particles get excited to higher level, after absorbing energy ε. If final velocities of particles be v₁ and v₂, then we must have
questions.explanation: Total initial energy = KE₁ + KE₂. After collision, one particle absorbs energy ε (internal energy), so final kinetic energy = initial KE - ε.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A mass m moves in a circle on a smooth horizontal plane with velocity v₀ at a radius R₀. The mass is attached to a string which passes through a smooth hole in the plane. The tension in the string is increased gradually and finally m moves in a circle of radius R₀/2. The final value of the kinetic energy is
questions.explanation: Angular momentum conserved: mv₀R₀ = mv_f(R₀/2) ⇒ v_f = 2v₀. Final KE = ½m(2v₀)² = 2mv₀².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A ball is thrown vertically downwards from a height of 20 m with an initial velocity v₀. It collides with the ground, loses 50% of its energy in collision and rebounds to the same height. The initial velocity v₀ is (Take, g = 10 m/s²)
questions.explanation: Rebound speed v = √(2gh) = √(2×10×20) = 20 m/s. Energy after rebound = 200m J. This is 50% of energy before collision, so energy before = 400m J. ½mv₀² + mgh = 400m ⇒ ½v₀² + 200 = 400 ⇒ v₀² = 400 ⇒ v₀ = 20 m/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A uniform force of (3î + ĵ) N acts on a particle of mass 2 kg. Hence, the particle is displaced from position (2î + k̂) m to position (4î + 3ĵ - k̂) m. The work done by the force on the particle is
questions.explanation: Displacement s = (4î + 3ĵ - k̂) - (2î + k̂) = 2î + 3ĵ - 2k̂. Work = F·s = (3î + ĵ)·(2î + 3ĵ - 2k̂) = 6 + 3 + 0 = 9 J.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The potential energy of a particle in a force field is U = A/r² - B/r where A and B are positive constants and r is the distance of particle from the centre of the field. For stable equilibrium, the distance of the particle is
questions.explanation: For equilibrium, F = -dU/dr = 0 ⇒ -2A/r³ + B/r² = 0 ⇒ 2A/r = B ⇒ r = 2A/B. d²U/dr² > 0 confirms stable equilibrium.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The potential energy of a system increases, if work is done

options.A: by the system against a conservative force
options.B: by the system against a non-conservative force
options.C: upon the system by a conservative force
options.D: upon the system by a non-conservative force

questions.explanation: When work is done by the system against a conservative force, the potential energy of the system increases by the amount of work done.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Force F on a particle moving in a straight line varies with distance d as shown in the figure. The work done on the particle during its displacement of 12 m is
questions.explanation: Work done = area under F-d graph = 2 × (7-3) + ½ × 2 × (12-7) = 2 × 4 + ½ × 2 × 5 = 8 + 5 = 13 J.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A block of mass M is attached to the lower end of a vertical spring. The spring is hung from a ceiling and has force constant value k. The mass is released from rest with the spring initially unstretched. The maximum extension produced in the length of the spring will be
questions.explanation: By conservation of energy: Mgx - ½kx² = 0 ⇒ 2Mgx = kx² ⇒ x = 2Mg/k.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A body of mass 1 kg is thrown upwards with a velocity 20 m/s. It momentarily comes to rest after attaining a height of 18 m. How much energy is lost due to air friction? (Take g = 10 m/s²)
questions.explanation: Initial KE = ½ × 1 × 400 = 200 J. PE at height = 1 × 18 × 10 = 180 J. Loss = KE - PE = 200 - 180 = 20 J.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: 300 J of work is done in sliding a 2 kg block up an inclined plane of height 10 m. Taking g = 10 m/s², work done against friction is
questions.explanation: Work against gravity = mgh = 2 × 10 × 10 = 200 J. Net work = Work against gravity + Work against friction ⇒ 300 = 200 + W_f ⇒ W_f = 100 J.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A body of mass 3 kg is under a constant force, which causes a displacement s in metre in it, given by the relation s = ⅓t², where t is in second. Work done by the force in 2 s is
questions.explanation: a = d²s/dt² = ⅔ m/s². F = ma = 3 × ⅔ = 2 N. At t = 2 s, s = ⅓ × 4 = 4/3 m. Work = F × s = 2 × 4/3 = 8/3 J.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A force F acting on an object varies with distance x as shown in the figure. The force is in newton and x is in metre. The work done by the force in moving the object from x = 0 to x = 6 m is
questions.explanation: Work = area under curve = area of square (3×3) + area of triangle (½×3×3) = 9 + 4.5 = 13.5 J.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A bomb of mass 30 kg at rest explodes into two pieces of masses 18 kg and 12 kg. The velocity of 18 kg mass is 6 m/s. The kinetic energy of the other mass is
questions.explanation: By momentum conservation: 18 × 6 = 12 × v₂ ⇒ v₂ = 9 m/s. KE₂ = ½ × 12 × 81 = 6 × 81 = 486 J.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle of mass m₁ is moving with a velocity v₁ and another particle of mass m₂ is moving with a velocity v₂. Both of them have the same momentum, but their different kinetic energies are E₁ and E₂ respectively. If m₁ > m₂, then
questions.explanation: p = √(2mE) ⇒ m₁E₁ = m₂E₂ (since p same). If m₁ > m₂, then E₁ < E₂.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A ball of mass 2 kg and another of mass 4 kg are dropped together from a 60 ft tall building. After a fall of 30 ft each towards earth, their respective kinetic energies will be in the ratio of
questions.explanation: Velocity depends only on height fallen, so v same for both. KE ∝ m. Ratio = 2 : 4 = 1 : 2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If kinetic energy of a body is increased by 300%, then percentage change in momentum will be
questions.explanation: KE' = KE + 300% KE = 4KE. p ∝ √(KE), so p' = √(4KE) = 2p. Percentage increase = (2p - p)/p × 100 = 100%.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A stone is thrown at an angle of 45° to the horizontal with kinetic energy K. The kinetic energy at the highest point is
questions.explanation: At highest point, velocity = u cos45° = u/√2. KE_h = ½m(u/√2)² = ¼mu² = K/2 (since K = ½mu²).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A child is swinging a swing. Minimum and maximum heights of swing from the earth's surface are 0.75 m and 2 m respectively. The maximum velocity of this swing is
questions.explanation: By energy conservation: ½mv_max² = mg(H₂ - H₁) ⇒ v_max = √(2g × 1.25) = √25 = 5 m/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two bodies with kinetic energies in the ratio 4 : 1 are moving with equal linear momentum. The ratio of their masses is
questions.explanation: KE = p²/2m ⇒ m = p²/2KE. For same p, m ∝ 1/KE. So m₁ : m₂ = KE₂ : KE₁ = 1 : 4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A force acts on a 3.0 g particle in such a way that the position of the particle as a function of time is given by x = 3t - 4t² + t³, where x is in metre and t in second. The work done during the first 4 s is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A rubber ball is dropped from a height of 5 m on a planet where the acceleration due to gravity is not known. On bouncing it rises to 1.8 m. The ball loses its velocity on bouncing by a factor of
questions.explanation: v₁ = √(2gh₁), v₂ = √(2gh₂). Fractional loss = (v₁ - v₂)/v₁ = 1 - √(h₂/h₁) = 1 - √(1.8/5) = 1 - 0.6 = 0.4 = 2/5.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If the momentum of a body is increased by 50%, then the percentage increase in its kinetic energy is
questions.explanation: p₂ = 1.5p₁. KE ∝ p², so KE₂ = (1.5)² KE₁ = 2.25 KE₁. Percentage increase = (2.25 - 1) × 100 = 125%.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The KE acquired by a mass m in travelling a certain distance d starting from rest, under the action of a constant force is directly proportional to
questions.explanation: KE = Work done = F × d. Since F is constant and d is fixed, KE is independent of m.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two masses 1 g and 9 g are moving with equal kinetic energies. The ratio of the magnitudes of their respective linear momenta is
questions.explanation: p = √(2mKE). For equal KE, p ∝ √m. p₁ : p₂ = √1 : √9 = 1 : 3.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A position dependent force F = (7 - 2x + 3x²) N acts on a small body of mass 2 kg and displace it from x = 0 to x = 5 m. Work done in joule is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A bullet of mass 10 g leaves a rifle at an initial velocity of 1000 m/s and strikes the earth at the same level with a velocity of 500 m/s. The work done in joule to overcome the resistance of air will be

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Water falls from a height of 60 m at the rate of 15 kg/s to operate a turbine. The losses due to frictional force are 10% of the input energy. How much power is generated by the turbine? (g = 10 m/s²)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A point mass m is moved in a vertical circle of radius r with the help of a string. The velocity of the mass is √(7gr) at the lowest point. The tension in the string at the lowest point is
questions.explanation: At lowest point, T - mg = mv²/r ⇒ T = mg + m(7gr)/r = mg + 7mg = 8mg.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An object of mass 500 g initially at rest acted upon by a variable force whose X component varies with X in the manner shown. The velocities of the object at point X = 8 m and X = 12 m would be the respective values of (nearly)
questions.explanation: Work done = area under F-x curve. At x = 8 m, W = 130 J ⇒ 130 = ½ × 0.5 × v² ⇒ v = 2√130 = 22.8 ≈ 23 m/s. At x = 12 m, W = 105 J ⇒ v = 2√105 = 20.6 m/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A mass m is attached to a thin wire and whirled in a vertical circle. The wire is most likely to break when:

options.B: the mass is at the lowest point
options.C: inclined at an angle of 60° from vertical
options.D: the mass is at the highest point

questions.explanation: Tension is maximum at lowest point: T = mg + mv²/r, so wire is most likely to break at lowest point.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Consider a drop of rain water having mass 1 g falling from a height of 1 km. It hits the ground with a speed of 50 m/s. Take g constant with a value of 10 m/s². The work done by the (i) gravitational force and the (ii) resistive force of air is
questions.explanation: W_g = mgh = 10⁻³ × 10 × 1000 = 10 J. From work-energy theorem: ½mv² = W_g + W_air ⇒ W_air = ½ × 10⁻³ × 2500 - 10 = 1.25 - 10 = -8.75 J.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A body of mass 1 kg begins to move under the action of a time dependent force F = (2tî + 3t²ĵ) N where î and ĵ are unit vectors along X and Y axes. What power will be developed by the force at the time t?
questions.explanation: a = F/m = 2tî + 3t²ĵ. v = ∫a dt = t²î + t³ĵ. Power P = F·v = (2tî + 3t²ĵ)·(t²î + t³ĵ) = 2t³ + 3t⁵.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A particle of mass m is driven by a machine that delivers a constant power k watts. If the particle starts from rest, the force on the particle at time t is
questions.explanation: P = Fv = k constant. m v dv/dt = k ⇒ ∫v dv = (k/m)∫dt ⇒ v²/2 = (k/m)t ⇒ v = √(2kt/m). Then F = m dv/dt = m × ½√(2k/m) t⁻¹/² = √(mk/2) t⁻¹/².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The heart of a man pumps 5 L of blood through the arteries per minute at a pressure of 150 mm of mercury. If the density of mercury be 13.6 × 10³ kg/m³ and g = 10 m/s², then the power of heart in watt is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An engine pumps water through a hose pipe. Water passes through the pipe and leaves it with a velocity of 2 m/s. The mass per unit length of water in the pipe is 100 kg/m. What is the power of the engine?
questions.explanation: Mass flow rate = (mass/length) × velocity = 100 × 2 = 200 kg/s. Power = (mass flow rate) × v² = 200 × 4 = 800 W.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An engine pumps water continuously through a hose. Water leaves the hose with a velocity v and m is the mass per unit length of the water jet. What is the rate at which kinetic energy is imparted to water?
questions.explanation: Rate of mass flow = (mass/length) × velocity = mv. Rate of KE = ½ × (mass flow rate) × v² = ½(mv)v² = ½ mv³.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Water falls from a height of 60 m at the rate of 15 kg/s to operate a turbine. The losses due to frictional forces are 10% of energy. How much power is generated by the turbine? (Take g = 10 m/s²)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A stone is attached to one end of a string and rotated in a vertical circle. If string breaks at the position of maximum tension, it will break at
questions.explanation: Tension is maximum at lowest point (T = mg + mv²/r), so string will break at lowest point.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: How much water a pump of 2 kW can raise in one minute to a height of 10 m (Take g = 10 m/s²)
questions.explanation: Power = mgh/t ⇒ 2000 = m × 10 × 10/60 ⇒ m = (2000 × 60)/100 = 1200 kg = 1200 L. Best Choice Questions - Calculation-then-Eliminate Format 100% Unique Questions - No Repeats from Previous Sets

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A spherical conductor of radius 10 cm has a charge of 3.2 × 10⁻⁷ C distributed uniformly. What is the magnitude of electric field at a point 15 cm from the centre of the sphere? (1/4πε₀ = 9 × 10⁹ Nm²/C²)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two point charges A and B having charges +Q and -Q respectively, are placed at certain distance apart and force acting between them is F. If 25% charge of A is transferred to B then force between the charges becomes
questions.explanation: Initial force F = KQ²/r². After transfer: A = 0.75Q, B = -0.75Q. New force F' = K(0.75Q)²/r² = 0.5625 KQ²/r² = (9/16)F.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An electron is moving round the nucleus of a hydrogen atom in a circular orbit of radius r. The coulomb force F between the two is (where k = 1/4πε₀)
questions.explanation: Force is attractive, towards nucleus, so direction is opposite to position vector: F = -k e²/r² r̂.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: When air is replaced by a dielectric medium of constant K, the maximum force of attraction between two charges, separated by a distance
questions.explanation: F_medium = (1/4πε₀K) × q₁q₂/r² = F_air/K, so force decreases by factor K.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A charge q is placed at the centre of the line joining two exactly equal positive charges Q. The system of three charges will be in equilibrium, if q is equal to
questions.explanation: For equilibrium of Q at one end: force from other Q and from q must balance. KQ²/(2x)² + KQq/x² = 0 ⇒ Q/4 + q = 0 ⇒ q = -Q/4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Point charges +4q, -q and +4q are kept on the x-axis at points x = 0, x = a and x = 2a respectively. Then,

options.B: None of the charges is in equilibrium
options.C: all the charges are in unstable equilibrium
options.D: all the charges are in stable equilibrium

questions.explanation: Net force on each is zero, but small displacement leads to further displacement, so unstable.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A dipole is placed in an electric field as shown. In which direction will it move?

options.A: Towards the left as its potential energy will increase
options.B: Towards the right as its potential energy will decrease
options.C: Towards the left as its potential energy will decrease
options.D: Towards the right as its potential energy will increase

questions.explanation: System moves to decrease potential energy. Force on +q > force on -q (field stronger on right), so net force rightwards.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A sphere encloses an electric dipole with charge ±3 × 10⁻⁶ C. What is the total electric flux across the sphere?
questions.explanation: Net charge enclosed = +3 × 10⁻⁶ - 3 × 10⁻⁶ = 0. By Gauss's law, flux = q_enclosed/ε₀ = 0.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two parallel infinite line charges with linear charge densities +λ C/m and -λ C/m are placed at a distance of 2R in free space. What is the electric field midway between the two line charges?
questions.explanation: Field due to each line = λ/2πε₀R, both directed towards negative line. Net = λ/2πε₀R + λ/2πε₀R = λ/πε₀R.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A hollow metal sphere of radius R is uniformly charged. The electric field due to the sphere at a distance r from the centre

options.A: zero as r increases for r < R, decreases as r increases for r > R
options.B: zero as r increases for r < R, increases as r increases for r > R
options.C: decreases as r increases for r < R and for r > R
options.D: increases as r increases for r < R and for r > R

questions.explanation: Inside hollow sphere, E = 0. Outside, E ∝ 1/r², so decreases with r.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The electric field in a certain region is acting radially outward and is given by E = Ar. A charge contained in a sphere of radius a centred at the origin of the field will be given by
questions.explanation: By Gauss's law: E × 4πa² = q/ε₀ ⇒ Aa × 4πa² = q/ε₀ ⇒ q = 4πε₀ A a³.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: What is the flux through a cube of side a if a point charge of q is at one of its corner?
questions.explanation: Charge at corner is shared by 8 cubes. Flux through given cube = (1/8)(q/ε₀) = q/8ε₀.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A charge Q is enclosed by a Gaussian spherical surface of radius R. If the radius is doubled, then the outward electric flux will
questions.explanation: Flux depends only on enclosed charge, not on radius.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A square surface of side L metre in the plane of the paper is placed in a uniform electric field E(V/m) acting along the same plane at an angle θ with the horizontal side of the square. The electric flux linked to the surface is
questions.explanation: Area vector is perpendicular to plane. Field is parallel to plane, so angle = 90°, flux = EA cos 90° = 0.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A hollow cylinder has a charge q coulomb within it. If φ is the electric flux associated with the curved surface B, the flux linked with the plane surface A will be
questions.explanation: Total flux = q/ε₀ = φ + φ_A + φ_C. By symmetry, φ_A = φ_C. So 2φ_A + φ = q/ε₀ ⇒ φ_A = (q/ε₀ - φ)/2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A square surface of side L metre is in the plane of the paper. A uniform electric field E(V/m) also in the plane of the paper, is limited only to the lower half of the square surface. The electric flux associated with the surface is
questions.explanation: Field is parallel to surface (in plane), so angle = 90°, flux = 0 regardless of where field exists.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A charge q is located at the centre of a cube. The electric flux through any face is
questions.explanation: Total flux = q/ε₀. Through one face = (q/ε₀)/6 = q/6ε₀ = (4πq)/(6 × 4πε₀).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A charge q μC is placed at the centre of a cube of a side 0.1 m then the electric flux diverging from each face of the cube is
questions.explanation: Total flux = q × 10⁻⁶/ε₀. Through each face = (1/6) of total = q × 10⁻⁶/6ε₀.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A hollow insulated conducting sphere is given a positive charge of 10 μC. What will be the electric field at the centre of the sphere if its radius is 2 m?
questions.explanation: Inside a hollow conductor, electric field is zero everywhere.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A point charge +q is placed at mid-point of a cube of side L. The electric flux emerging from the cube is
questions.explanation: By Gauss's law, total flux through closed surface = enclosed charge/ε₀ = q/ε₀.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The electric field strength in air at NTP is 3 × 10⁶ V/m. The maximum charge that can be given to a spherical conductor of radius 3 m is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two spherical conductors A and B of radii 1 mm and 2 mm are separated by a distance of 5 cm and are uniformly charged. If the spheres are connected by a conducting wire then in equilibrium condition, the ratio of the magnitude of the electric fields at the surfaces of spheres A and B is
questions.explanation: After connection, potentials equal: KQ₁/r₁ = KQ₂/r₂ ⇒ Q₁/Q₂ = r₁/r₂ = 1/2. Electric field at surface: E = KQ/r², so E₁/E₂ = (Q₁/Q₂)(r₂²/r₁²) = (1/2)(4/1) = 2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Twenty seven drops of same size are charged at 220 V each. They combine to form a bigger drop. Calculate the potential of the bigger drop.
questions.explanation: Volume constant: (4/3)πR³ = 27 × (4/3)πr³ ⇒ R = 3r. Charge Q = 27q. Potential of big drop = KQ/R = K(27q)/(3r) = 9(Kq/r) = 9 × 220 = 1980 V.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two charged spherical conductors of radii R₁ and R₂ are connected by a wire. Then, the ratio of surface charge densities of the spheres (σ₁/σ₂) is
questions.explanation: After connection, potentials equal: KQ₁/R₁ = KQ₂/R₂ ⇒ Q₁/Q₂ = R₁/R₂. Surface charge density σ = Q/4πR², so σ₁/σ₂ = (Q₁/Q₂)(R₂²/R₁²) = (R₁/R₂)(R₂²/R₁²) = R₂/R₁.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In a certain region of space with volume 0.2 m³, the electric potential is found to be 5 V throughout. The magnitude of electric field in this region is
questions.explanation: E = -dV/dr. If V is constant, dV/dr = 0, so E = 0.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A short electric dipole has a dipole moment of 16 × 10⁻⁹ C-m. The electric potential due to the dipole at a point at a distance of 0.6 m from the centre of the dipole, situated on a line making an angle of 60° with the dipole axis is (1/4πε₀ = 9 × 10⁹ Nm²/C²)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The diagrams below show regions of equipotentials. A positive charge is moved from A to B in each diagram. The work done is
questions.explanation: Work done = q(V_B - V_A). Potential difference is 20 V in all cases.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: If potential (in volts) in a region is expressed as V(x, y, z) = 6xy - y + 2yz, the electric field (in N/C) at point (1, 1, 0) is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A conducting sphere of radius R is given a charge Q. The electric potential and the electric field at the centre of the sphere respectively are
questions.explanation: Inside conductor, E = 0, V = constant = surface potential = Q/4πε₀R.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In a region, the potential is represented by V(x, y, z) = 6x - 8xy - 8y + 6yz, where V is in volts and x, y, z are in metres. The electric force experienced by a charge of 2 C situated at point (1, 1, 1) is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A, B and C are three points in a uniform electric field. The electric potential is

options.D: same at all the three points A, B and C

questions.explanation: Electric field direction is from high to low potential. In uniform field, potential decreases along field direction.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Four point charges -Q, -q, 2q and 2Q are placed, one at each corner of the square. The relation between Q and q for which the potential at the centre of the square is zero, is
questions.explanation: Potential at centre = K(-Q)/r + K(-q)/r + K(2q)/r + K(2Q)/r = (K/r)(-Q - q + 2q + 2Q) = (K/r)(Q + q) = 0 ⇒ Q = -q.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Four electric charges +q, +q, -q and -q are placed at the corners of a square of side 2L. The electric potential at point A, midway between the two charges +q and +q is
questions.explanation: Distance from A to each +q = L, to each -q = √(L² + (2L)²) = √5 L. V = (1/4πε₀)[2q/L - 2q/√5 L] = (1/4πε₀)(2q/L)(1 - 1/√5).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: The electric potential at a point (x, y, z) is given by V = -x²y - xz³ + 4. The electric field E at that point is
questions.explanation: E = -∇V. ∂V/∂x = -2xy - z³, so -∂V/∂x = 2xy + z³. ∂V/∂y = -x², so -∂V/∂y = x². ∂V/∂z = -3xz², so -∂V/∂z = 3xz². Thus E = (2xy + z³)î + x²ĵ + 3xz²k̂.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Charges +q and -q are placed at points A and B respectively which are a distance 2L apart, C is the midpoint between A and B. The work done in moving a charge +Q along the semicircle CRD is
questions.explanation: Work = Q(V_D - V_C). V_C = Kq/L + K(-q)/L = 0. V_D = Kq/3L + K(-q)/L = Kq(1/3L - 1/L) = -2Kq/3L. So W = Q(-2Kq/3L) = -2QKq/3L = -2Qq/(3 × 4πε₀L) = -Qq/6πε₀L.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Two charges q₁ and q₂ are placed 30 cm apart, as shown in the figure. A third charge q₃ is moved along the arc of a circle of radius 40 cm from C to D. The change in the potential energy of the system is (q₃/4πε₀) k, where k is
questions.explanation: ΔU = U_D - U_C. At C: U_C = (1/4πε₀)[q₁q₃/0.4 + q₂q₃/0.5]. At D: U_D = (1/4πε₀)[q₁q₃/0.4 + q₂q₃/0.1]. ΔU = (q₃/4πε₀)[q₂(1/0.1 - 1/0.5)] = (q₃/4πε₀)[q₂(10 - 2)] = (q₃/4πε₀)(8q₂). So k = 8q₂.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A bullet of mass 2 g is having a charge of 2 μC. Through what potential difference must it be accelerated, starting from rest, to acquire a speed of 10 m/s?

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Some charge is being given to a conductor, then its potential is

options.D: maximum somewhere between surface and centre

questions.explanation: Conductor is equipotential.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Identical charges (-q) are placed at each corners of a cube of side b, then the electrostatic potential energy of charge (+q) placed at the centre of the cube will be
questions.explanation: Distance from centre to each corner = (√3 b)/2. U = 8 × (1/4πε₀) × (-q)(+q)/[(√3 b)/2] = 8 × (1/4πε₀) × (-q²) × (2/√3 b) = -16q²/(4√3 πε₀b) = -4q²/√3 πε₀b.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: In bringing an electron towards another electron, the electrostatic potential energy of the system
questions.explanation: Like charges repel, work done against force increases potential energy.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: An electron of mass m and charge e is accelerated from rest through a potential difference of V volt in vacuum. Its final speed will be
questions.explanation: ½mv² = eV ⇒ v² = 2eV/m ⇒ v = √(2eV/m).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: There is an electric field E in x-direction. If the work done on moving a charge of 0.2 C through a distance of 2 m along a line making an angle 60° with x-axis is 4 J then what is the value of E?

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A hollow metal sphere of radius 10 cm is charged such that the potential on its surface is 80 V. The potential at the centre of the sphere is
questions.explanation: Inside hollow conductor, potential constant = surface potential.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a fundamental quantity?
questions.explanation: Mass, length, and time are fundamental quantities. Force is a derived quantity (MLT⁻²).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a unit of pressure?
questions.explanation: Joule is the unit of energy or work. Pascal, N/m², and bar are all units of pressure.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a vector quantity?
questions.explanation: Speed is a scalar quantity (magnitude only). Velocity, acceleration, and force are vectors (have both magnitude and direction).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a unit of energy?
questions.explanation: Newton is the unit of force. Joule, erg, and electron volt (eV) are all units of energy.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a dimensionless quantity?
questions.explanation: Force has dimensions [MLT⁻²]. Strain, refractive index, and angle (radian) are dimensionless quantities.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of plane mirror image?
questions.explanation: Plane mirror always forms an image of the same size as the object (magnification = 1). It is virtual, erect, and laterally inverted.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for a concave mirror?
questions.explanation: Concave mirror can form erect virtual image when object is between pole and focus. It has negative focal length (sign convention) and can form both real and virtual images.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a property of convex mirror?
questions.explanation: Convex mirror always forms virtual, erect, and diminished image regardless of object position. It has positive focal length (sign convention) and never forms real image.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for total internal reflection?

options.A: Light travels from denser to rarer medium

questions.explanation: In total internal reflection, when angle of incidence > critical angle, 100% of light is reflected back into the denser medium. No light is transmitted.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a primary colour of light?
questions.explanation: Red, green, and blue are the primary colours of light (additive mixing). Yellow is a secondary colour (red + green).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for a prism?
questions.explanation: Prism disperses white light into its constituent colours only if the incident light is polychromatic. For monochromatic light, no spectrum is produced, only deviation occurs.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of laser light?
questions.explanation: Laser light is highly directional (low divergence), coherent, and monochromatic. It is not divergent like ordinary light.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for interference of light?

options.B: Produces alternate bright and dark fringes

questions.explanation: For sustained interference, sources must be coherent (same frequency, constant phase difference). Amplitudes can be equal or different, but equal amplitudes give better contrast.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a part of the electromagnetic spectrum?
questions.explanation: Sound waves are mechanical waves requiring a medium. Radio waves, X-rays, and gamma rays are all electromagnetic waves.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for electromagnetic waves?

options.B: They travel with speed of light in vacuum
options.C: They require a medium for propagation

questions.explanation: Electromagnetic waves do not require any medium for propagation. They can travel through vacuum.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following electromagnetic waves has the longest wavelength?
questions.explanation: Radio waves have the longest wavelength (from mm to km). Gamma rays have the shortest wavelength.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT correctly matched?

options.D: Gamma rays - Used in remote controls

questions.explanation: Remote controls use infrared rays, not gamma rays. Gamma rays are highly penetrating and used in cancer treatment, not in remote controls.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a use of microwaves?
questions.explanation: Sterilization is done using ultraviolet rays or gamma rays. Microwaves are used in radar, cooking (microwave ovens), and communication.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for X-rays?

options.B: They have wavelength shorter than UV
options.C: They are deflected by electric field

questions.explanation: X-rays are electromagnetic waves and are not deflected by electric or magnetic fields because they have no charge.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a property of intrinsic semiconductor?

options.A: Number of electrons = number of holes
options.C: Fermi level lies exactly in the middle of band gap
options.D: It has high conductivity at room temperature

questions.explanation: Intrinsic semiconductors have low conductivity at room temperature. Conductivity increases with temperature. In intrinsic semiconductors, n = p, and Fermi level is at the middle of the band gap.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for n-type semiconductor?

options.C: Fermi level is closer to conduction band
options.D: It has holes as majority carriers

questions.explanation: In n-type semiconductors, electrons are majority carriers, holes are minority carriers. Donor impurities (pentavalent) are added, and Fermi level is closer to conduction band.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a donor impurity for silicon?
questions.explanation: Boron is a trivalent impurity (acceptor), used to make p-type semiconductors. Phosphorus, arsenic, and antimony are pentavalent (donor) impurities.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for a p-n junction diode in forward bias?
questions.explanation: In forward bias, resistance is very low (typically few ohms). Depletion width decreases, barrier potential decreases, and current flows easily.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for a p-n junction diode in reverse bias?
questions.explanation: In reverse bias, resistance is very high (megaohms). Depletion width increases, barrier potential increases, and only a small leakage current flows.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of Zener diode?
questions.explanation: Zener diode is a one-way device like normal diode. It conducts in forward bias and in reverse bias after breakdown, but not equally in both directions.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a type of transistor configuration?
questions.explanation: The three transistor configurations are common base (CB), common emitter (CE), and common collector (CC). Common resistor is not a transistor configuration.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for common emitter configuration?

options.D: Output and input are in phase

questions.explanation: In CE configuration, output voltage is 180° out of phase with input voltage (phase reversal). It has high current gain, high voltage gain, and moderate input resistance.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for common base configuration?

options.D: Output and input are in phase

questions.explanation: Common base configuration has very low input resistance (few ohms). It has current gain α < 1, high voltage gain, and output is in phase with input.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a logic gate?
questions.explanation: Amplifier is not a logic gate; it is an analog circuit. AND, OR, NOT are basic logic gates.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a universal gate?
questions.explanation: NAND and NOR are universal gates because any logic gate can be realized using only NAND or only NOR gates. AND gate alone cannot realize all logic functions.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for a NAND gate?

options.A: It is equivalent to AND followed by NOT
options.B: Output is 0 only when all inputs are 1
options.D: Output is 1 only when all inputs are 1

questions.explanation: For NAND gate, output is 0 only when all inputs are 1. For all other input combinations, output is 1. Option (d) describes AND gate, not NAND.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for a NOR gate?

options.A: It is equivalent to OR followed by NOT
options.B: Output is 1 only when all inputs are 0
options.D: Output is 0 only when all inputs are 0

questions.explanation: For NOR gate, output is 1 only when all inputs are 0. For all other input combinations, output is 0. Option (d) describes OR gate.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a Boolean algebra law?
questions.explanation: In Boolean algebra, A · A = A (idempotent law), not 0. A · A' = 0 is correct.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a type of semiconductor?
questions.explanation: Conductor is not a type of semiconductor. Semiconductors are classified as intrinsic and extrinsic (n-type and p-type).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for a photodiode?
questions.explanation: Photodiode converts light energy into electrical energy (photocurrent). LED converts electrical energy into light.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for an LED?

options.C: Light colour depends on band gap

questions.explanation: LED converts electrical energy into light (electroluminescence). Photodiode converts light into electrical energy.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for a solar cell?
questions.explanation: Solar cell operates with zero bias (no external voltage). Light generates electron-hole pairs which are separated by the built-in field of the junction.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a factor affecting resistance of a conductor?
questions.explanation: Resistance depends on length, area, temperature, and nature of material (resistivity). Colour does not affect resistance.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for Ohm's law?

options.B: It is obeyed by all materials
options.C: Graph between V and I is a straight line

questions.explanation: Ohm's law is not obeyed by all materials. Semiconductors, electrolytes, and devices like diodes do not obey Ohm's law (non-ohmic materials).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a unit of resistance?
questions.explanation: Siemens is the unit of conductance (reciprocal of resistance). Ohm, Volt/Ampere, and Ω are units of resistance.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for Kirchhoff's laws?

options.A: Junction law follows from conservation of charge
options.B: Loop law follows from conservation of energy
options.C: Junction law states ΣI = 0 at a junction
options.D: Loop law is not applicable in AC circuits

questions.explanation: Kirchhoff's laws are applicable in both DC and AC circuits (using phasors in AC). Junction law (KCL) and loop law (KVL) are fundamental circuit laws.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a magnetic material?
questions.explanation: Iron, nickel, and cobalt are ferromagnetic materials. Copper is diamagnetic (weakly repelled by magnets).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for a bar magnet?

options.A: It has two poles - North and South
options.D: It aligns in North-South direction when suspended freely

questions.explanation: Isolated magnetic poles (monopoles) do not exist. Magnetic poles always occur in pairs (North and South).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a property of diamagnetic materials?
questions.explanation: Diamagnetic materials are weakly repelled by magnets, not attracted. They have negative susceptibility, which is temperature independent.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for electromagnetic induction?

options.A: Induced emf is produced when magnetic flux changes
options.B: Direction of induced emf is given by Lenz's law
options.C: Induced emf depends on resistance of the circuit
options.D: Self-inductance is the property of a coil to oppose change in current

questions.explanation: Induced emf depends only on rate of change of flux (e = -dφ/dt). It is independent of circuit resistance. Induced current depends on resistance.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a unit of length?
questions.explanation: Tesla is the unit of magnetic field induction. Metre, kilometre, and angstrom are all units of length.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a fundamental unit in SI system?
questions.explanation: Newton is a derived unit (kg m/s²). Metre, kilogram, and second are fundamental SI units.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a unit of time?
questions.explanation: Light year is a unit of distance (distance travelled by light in one year). Second, hour, and year are units of time.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a dimensionless constant?
questions.explanation: G has dimensions [M⁻¹L³T⁻²]. π and e are mathematical constants, refractive index is dimensionless.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a type of mirror?
questions.explanation: Prism is not a mirror; it is a refracting device. Plane, concave, and convex are types of mirrors.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for the image formed by a convex mirror?
questions.explanation: Convex mirror always forms virtual, erect, and diminished images. It never forms inverted images.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for the image formed by a concave mirror when the object is at infinity?

options.D: Image is formed behind the mirror

questions.explanation: When object is at infinity, concave mirror forms image at focus, which is real, inverted, and highly diminished. The image is formed in front of the mirror, not behind.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following colours has the longest wavelength in visible spectrum?
questions.explanation: In visible spectrum, red has the longest wavelength (≈700 nm) and violet has the shortest (≈400 nm).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for dispersion of light?

options.A: Different colours have different speeds in a medium
options.C: All colours have same speed in vacuum
options.D: Dispersion is greater for longer wavelengths

questions.explanation: Dispersion is greater for shorter wavelengths (violet bends more than red). Shorter wavelengths have higher refractive index and deviate more.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of a rainbow?
questions.explanation: Rainbow formation involves refraction, dispersion, and total internal reflection in water droplets. Diffraction is not involved.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for Huygens' principle?

options.A: Every point on wavefront acts as secondary source
options.C: New wavefront is the envelope of secondary wavelets
options.D: It explains particle nature of light

questions.explanation: Huygens' principle explains wave nature of light (wavefront propagation). Particle nature is explained by photon theory.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an electromagnetic wave?
questions.explanation: Sound waves are mechanical waves requiring a medium. Radio waves, light waves, and X-rays are electromagnetic waves.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a use of ultraviolet rays?
