questions.explanation: Night vision devices use infrared rays, not ultraviolet. UV rays are used for sterilization, detecting forged currency, and vitamin D synthesis.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for an intrinsic semiconductor?

options.A: Number of electrons = number of holes
options.C: Fermi level is exactly at the centre of band gap
options.D: It has high conductivity at absolute zero

questions.explanation: At absolute zero, intrinsic semiconductors behave as insulators with no free electrons. Conductivity increases with temperature.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a pentavalent impurity?
questions.explanation: Boron is trivalent (3 valence electrons), used as acceptor impurity for p-type semiconductors. Phosphorus, arsenic, and antimony are pentavalent (donors).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for a p-type semiconductor?

options.C: Fermi level is closer to valence band

questions.explanation: In p-type semiconductors, holes are majority carriers, electrons are minority carriers. Fermi level is closer to valence band.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for a p-n junction diode?

options.A: It allows current in one direction only
options.C: It can be used as a rectifier

questions.explanation: Diode does not amplify signals; it rectifies them. Amplification is done by transistors.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for forward bias?
questions.explanation: In forward bias, depletion width decreases. In reverse bias, depletion width increases.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for reverse bias?
questions.explanation: In reverse bias, depletion width increases. Very small leakage current flows due to minority carriers.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for Zener diode?

options.B: Breakdown voltage is sharp and well-defined
options.C: Voltage across it remains constant after breakdown
options.D: It is always used in forward bias

questions.explanation: Zener diode is specifically designed to operate in reverse bias breakdown region for voltage regulation.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a transistor configuration?
questions.explanation: The three transistor configurations are common base, common emitter, and common collector (emitter follower). Common ground is not a configuration.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for common collector configuration?
questions.explanation: Common collector configuration has voltage gain approximately 1 (less than 1). It has high current gain and high input resistance.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for a transistor in cut-off region?

options.C: Transistor acts as an open switch

questions.explanation: In cut-off region, collector current is nearly zero (transistor OFF). Maximum collector current occurs in saturation region.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for a transistor in saturation region?

options.C: Transistor acts as a closed switch

questions.explanation: In saturation, collector current is maximum, not zero. Zero current occurs in cut-off region.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a basic logic gate?
questions.explanation: Flip-flop is a sequential circuit element made from logic gates. AND, OR, and NOT are basic logic gates.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for an AND gate?

options.A: Output is 1 only when all inputs are 1
options.B: It can have two or more inputs
options.C: Output is 1 when any input is 1

questions.explanation: AND gate gives output 1 only when ALL inputs are 1. OR gate gives output 1 when any input is 1.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for an OR gate?

options.A: Output is 1 when at least one input is 1
options.B: Output is 0 only when all inputs are 0
options.D: Output is 1 only when all inputs are 1

questions.explanation: OR gate gives output 1 when at least one input is 1. Output 1 only when all inputs are 1 is the property of AND gate.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a valid Boolean identity?
questions.explanation: A · A = A (idempotent law), not 0. A · A' = 0 is the correct complement law.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a factor affecting the resistance of a wire?
questions.explanation: Resistance depends on length, area, material (resistivity), and temperature. Colour of insulation does not affect resistance.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for resistors in series?

options.A: Same current flows through each resistor
options.C: Equivalent resistance is sum of individual resistances
options.D: Same voltage appears across each resistor

questions.explanation: In series, current is same through all resistors, but voltage divides. Same voltage appears across each resistor is true for parallel combination.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for resistors in parallel?

options.A: Same voltage appears across each resistor
options.B: Current divides inversely proportional to resistance
options.C: Equivalent resistance is less than the smallest resistance
options.D: Same current flows through each resistor

questions.explanation: In parallel, voltage is same across all resistors, but current divides. Same current flows through each resistor is true for series combination.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for Kirchhoff's current law (KCL)?

options.A: It is based on conservation of charge
options.B: Sum of currents entering a junction equals sum leaving
options.C: Algebraic sum of currents at a junction is zero
options.D: It is not applicable in AC circuits

questions.explanation: KCL is applicable in AC circuits as well (using phasor sums). It follows from conservation of charge.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for a magnetic field line?

options.C: They start from north pole and end at south pole

questions.explanation: Magnetic field lines are curved, not always straight. They form closed loops, never intersect, and outside a magnet they go from north to south.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for diamagnetic materials?

options.C: They have no permanent magnetic dipoles
options.D: They are strongly attracted by magnets

questions.explanation: Diamagnetic materials are weakly repelled, not attracted. Ferromagnetic materials are strongly attracted.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for paramagnetic materials?

options.B: They are weakly attracted by magnets
options.D: They have no permanent magnetic dipoles

questions.explanation: Paramagnetic materials have permanent magnetic dipoles that align with external field. Diamagnetic materials have no permanent dipoles.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a unit of magnetic field?
questions.explanation: Weber is the unit of magnetic flux. Tesla, gauss, and oersted are units of magnetic field.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for Lenz's law?

options.A: It gives direction of induced current
options.B: Induced current opposes the change in flux
options.C: It is a consequence of conservation of energy
options.D: Induced current supports the change in flux

questions.explanation: Lenz's law states that induced current opposes (not supports) the change in flux that produces it.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for an ideal transformer?

options.D: It works on both AC and DC

questions.explanation: Transformer works only on AC because it requires changing magnetic flux. DC produces constant flux, so no induced emf in secondary.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for a step-up transformer?
questions.explanation: In step-up transformer, voltage increases but current decreases (since power is constant). I_s < I_p, not greater.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a unit of electric current?
questions.explanation: Volt is the unit of potential difference. Ampere, coulomb/second, and volt/ohm (from Ohm's law V = IR ⇒ I = V/R) are all units of current.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a unit of electric potential?
questions.explanation: N/C is the unit of electric field (force per unit charge). Volt, J/C, and Nm/C are all units of electric potential.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a unit of capacitance?
questions.explanation: Ohm is the unit of resistance. Farad, C/V, and coulomb/volt are all units of capacitance (C = Q/V).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a unit of magnetic flux?
questions.explanation: Gauss is the unit of magnetic field (1 Gauss = 10⁻⁴ Tesla). Weber, Tesla·m², and Volt·second are units of magnetic flux.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a type of lever?
questions.explanation: Levers are classified into three classes based on the position of fulcrum, load, and effort. There is no fourth class lever.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for a first class lever?

options.A: Fulcrum is between load and effort
options.D: Load is between fulcrum and effort

questions.explanation: In first class lever, fulcrum is between load and effort. Load between fulcrum and effort is characteristic of second class lever.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a simple machine?
questions.explanation: Engine is a complex machine. Lever, pulley, and inclined plane are simple machines.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for mechanical advantage (MA)?
questions.explanation: MA can be less than 1, equal to 1, or greater than 1 depending on the machine. MA is a ratio and has no unit.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a state of matter?
questions.explanation: Energy is not a state of matter. The three common states of matter are solid, liquid, and gas (plasma is the fourth state).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for solids?
questions.explanation: Solids have very low compressibility. Gases have high compressibility.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for liquids?

options.A: Definite volume but no definite shape
options.B: Particles can move past each other

questions.explanation: Liquids have very low compressibility. They are nearly incompressible.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for gases?
questions.explanation: Gases have very weak intermolecular forces. Strong intermolecular forces are characteristic of solids.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a type of stress?
questions.explanation: The correct term is volumetric stress or bulk stress. "Volume stress" is not the standard terminology.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a type of strain?
questions.explanation: Pressure is stress, not strain. The three types of strain are longitudinal, volumetric, and shear strain.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for Young's modulus?

options.A: It is the ratio of longitudinal stress to longitudinal strain
options.B: It is applicable for solids only
options.C: It is independent of the dimensions of the body

questions.explanation: Young's modulus has the same unit as stress: N/m² (Pascal). N/m is the unit of force per unit length (spring constant).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for bulk modulus?

options.A: It is the ratio of volumetric stress to volumetric strain
options.B: It is applicable for solids, liquids, and gases
options.C: For gases, bulk modulus depends on the process

questions.explanation: Bulk modulus has unit N/m² (Pascal), the same as pressure. m²/N would be the unit of compressibility (reciprocal of bulk modulus).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a mode of heat transfer?
questions.explanation: Reflection is an optical phenomenon, not a mode of heat transfer. The three modes of heat transfer are conduction, convection, and radiation.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for conduction?

options.D: Faster in metals due to free electrons

questions.explanation: Conduction occurs in solids, liquids, and gases, though it is most significant in solids. In liquids and gases, conduction is usually much slower than convection.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for convection?

options.D: Natural convection occurs due to density differences

questions.explanation: Convection occurs only in fluids (liquids and gases). Solids cannot undergo convection because particles cannot move freely.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for thermal radiation?

options.B: All objects emit radiation at all temperatures
options.C: Emitted radiation depends only on temperature

questions.explanation: Thermal radiation depends on temperature and also on the nature of the surface (emissivity). For a black body, radiation depends only on temperature.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a type of thermometer?
questions.explanation: Speedometer measures speed, not temperature. Mercury, alcohol, and gas thermometers measure temperature.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true for absolute zero temperature?

options.C: At this temperature, molecular motion ceases
options.D: It is the highest possible temperature

questions.explanation: Absolute zero is the lowest possible temperature, not the highest. There is no upper limit to temperature.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): The velocity of a body can be changed without changing its speed. Reason (R): Velocity is a vector quantity while speed is a scalar.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Velocity being a vector can change by changing direction while magnitude (speed) remains constant. This is possible in uniform circular motion.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): In uniform circular motion, the acceleration of the particle is directed towards the centre. Reason (R): The velocity and acceleration vectors are perpendicular to each other in uniform circular motion.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Acceleration is towards centre due to centripetal force, not because velocity and acceleration are perpendicular. The perpendicular nature is a consequence, not the cause.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): A body moving on a circular track has constant acceleration. Reason (R): In circular motion, the direction of velocity changes continuously.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: In circular motion, acceleration is not constant in direction (centripetal acceleration always points to centre, changing direction as particle moves). R is true but A is false.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): In a uniform circular motion, the work done by centripetal force is zero. Reason (R): Centripetal force is always perpendicular to the displacement of the particle.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Work done = F·s cos θ. Since centripetal force is perpendicular to displacement (θ = 90°), work done is zero.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): A particle in uniform circular motion is not in equilibrium. Reason (R): A particle is in equilibrium only when net force acting on it is zero.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: In uniform circular motion, centripetal force acts on the particle, so net force ≠ 0. Hence particle is not in equilibrium.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): For a body rotating about a fixed axis, the angular acceleration is independent of the choice of origin. Reason (R): Angular acceleration is an axial vector.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Angular acceleration is independent of origin because it depends on change in angular velocity, which is same for all points on rigid body. Being axial vector is a property, not the reason for origin independence.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): In pure rolling motion, the velocity of the point of contact with respect to the surface is zero. Reason (R): Rolling is the combination of translation and rotation such that v = ωR.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: In pure rolling, velocity of point of contact = v - ωR = 0 ⇒ v = ωR. This is the condition for no slipping.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): The moment of inertia of a rigid body depends on the distribution of mass about the axis of rotation. Reason (R): Moment of inertia is given by I = Σmr².

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: I = Σmr² clearly shows dependence on mass (m) and distance from axis (r), i.e., mass distribution.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): Radius of gyration of a body depends on the axis of rotation. Reason (R): Radius of gyration is defined as k = √(I/M).

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Since I depends on axis, k = √(I/M) also depends on axis.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): A solid sphere and a hollow sphere of same mass and radius, when rolled down an inclined plane, the solid sphere reaches the bottom first. Reason (R): Moment of inertia of solid sphere is less than that of hollow sphere.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Acceleration a = g sin θ/(1 + I/MR²). Lower I gives higher acceleration, so solid sphere (I = 2/5 MR²) reaches first compared to hollow sphere (I = 2/3 MR²).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): In simple harmonic motion, the acceleration of the particle is proportional to its displacement from mean position. Reason (R): In SHM, the restoring force is proportional to displacement and directed towards mean position.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: F = -kx, and F = ma ⇒ a = -(k/m)x, so acceleration ∝ -displacement.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): The time period of a simple pendulum is independent of the mass of the bob. Reason (R): The restoring force is proportional to the mass of the bob.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: T = 2π√(L/g) is indeed independent of mass. Restoring force = mg sin θ ∝ m, but acceleration = F/m = g sin θ, so m cancels out. R is true but doesn't explain A directly - the cancellation explains it.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): In SHM, the total energy of the particle is constant. Reason (R): The restoring force acting on the particle is conservative in nature.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Conservative force implies mechanical energy conservation. SHM force F = -kx is conservative.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): For a particle executing SHM, the velocity is maximum at the mean position. Reason (R): At mean position, the displacement is zero and the acceleration is zero.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: v = ω√(A² - x²) is maximum at x = 0. R is true but doesn't explain why v is maximum - it's because all energy becomes kinetic at mean position.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): In SHM, the phase difference between displacement and acceleration is π. Reason (R): In SHM, acceleration is proportional to negative of displacement.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: If x = A sin ωt, then a = -ω²A sin ωt = ω²A sin(ωt + π). So phase difference = π, and this comes from a ∝ -x.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): Zeroth law of thermodynamics leads to the concept of temperature. Reason (R): Zeroth law states that if two bodies are in thermal equilibrium with a third, they are in thermal equilibrium with each other.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Zeroth law establishes temperature as a property that determines thermal equilibrium.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): In an adiabatic process, the change in internal energy of a gas is equal to the work done on the gas. Reason (R): In an adiabatic process, no heat is transferred between the system and surroundings.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: From first law, ΔQ = ΔU + ΔW. For adiabatic, ΔQ = 0 ⇒ ΔU = -ΔW. Work done on gas = -ΔW, so ΔU = work done on gas.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): The efficiency of a Carnot engine depends only on the temperatures of source and sink. Reason (R): Carnot engine is a reversible engine operating between two temperatures.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Efficiency η = 1 - T₂/T₁ depends only on temperatures. R describes Carnot engine but doesn't explain why efficiency depends only on temperatures - that comes from reversibility and second law.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): In an isothermal expansion of an ideal gas, the internal energy remains constant. Reason (R): Internal energy of an ideal gas depends only on temperature.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: For ideal gas, U = f(T). Isothermal means T constant, so U constant.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): The efficiency of a heat engine can never be unity. Reason (R): Second law of thermodynamics forbids conversion of entire heat into work.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Kelvin-Planck statement of second law: no process is possible whose sole result is the conversion of heat completely into work.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): The coefficient of performance of a refrigerator can be greater than unity. Reason (R): Refrigerator transfers heat from cold to hot body by doing work.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: COP = Q₂/W can be > 1 because Q₂ can be greater than W. R describes how refrigerator works but doesn't explain why COP > 1.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): Entropy of an isolated system always increases. Reason (R): Second law of thermodynamics states that entropy of an isolated system never decreases.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Second law directly gives that entropy of isolated system increases (for irreversible processes) or remains constant (for reversible).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): When a current-carrying wire is placed in a magnetic field, it experiences a force. Reason (R): The force is given by F = BIL sin θ.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: R gives the formula for force, not the reason why force exists. The reason is that moving charges experience Lorentz force in magnetic field.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): The magnetic flux linked with a coil changes, an emf is induced in the coil. Reason (R): Faraday's law of electromagnetic induction states that induced emf is equal to rate of change of flux.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Faraday's law directly gives e = -dφ/dt, so change in flux induces emf.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): Lenz's law is a consequence of conservation of energy. Reason (R): The induced emf always opposes the cause that produces it.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Lenz's law states that induced emf opposes the cause. This ensures conservation of energy - work done against opposing force appears as electrical energy. R states Lenz's law, A says it follows from energy conservation.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): When a magnet is moved towards a coil, the induced current opposes the motion of the magnet. Reason (R): The induced current produces a magnetic field that opposes the change in flux.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: As magnet approaches, flux increases. Induced current produces field opposing this increase, which repels the magnet, opposing its motion.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): Self-inductance of a coil depends on the geometry of the coil. Reason (R): Self-inductance is given by L = μ₀N²A/l for a solenoid.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Formula shows dependence on N (number of turns), A (area), l (length) - all geometrical parameters.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): Mutual inductance between two coils depends on their geometry and separation. Reason (R): Mutual inductance is given by M = k√(L₁L₂).

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: M depends on geometry and separation. R gives relation between M and self-inductances with coupling coefficient k, which itself depends on geometry.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): In an AC circuit containing only an inductor, the current lags behind the voltage by 90°. Reason (R): In a pure inductor, the induced emf opposes the change in current.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: When voltage increases, inductor opposes current increase, causing current to reach maximum later than voltage - phase lag of 90°.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): In a series LCR circuit at resonance, the current is maximum. Reason (R): At resonance, the impedance of the circuit is minimum and equal to R.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: At resonance, X_L = X_C, so Z = √[R² + (X_L - X_C)²] = R, minimum. I = V/Z is maximum.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): Power factor of a series LCR circuit at resonance is unity. Reason (R): At resonance, the phase difference between voltage and current is zero.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Power factor = cos φ. At resonance, φ = 0, so cos φ = 1.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): In a transformer, if the secondary coil has more turns than the primary, the voltage is stepped up. Reason (R): For an ideal transformer, V_s/V_p = N_s/N_p.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: The turns ratio formula directly gives that if N_s > N_p, then V_s > V_p.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): Alpha particles are helium nuclei. Reason (R): Alpha particles have mass number 4 and atomic number 2.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: A = 4, Z = 2 exactly matches helium nucleus (²He₄).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): In a radioactive decay, the nucleus may emit a beta particle. Reason (R): Beta decay involves conversion of a neutron into a proton inside the nucleus.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: β⁻ decay: n → p + e⁻ + ν̄. The emitted electron is the beta particle.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): The half-life of a radioactive substance is independent of temperature and pressure. Reason (R): Radioactive decay is a nuclear phenomenon and does not depend on external physical conditions.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Nuclear forces are unaffected by temperature/pressure, so decay rate constant.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): In a nuclear fission reaction, a heavy nucleus splits into two lighter nuclei with release of energy. Reason (R): The binding energy per nucleon of fission products is greater than that of the parent nucleus.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Higher binding energy per nucleon means more stable, and the mass defect appears as released energy.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): In a nuclear fusion reaction, light nuclei combine to form a heavier nucleus with release of energy. Reason (R): The binding energy per nucleon increases with mass number for light nuclei.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: For A < 20, binding energy per nucleon increases with A, so fusion to higher A releases energy.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): In a p-n junction diode, the depletion region has immobile ions. Reason (R): The depletion region is formed by diffusion of charge carriers across the junction.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: When electrons and holes diffuse across junction, they leave behind ionized atoms (donors on n-side, acceptors on p-side) that are immobile.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): A p-n junction diode allows current to flow easily in forward bias but not in reverse bias. Reason (R): In forward bias, the depletion region width decreases, while in reverse bias it increases.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Smaller depletion region in forward bias allows easier carrier flow. Larger depletion region in reverse bias blocks current.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): A Zener diode is used as a voltage regulator. Reason (R): In reverse bias, after breakdown, the voltage across Zener diode remains nearly constant even with changing current.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Constant voltage characteristic in breakdown region makes Zener ideal for voltage regulation.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): An n-type semiconductor is electrically neutral. Reason (R): In n-type semiconductor, the number of free electrons equals the number of holes.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: n-type semiconductor is neutral because total positive charge (holes + donor ions) equals total negative charge (electrons). Number of electrons ≠ number of holes in n-type (electrons are majority).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): In a transistor, the emitter is heavily doped. Reason (R): Heavily doping emitter increases the number of charge carriers injected into the base.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: High doping in emitter ensures large number of carriers for injection into base, improving current gain.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): In a common emitter amplifier, the output voltage is 180° out of phase with the input voltage. Reason (R): When input signal increases, collector current increases, causing voltage drop across load resistance to increase, which decreases collector voltage.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Increase in input → increase in I_C → increase in voltage drop across R_C → decrease in V_CE (output), hence 180° phase shift.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): NAND and NOR gates are called universal gates. Reason (R): Any logic gate can be realized using only NAND gates or only NOR gates.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Universal gates can implement any Boolean function. NAND and NOR have this property.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Assertion (A): The input resistance of a common emitter transistor amplifier is low. Reason (R): The base-emitter junction is forward biased.

options.A: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.B: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
options.C: A is true but R is false
options.D: A is false but R is true

questions.explanation: Forward-biased junction has low resistance, so input resistance at base is low.
