Consider the following statements about centre of mass and torque:
a. Two particles of mass 5 kg and 10 kg respectively are attached to the two ends of a rigid rod of length 1 m with negligible mass. The centre of mass of the system from the 5 kg particle is nearly at a distance of 67 cm.
b. The moment of the force, F = 4î + 5ĵ - 6k̂ at (2, 0, -3), about the point (2, -2, -2), is given by -7î - 4ĵ - 8k̂.
c. A rope is wound around a hollow cylinder of mass 3 kg and radius 40 cm. The angular acceleration of the cylinder, if the rope is pulled with a force of 30 N, is 25 rad/s².
questions.explanation:
Statement a: Correct. Let distance from 5 kg mass be x. Then 5x = 10(1-x) ⇒ 5x = 10 - 10x ⇒ 15x = 10 ⇒ x = 2/3 m = 66.7 cm ≈ 67 cm.
Statement b: Correct. r₁ = (2,0,-3), r₂ = (2,-2,-2). r = r₁ - r₂ = (0,2,-1). τ = r × F = determinant |i j k; 0 2 -1; 4 5 -6| = i(2×(-6) - (-1)×5) - j(0×(-6) - (-1)×4) + k(0×5 - 2×4) = i(-12+5) - j(0+4) + k(0-8) = -7î -4ĵ -8k̂.
Statement c: Correct. τ = Fr = 30 × 0.4 = 12 Nm. I = mr² = 3 × (0.4)² = 0.48 kg m². α = τ/I = 12/0.48 = 25 rad/s².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Consider the following statements about moment of inertia:
a. A thin circular ring of mass M and radius R is rotating about its axis with constant angular velocity ω. Four objects each of mass m are kept gently to the opposite ends of two perpendicular diameters of the ring. The angular velocity of the ring will be Mω/(M+4m).
b. A rod is of length 3 m and its mass acting per unit length is directly proportional to distance x from its one end. The centre of gravity of the rod from that end will be at 2 m.
c. A disc is rotating with angular velocity ω. If a child sits on it, angular momentum is conserved.
questions.explanation:
Statement a: Correct. Angular momentum conserved: I₁ω₁ = I₂ω₂ ⇒ MR²ω = (MR² + 4mR²)ω₂ ⇒ ω₂ = Mω/(M+4m).
Statement b: Incorrect. For linear mass density λ ∝ x, dm = kx dx. Centre of mass x_cm = ∫x dm / ∫dm = ∫₀³ x·kx dx / ∫₀³ kx dx = (k[ x³/3]₀³)/(k[ x²/2]₀³) = (27/3)/(9/2) = 9/(4.5) = 2 m. This is correct! Source says 1.5 m, but calculation gives 2 m. Let's verify: ∫x² dx from 0 to 3 = 27/3 = 9. ∫x dx = 9/2 = 4.5. Ratio = 9/4.5 = 2. So statement is correct.
Statement c: Correct. No external torque, so angular momentum conserved.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Consider the following statements about gravitation:
a. A geostationary satellite is orbiting the earth at a height of 5R above the surface of the earth, R being the radius of the earth. The time period of another satellite at a height of 2R from the surface of the earth is 6√2 h.
b. Two spheres of masses m and M are situated in air and the gravitational force between them is F. The space around the masses is now filled with a liquid of specific gravity 3. The gravitational force will now be F.
c. The period of revolution of planet A round the sun is 8 times that of B. The distance of A from the sun is 4 times greater than that of B from the sun.
questions.explanation:
Statement a: Correct. T² ∝ r³. For geostationary, r₁ = 6R, T₁ = 24 h. For second, r₂ = 3R. T₂/T₁ = (r₂/r₁)^(3/2) = (3R/6R)^(3/2) = (1/2)^(3/2) = 1/(2√2). T₂ = 24/(2√2) = 12/√2 = 6√2 h.
Statement b: Correct. Gravitational force does not depend on intervening medium.
Statement c: Correct. T² ∝ r³. (T_A/T_B)² = (r_A/r_B)³ ⇒ (8)² = (r_A/r_B)³ ⇒ 64 = (r_A/r_B)³ ⇒ r_A/r_B = 4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Consider the following statements about acceleration due to gravity:
a. A body projected vertically from the earth reaches a height equal to earth's radius before returning to the earth. The power exerted by the gravitational force is greatest at the instant just before the body hits the earth.
b. A particle of mass M is situated at the centre of a spherical shell of same mass and radius a. The gravitational potential at a point situated at a/2 distance from the centre is -3GM/a.
c. Imagine a new planet having the same density as that of the earth but it is 3 times bigger than the earth in size. If the acceleration due to gravity on the surface of the earth is g and that on the surface of the new planet is g', then g' = 3g.
questions.explanation:
Statement a: Correct. Power = F·v = mgv. Just before hitting, v is maximum, so power is maximum.
Statement b: Correct. Potential at point inside shell due to shell = -GM/a (constant). Due to point mass at centre = -GM/(a/2) = -2GM/a. Total = -3GM/a.
Statement c: Correct. g = GM/R² = G(4/3πR³ρ)/R² = (4/3)πGρR. So g ∝ R. If R' = 3R, then g' = 3g.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Consider the following statements about escape speed and satellites:
a. The ratio of escape velocity at earth (v_e) to the escape velocity at a planet (v_p) whose radius and mean density are twice as that of earth is 1:2√2.
b. A satellite of mass m is orbiting the earth (of radius R) at a height h from its surface. The total energy of the satellite in terms of g₀, the value of acceleration due to gravity at the earth's surface, is -mg₀R²/[2(R+h)].
c. A remote sensing satellite of earth revolves in a circular orbit at a height of 0.25 × 10⁶ m above the surface of earth. If earth's radius is 6.38 × 10⁶ m and g = 9.8 ms⁻², then the orbital speed of the satellite is 7.76 km/s.
questions.explanation:
Statement a: Correct. v_e ∝ √(M/R) ∝ √(ρR³/R) ∝ R√ρ. For planet, R' = 2R, ρ' = 2ρ, so v_p ∝ 2R√(2ρ) = 2√2 R√ρ. So v_e/v_p = 1/(2√2).
Statement b: Correct. Total energy = -GMm/(2(R+h)). Since g₀ = GM/R², GM = g₀R². So energy = -g₀R²m/[2(R+h)].
Statement c: Correct. v = √[GM/(R+h)] = √[gR²/(R+h)] = √[9.8 × (6.38×10⁶)²/(6.63×10⁶)] = √[9.8 × 40.7×10¹²/6.63×10⁶] = √(60.2×10⁶) = 7.76×10³ m/s = 7.76 km/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Consider the following statements about mechanical properties of solids:
a. A wire of length L, area of cross-section A is hanging from a fixed support. The length of the wire changes to L₁ when mass M is suspended from its free end. The expression for Young's modulus is MgL/[A(L₁ - L)].
b. Two wires are made of the same material and have the same volume. The first wire has cross-sectional area A and the second wire has cross-sectional area 3A. If the length of the first wire is increased by Δl on applying force F, the force needed to stretch the second wire by the same amount is 9F.
c. The bulk modulus of a spherical object is B. If it is subjected to uniform pressure p, the fractional decrease in radius is p/(3B).
questions.explanation:
Statement a: Correct. Y = (F/A)/(ΔL/L) = (Mg/A)/[(L₁-L)/L] = MgL/[A(L₁-L)].
Statement b: Correct. Y = FL₁/(A₁Δl) = FL₂/(A₂Δl) with same Δl. Volume same: A₁L₁ = A₂L₂. L₂/L₁ = A₁/A₂ = 1/3. So F₂/F₁ = (A₂/A₁)(L₁/L₂) = (3)(3) = 9.
Statement c: Correct. B = -p/(ΔV/V). For sphere, ΔV/V = 3ΔR/R. So ΔR/R = -p/(3B).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Consider the following statements about mechanical properties of fluids:
a. In a U-tube as shown, water and oil are in the left side and right side of the tube respectively. The heights from the bottom for water and oil columns are 15 cm and 20 cm respectively. The density of the oil is 750 kg/m³.
b. A small hole of area of cross-section 2 mm² is present near the bottom of a fully filled open tank of height 2 m. Taking g = 10 m/s², the rate of flow of water through the open hole would be nearly 12.6 × 10⁻⁶ m³/s.
c. A small sphere of radius r falls from rest in a viscous liquid. As a result, heat is produced due to viscous force. The rate of production of heat when the sphere attains its terminal velocity is proportional to r⁵.
questions.explanation:
Statement a: Correct. Pressure at same horizontal level equal: 15ρ_w g = 20ρ_o g ⇒ ρ_o = (15/20)×1000 = 750 kg/m³.
Statement b: Correct. v = √(2gh) = √(2×10×2) = √40 = 6.32 m/s. Rate = A×v = 2×10⁻⁶ × 6.32 = 12.64×10⁻⁶ m³/s. (-87)
Statement c: Correct. Rate of heat = F_v × v_t = 6πηr v_t². Since v_t ∝ r², rate ∝ r × (r²)² = r⁵.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Consider the following statements about thermal properties of matter:
a. The quantities of heat required to raise the temperature of two solid copper spheres of radii r₁ and r₂ (r₁ = 1.5 r₂) through 1 K are in the ratio 27:8.
b. A piece of ice falls from a height h so that it melts completely. Only one-quarter of the heat produced is absorbed by the ice and all energy of ice gets converted into heat during its fall. The value of h is 136 km. (Latent heat of ice = 3.4 × 10⁵ J/kg, g = 10 N/kg)
c. Two identical bodies are made of a material for which the heat capacity increases with temperature. One is at 100°C while the other is at 0°C. If they are brought into contact, assuming no heat loss, the final common temperature is more than 50°C.
questions.explanation:
Statement a: Correct. Heat Q = m c ΔT = (4/3πr³ρ) c ΔT. So Q₁/Q₂ = (r₁/r₂)³ = (1.5)³ = 27/8 = 3.375.
Statement b: Correct. Energy = mgh. Heat absorbed = (1/4)mgh = mL ⇒ h = 4L/g = 4×3.4×10⁵/10 = 1.36×10⁵ m = 136 km.
Statement c: Correct. Body at higher temperature has greater heat capacity, so final temperature will be closer to 100°C, i.e., more than 50°C.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Consider the following statements about heat transfer:
a. Three stars A, B, C have surface temperatures T_A, T_B, T_C respectively. Star A appears bluish, star B appears reddish and star C yellowish. Hence T_A > T_C > T_B.
b. A deep rectangular pond of surface area A, containing water (density ρ, specific heat capacity s), is located in a region where the outside air temperature is a steady value at -26°C. The thickness of the frozen ice layer at a certain instant is x. Taking thermal conductivity of ice as K and specific latent heat of fusion as L, the rate of increase of thickness of ice layer is 26K/(ρxL).
c. The power radiated by a black body is P and it radiates maximum energy at wavelength λ₀. If the temperature of the black body is now changed so that it radiates maximum energy at wavelength 3λ₀/4, the power radiated becomes 256P/81.
questions.explanation:
Statement a: Correct. Wein's law: λ_max ∝ 1/T. Bluish means shorter wavelength, so higher temperature. Reddish means longer wavelength, so lower temperature. So T_A > T_C > T_B.
Statement b: Correct. Rate of heat flow through ice layer = KA(0 - (-26))/x = 26KA/x. This heat is used to freeze water: dQ/dt = ρA(dx/dt)L. Equating: 26KA/x = ρA(dx/dt)L ⇒ dx/dt = 26K/(ρxL).
Statement c: Correct. From Wein's law: λT = constant ⇒ T₂/T₁ = λ₁/λ₂ = λ₀/(3λ₀/4) = 4/3. From Stefan's law: P ∝ T⁴ ⇒ P₂/P₁ = (T₂/T₁)⁴ = (4/3)⁴ = 256/81.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Consider the following statements about kinetic theory:
a. The mean free path λ for a gas, with molecular diameter d and number density n can be expressed as 1/(√2 n π d²).
b. An ideal gas equation can be written as p = ρRT/M₀, where ρ and M₀ are mass density and mass of the gas respectively.
c. Increase in temperature of a gas filled in a container would lead to increase in its kinetic energy.
questions.explanation:
Statement a: Correct. Mean free path λ = 1/(√2 n π d²).
Statement b: Correct. Ideal gas equation: pV = nRT = (m/M₀)RT ⇒ p = (m/V)(RT/M₀) = ρRT/M₀.
Statement c: Correct. Average kinetic energy ∝ T, so increase in temperature increases kinetic energy.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Arrange the following units in increasing order of their magnitude:
(i) 1 minute of arc in radian
(ii) 1 degree in radian
(iii) 1 second of arc in radian
(iv) 1 radian
questions.explanation: 1 second of arc = (1/60) minute = (1/3600) degree = (1/3600)×(π/180) rad ≈ 4.85×10⁻⁶ rad. 1 minute of arc = (1/60) degree = (1/60)×(π/180) rad ≈ 2.91×10⁻⁴ rad. 1 degree = π/180 rad ≈ 1.745×10⁻² rad. 1 radian ≈ 57.3°. So order: second < minute < degree < radian.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Match the physical quantities in Column I with their units in Column II:
questions.explanation: Thermal conductivity unit = W m⁻¹ K⁻¹. Permittivity of free space unit = C²/(N·m²). Planck's constant has dimensions [ML²T⁻¹] so unit kg m² s⁻¹. Force in new system = 0.1 N. So A-2, B-1, C-3, D-4 is correct.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Arrange the following lengths in increasing order of their values:
(i) 1 fermi
(ii) 1 angstrom
(iii) 1 micron
(iv) 1 mm
questions.explanation: 1 fermi = 10⁻¹⁵ m, 1 angstrom = 10⁻¹⁰ m, 1 micron = 10⁻⁶ m, 1 mm = 10⁻³ m. So increasing order: fermi < angstrom < micron < mm. (Based on standard prefixes)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Match the error propagation formulas in Column I with the physical quantities in Column II:
questions.explanation: For P = a³b²/(cd), ΔP/P = 3Δa/a + 2Δb/b + Δc/c + Δd/d. For volume of sphere V = (4/3)πr³, ΔV/V = 3Δr/r. For density ρ = m/l³, Δρ/ρ = Δm/m + 3Δl/l. For KE = (1/2)mv², ΔK/K = Δm/m + 2Δv/v. So A-3, B-1, C-2, D-4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Arrange the following measurements in increasing order of their percentage errors:
(i) Mass: 22.42 g with error 0.01 g
(ii) Volume: 4.7 cc with error 0.1 cc
(iii) Length: 10.0 cm with error 0.1 cm
(iv) Time: 1.25 s with error 0.02 s

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Match the dimensional formulas in Column I with the physical quantities in Column II:
questions.explanation: Energy has dimensions [ML²T⁻²]. Pressure has dimensions [ML⁻¹T⁻²]. Resistance has dimensions [ML²T⁻³A⁻²]? Wait, source says resistance [ML²T⁻³A⁻²]. Electric field [MLT⁻³A⁻¹]. So A-2, B-1, C-4, D-3.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Arrange the following in increasing order of their values:
(i) 1 minute of arc in radian
(ii) Angle of 60° in radian
(iii) 1 radian in degrees
(iv) Angle of 30° in radian

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Match the screw gauge/vernier calliper terms in Column I with their values in Column II:
questions.explanation: LC of screw gauge = pitch/n = 1/100 mm = 0.01 mm. Vernier LC = 1/n MSD = (1/n)×(1/n) cm = 1/n² cm. Diameter = MSR + CSR×LC = 0 + 52×0.001 = 0.052 cm. Pitch = LC × divisions = 0.01×50 = 0.5 mm. So A-2, B-1, C-4, D-3.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Arrange the following percentage errors in increasing order:
(i) Error in P = a³b²/(cd) with a,b,c,d errors 1%,2%,3%,4%
(ii) Error in volume when radius error is 2%
(iii) Error in density when mass error 4%, length error 3%
(iv) Error in KE when mass error 2%, speed error 3%

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Match the dimensions in Column I with the quantities in Column II:
questions.explanation: Force × displacement has dimensions [MLT⁻²]×[L] = [ML²T⁻²] (A and C both [ML²T⁻²]). Pressure × volume = [ML⁻¹T⁻²]×[L³] = [ML²T⁻²]. Power = Energy/time = [ML²T⁻³]. Energy/time also [ML²T⁻³]. So A and C are both [ML²T⁻²] and B and D are both [ML²T⁻³]. The matching pairs are (A,1) or (A,2) and (C, the other). (-4)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Arrange the following in increasing order of their magnitude:
(i) 4.85 × 10⁻⁴ rad
(ii) 2.91 × 10⁻⁴ rad
(iii) 4.80 × 10⁻⁶ rad
(iv) 1.75 × 10⁻² rad

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Match the physical quantities in Column I with their dimensional formulas in Column II:
questions.explanation: Gravitational constant G = [M⁻¹L³T⁻²]. Planck's constant h = [ML²T⁻¹]. Angular momentum = [ML²T⁻¹]. Energy = [ML²T⁻²]. So A-2, B-1, C-4, D-3. Note B and C have same dimensions.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Arrange the following in decreasing order of their values:
(i) 1 second of arc in radian
(ii) 1 minute of arc in radian
(iii) 1 degree in radian
(iv) 1 radian in degree
questions.explanation:
(i) 1 second ≈ 4.85×10⁻⁶ rad
(ii) 1 minute ≈ 2.91×10⁻⁴ rad
(iii) 1 degree ≈ 1.745×10⁻² rad
(iv) 1 radian ≈ 57.3°
So decreasing order: (iv) 57.3° > (iii) 0.01745 rad > (ii) 0.000291 rad > (i) 0.00000485 rad.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Match the measurement terms in Column I with their definitions in Column II:
questions.explanation: Least count = 1 MSD - 1 VSD. Pitch = LC × number of divisions on circular scale. Zero error is reading when jaws are closed. Vernier constant is another name for least count. So A-2, B-1, C-3, D-4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Arrange the following in increasing order of their percentage errors:
(i) Measurement of a with 1% error
(ii) Measurement of b with 2% error
(iii) Measurement of c with 3% error
(iv) Measurement of d with 4% error
questions.explanation:
Contribution of a: 3×1% = 3%
Contribution of b: 2×2% = 4%
Contribution of c: 1×3% = 3%
Contribution of d: 1×4% = 4%
Wait, c and d both 1× their errors. So contributions: a=3%, b=4%, c=3%, d=4%. Increasing order: a(3%) = c(3%) < b(4%) = d(4%). But among a and c, both 3%. So (c) 3% = (a) 3% < (b) 4% = (d) 4%.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Match the forces in Column I with their correct expressions in Column II:
questions.explanation: Force on rocket = -v dm/dt. Viscous force on sphere = 6π η r v. Force in SHM = -mω²x. Spring force = -kx. So A-2, B-1, C-4, D-3.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Arrange the following in increasing order of their time periods for a simple pendulum:
(i) Length 0.5 m, g = 9.8 m/s²
(ii) Length 1.0 m, g = 9.8 m/s²
(iii) Length 2.0 m, g = 9.8 m/s²
(iv) Length 1.0 m, g = 4.9 m/s²

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Match the vectors in Column I with their properties in Column II:
questions.explanation: A·B = 9+16-25=0, so angle 90°. |A+B| = |A-B| ⇒ angle 90°. |A×B| = √3 A·B ⇒ tanθ = √3 ⇒ θ=60°. A·B=0 means perpendicular. So A-2, B-3, C-1, D-3 (B and D both perpendicular).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Arrange the following angles in increasing order of their values:
(i) Angle between A and B when |A+B| = |A-B|
(ii) Angle between A and B when |A×B| = √3 A·B
(iii) Angle between A and B when A·B = 0
(iv) Angle between A and B when A and B are anti-parallel

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Match the projectile motion quantities in Column I with their expressions in Column II:
questions.explanation: Range R = u² sin2θ/g. Maximum height H = u² sin²θ/(2g). Maximum range at θ=45° = u²/g. Time of flight T = 2u sinθ/g. So A-3, B-2, C-1, D-4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Arrange the following in increasing order of their ranges for same initial speed u:
(i) θ = 30°
(ii) θ = 45°
(iii) θ = 60°
(iv) θ = 90°

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Match the circular motion quantities in Column I with their expressions in Column II:
questions.explanation: Centripetal acceleration = v²/r = rω². Angular velocity ω = 2π/T. Time period T = 2πr/v. Frequency f = 1/T. So A-1, B-2, C-3, D-4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Arrange the following in increasing order of their angular speeds:
(i) 360 rpm
(ii) 1200 rpm
(iii) 10 rad/s
(iv) 2π rad/s
questions.explanation:
Convert to rad/s:
(i) 360 rpm = 360×2π/60 = 12π ≈ 37.7 rad/s
(ii) 1200 rpm = 1200×2π/60 = 40π ≈ 125.7 rad/s
(iii) 10 rad/s
(iv) 2π ≈ 6.28 rad/s
So order: (iv) 6.28 < (iii) 10 < (i) 37.7 < (ii) 125.7.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Match the laws of motion in Column I with their statements in Column II:
questions.explanation: First law deals with physical independence of force. Second law is F = dp/dt. Third law is action = -reaction. Conservation of momentum follows from absence of external force. So A-3, B-1, C-2, D-4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Arrange the following forces in increasing order of their magnitude for the same situation:
(i) Force on a rocket when dm/dt = 0.7 kg/s, v = 300 m/s
(ii) Viscous force on a sphere of radius r moving with v
(iii) Force on a bullet from gun F = 600 - 2×10⁵ t at t=0
(iv) Force on a 0.5 kg ball striking wall with 12 m/s at 30° for 0.25 s
questions.explanation:
(i) F = v dm/dt = 300×0.7 = 210 N
(ii) Need values, but viscous force is small
(iii) F at t=0 = 600 N
(iv) F = 2mv sinθ/t = 2×0.5×12×0.5/0.25 = 24 N
Order: (ii) small < (iv) 24 < (i) 210 < (iii) 600. But (ii) needs specific values. Based on context, (ii) is smallest.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Match the friction terms in Column I with their correct descriptions in Column II:
questions.explanation: Static friction is self-adjusting up to a limit. Kinetic friction = μkN. Limiting friction is maximum static friction. Rolling friction is smaller than sliding friction. So A-2, B-1, C-3, D-4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Arrange the following angles of inclination in increasing order for a block to just start sliding:
(i) μ = 0.2
(ii) μ = 0.4
(iii) μ = 0.6
(iv) μ = 0.8
questions.explanation:
Angle of repose θ = tan⁻¹μ. As μ increases, θ increases.
(i) θ = tan⁻¹0.2 ≈ 11.3°
(ii) θ = tan⁻¹0.4 ≈ 21.8°
(iii) θ = tan⁻¹0.6 ≈ 31.0°
(iv) θ = tan⁻¹0.8 ≈ 38.7°
So (i) < (ii) < (iii) < (iv).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Match the banking of roads terms in Column I with their expressions in Column II:
questions.explanation: Optimum speed on frictionless bank = √(Rg tanθ). Maximum safe speed = √[Rg((tanθ+μ)/(1-μ tanθ))]. Angle of banking = tan⁻¹(v²/Rg). Minimum safe speed = √[Rg((tanθ-μ)/(1+μ tanθ))]. So A-2, B-1, C-3, D-4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Arrange the following in increasing order of their kinetic energies for same momentum:
(i) Mass m
(ii) Mass 2m
(iii) Mass 4m
(iv) Mass 8m
questions.explanation:
KE = p²/(2m). For same p, KE ∝ 1/m. So larger mass means smaller KE.
(i) m: KE ∝ 1/m
(ii) 2m: KE ∝ 1/(2m) = 0.5/m
(iii) 4m: KE ∝ 0.25/m
(iv) 8m: KE ∝ 0.125/m
So increasing KE order: (iv) < (iii) < (ii) < (i).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Match the collision terms in Column I with their characteristics in Column II:
questions.explanation: Level: Medium

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Arrange the following in increasing order of their moment of inertia for same mass and radius:
(i) Solid sphere about diameter
(ii) Circular ring about axis
(iii) Circular disc about axis
(iv) Hollow sphere about diameter
questions.explanation:
(i) Solid sphere I = (2/5)MR² = 0.4MR²
(ii) Circular ring I = MR² = 1.0MR²
(iii) Circular disc I = (1/2)MR² = 0.5MR²
(iv) Hollow sphere I = (2/3)MR² = 0.667MR²
So order: (i) 0.4 < (iii) 0.5 < (iv) 0.667 < (ii) 1.0.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Match the moments of inertia in Column I with their expressions in Column II:
questions.explanation: Solid sphere about diameter = (2/5)MR². Circular ring about axis = MR². Circular disc about axis = (1/2)MR². Thin rod about centre perpendicular to length = (1/12)ML². So A-3, B-1, C-2, D-4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Arrange the following in increasing order of their radii of gyration for same mass and radius:
(i) Circular ring about axis
(ii) Circular disc about axis
(iii) Solid sphere about diameter
(iv) Hollow sphere about diameter
questions.explanation:
Radius of gyration k = √(I/M).
(i) Ring: k = √(MR²/M) = R
(ii) Disc: k = √((1/2)MR²/M) = R/√2 = 0.707R
(iii) Solid sphere: k = √((2/5)MR²/M) = R√(0.4) = 0.632R
(iv) Hollow sphere: k = √((2/3)MR²/M) = R√(0.667) = 0.816R
So order: (iii) 0.632R < (ii) 0.707R < (iv) 0.816R < (i) R.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Match the Kepler's laws in Column I with their statements in Column II:
questions.explanation: First law: elliptical orbits. Second law: areal velocity constant. Third law: T² ∝ r³. Law of periods is third law. So A-2, B-3, C-1, D-4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Arrange the following in increasing order of their orbital speeds for same orbital radius:
(i) Satellite around Earth
(ii) Satellite around Jupiter (M_J > M_E)
(iii) Satellite around Mars (M_M < M_E)
(iv) Satellite around Moon (M_Mo < M_E)
questions.explanation:
Orbital speed v = √(GM/r). For same r, v ∝ √M.
Mars mass < Moon mass? Actually Moon mass ≈ 0.012 M_E, Mars mass ≈ 0.107 M_E. So Mars > Moon. Earth mass = 1, Jupiter ≈ 318. So order: Moon (smallest) < Mars < Earth < Jupiter. So (iv) < (iii) < (i) < (ii).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Match the escape velocity expressions in Column I with their values in Column II:
questions.explanation: Escape velocity from Earth = 11.2 km/s. From Moon ≈ 2.5 km/s. For planet with R'=2R, ρ'=2ρ, v' = 2√2 v_e. For R'=4R, ρ'=ρ, v' = 4v_e. So A-2, B-1, C-4, D-3.

(ii) Satellite at height 2.5R_E
(iii) Satellite at height R_E
(iv) Satellite just above surface

questions.question:
Arrange the following in increasing order of their thermal conductivities:
(i) Copper
(ii) Wood
(iii) Air
(iv) Water
questions.explanation: Air is the poorest conductor, then wood, then water, then copper is the best conductor. So increasing order of thermal conductivity: Air < Wood < Water < Copper. (General knowledge from thermal properties)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Match the thermodynamic processes in Column I with their characteristics in Column II:
questions.explanation: Isothermal: constant temperature, ΔT=0. Adiabatic: no heat exchange, ΔQ=0. Isobaric: constant pressure, ΔP=0. Isochoric: constant volume, ΔV=0. So A-2, B-1, C-4, D-3.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Arrange the following in increasing order of their efficiency for same source and sink temperatures:
(i) Carnot engine
(ii) Real engine
(iii) Reversible engine
(iv) Irreversible engine
questions.explanation: Carnot engine is the most efficient reversible engine. All reversible engines have same efficiency. Irreversible engines have lower efficiency than reversible ones. Real engines are irreversible, so less efficient than Carnot. So order: Irreversible < Real < Reversible = Carnot. (-106)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Match the gas laws in Column I with their expressions in Column II:
questions.explanation: Boyle's law: PV = constant at constant T. Charles' law: V/T = constant at constant P. Gay-Lussac's law: P/T = constant at constant V. Ideal gas equation: PV = nRT. So A-2, B-1, C-3, D-4.

(i) O₂ (M = 32)
(ii) H₂ (M = 2)
(iii) N₂ (M = 28)
(iv) He (M = 4)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Match the degrees of freedom in Column I with their values for different gases in Column II:
questions.explanation: Monoatomic: f=3 (3 translational). Diatomic rigid: f=5 (3+2 rotational). Diatomic with vibration: f=7 (3+2+2 vibrational). Triatomic non-linear: f=6 (3+3 rotational). So A-3, B-1, C-4, D-2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Arrange the following in increasing order of their wavelengths in hydrogen spectrum:
(i) Lyman series first line
(ii) Balmer series first line
(iii) Paschen series first line
(iv) Brackett series first line
questions.explanation:
1/λ = R(1/n₁² - 1/n₂²). For first lines:
Lyman: n₁=1, n₂=2 ⇒ 1/λ = R(1 - 1/4) = 3R/4 ⇒ λ = 4/(3R)
Balmer: n₁=2, n₂=3 ⇒ 1/λ = R(1/4 - 1/9) = 5R/36 ⇒ λ = 36/(5R)
Paschen: n₁=3, n₂=4 ⇒ 1/λ = R(1/9 - 1/16) = 7R/144 ⇒ λ = 144/(7R)
Brackett: n₁=4, n₂=5 ⇒ 1/λ = R(1/16 - 1/25) = 9R/400 ⇒ λ = 400/(9R)
So λ increases: Lyman < Balmer < Paschen < Brackett.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Match the series in hydrogen spectrum in Column I with their regions in Column II:
questions.explanation: Lyman series in ultraviolet region, Balmer in visible, Paschen in infrared, Brackett in far infrared. So A-3, B-1, C-2, D-4. (-256)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Match the radioactive decays in Column I with their effects in Column II:
questions.explanation: α-decay: A decreases by 4, Z decreases by 2. β⁻-decay: A unchanged, Z increases by 1. β⁺-decay: A unchanged, Z decreases by 1. γ-decay: A and Z unchanged. So A-2, B-1, C-3, D-4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question:
Arrange the following in increasing order of their binding energy per nucleon:
(i) Hydrogen (¹H)
(ii) Iron (⁵⁶Fe)
(iii) Uranium (²³⁵U)
(iv) Helium (⁴He)
questions.explanation: Hydrogen has no binding energy (1 nucleon). Helium has high BE/nucleon (~7 MeV). Iron has maximum BE/nucleon (~8.8 MeV). Uranium has BE/nucleon (~7.6 MeV). So order: H < He < U < Fe. Actually He ~7, U ~7.6, Fe ~8.8. So (i) H ≈0 < (iv) He 7 < (iii) U 7.6 < (ii) Fe 8.8. (-271)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Match the semiconductor types in Column I with their characteristics in Column II:
questions.explanation: n-type: electrons majority, doped with pentavalent impurity. p-type: holes majority, doped with trivalent impurity. Intrinsic: pure semiconductor. Extrinsic: doped semiconductor (n or p type). So A-2 (electrons majority), B-1 (holes majority), C not matching with 3 or 4. Actually C intrinsic doesn't match 3 or 4. Let's reassign: A (n-type) = 2 (electrons majority) and also 3 (pentavalent). B (p-type) = 1 (holes majority) and also 4 (trivalent). So the correct matching pairs A-2,3 and B-1,4. But options have single matches. Based on typical matching: A-2, B-1, C-?, D-?. (-275)
